repo.one_liner: Distributed heterogeneous cache system with client/worker/master architecture, RDMA-optimized transport, and Python/C++ bindings for object and KV storage across heterogeneous devices.
repo.architecture: Clients (ObjectClientImpl, KvClient) communicate via RPC with worker nodes (WorkerOCServiceImpl) for object storage/retrieval and device transfers (H2D/D2H). The master node (OCMetadataManager) coordinates consistency across workers, tracking object versions, TTL expiration, and location metadata via RocksDB and optional etcd replication. Workers coordinate with each other (WorkerWorkerOCServiceImpl) for remote object retrieval and data migration during scale-down. Transport is optimized for URMA/RDMA ultra-bandwidth communication with fallback to UCP and TCP. Buffer management supports three modes: shared memory (inter-process), heap-allocated, and remote storage via RDMA.

Graceful scale-down: Migrating worker coordinates with master and peer workers to move objects before exit

Batch atomicity: Multi-object operations (publish, get) enforce ordered locking to prevent deadlock and ensure transactional semantics
Multi-device support: H2D/D2H transfers for NPU and GPU via hetero_client; framework-agnostic (PyTorch, MindSpore)

Transport abstraction: FastTransportManagerWrapper isolates URMA/UCP/TCP variants behind unified interface
Async lifecycle: Request manager tracks completion; task thread pools for parallel execution and graceful shutdown
Error chains: Timeouts propagate through batch operations; fallback transports kick in on failures

gotchas: URMA/RDMA memory management: Lifecycle of memory buffers, jetty endpoints, and completion channels must align with connection lifecycle; careless cleanup causes resource leaks or dangling pointers
Master metadata is source of truth: Workers cache metadata but must reconcile on startup; master-worker split-brain scenarios require etcd consensus if replicated
Client multi-SHM registration: Complex state synchronization across multiple shared memory regions; uncoordinated access during concurrent client registration causes corruption
Batch operation ordering: Ordered locking prevents deadlock but requires strict adherence to lock acquisition order across all batch operations
Transport timeout propagation: Fallback chains (URMA→UCP→TCP) must propagate per-hop timeouts correctly; incorrect timeout handling in wrapper causes hangs or premature failures
Eviction under memory pressure: Spill storage interaction with eviction list; evicting already-spilled objects requires careful sequencing to avoid data loss
Device transfer complexity: H2D/D2H with multiple device types and frameworks; buffer pinning and async operation coordination must account for device-specific constraints

src/datasystem/common/rdma/urma_manager..description: UrmaManager is a singleton managing URMA (User-Mode RDMA) transport infrastructure for the datasystem, including remote jetty connections, client/server lifecycle, memory buffers, and event polling threads. The file enforces critical invariants around bitfield flag initialization (zeroing the whole flag word before setting bitfields to prevent uninitialized heap garbage under -O2 optimization—see Issue #12 comment), memory mmap/munmap lifecycle, atomic access to ubTransportMemSize_, and thread-safe clientIdMapping_ protected by clientIdMutex_. The constructor pattern establishes registerSegmentFlag_ and importSegmentFlag_ with all-zero initialization before individual field assignments, a pattern that must be preserved in any flag additions.

data_constants: K_URMA_WARNING_LOG_EVERY_N=100: controls log frequency for URMA warnings
K_URMA_ERROR_LOG_EVERY_N=100: controls log frequency for URMA errors
DEFAULT_TRANSPORT_MEM_SIZE=256MB: default memory buffer size, must match SetClientUrmaConfig expectations
MAX_TRANSPORT_MEM_SIZE=2GB: hard upper bound for transport memory, validated in InitMemoryBufferPool
MAX_STUB_CACHE_NUM=2048: stub cache limit passed to RpcStubCacheMgr
RECV_JETTY_KEY_PREFIX="recv:": prefix for receive jetty keys, must match BuildLocalJettyKey logic
Bitfield initialization: registerSegmentFlag_.value and importSegmentFlag_.value must be zeroed BEFORE setting .bs fields
edit_checklist.warning: Bitfield flag initialization (registerSegmentFlag_, importSegmentFlag_) must zero .value first; setting only .bs members leaves uninitialized bits that cause driver crashes under -O2 optimization (Issue #12 pattern).

historical_insights.title: Bitfield flag corruption under -O2 optimization
historical_insights.problem: Uninitialized bits in flag word caused URMA driver crashes when only individual bitfields (.bs.xxx) were set without zeroing the entire flag word first
historical_insights.root_cause: Bazel -O2 optimization leaves padding/reserved bits as heap garbage; driver reads entire flag word including those bits
historical_insights.solution: Zero registerSegmentFlag_.value and importSegmentFlag_.value to 0 before setting individual bitfields (see constructor, references Issue #12)

historical_insights.title: Removed deprecated IB (InfiniBand) mode code
historical_insights.problem: Project fully transitioned to URMA/UB mode but dead IB code paths remained, creating maintenance burden and causing build failures
historical_insights.root_cause: CMake always sets URMA_OVER_UB=ON, making IB paths unreachable; UB-specific headers unconditionally included breaking IB builds
historical_insights.solution: Removed UrmaMode enum, GetUrmaMode(), EthToRdmaDevName(), ReadGids(), IB_PATH constant, and all URMA_OVER_UB conditionals (now always true)

historical_insights.title: URMA reconnection failure when client reconnected
historical_insights.problem: UB unable to reconnect when client reconnected; connection became stale without recovery
historical_insights.root_cause: Stale instance ID detection in CheckUrmaConnection() required proper tracking of remote instance IDs via uniqueInstanceId field
historical_insights.solution: Added instance ID validation in ExchangeJfr() and CheckUrmaConnection() to detect and report reconnection needs (K_URMA_NEED_CONNECT status)

historical_insights.title: munmap blocking get/set operations
historical_insights.problem: Unmapping old memory pointer caused subsequent get/set operations to block unexpectedly
historical_insights.root_cause: Memory buffer lifecycle not properly sequenced with active transport operations
historical_insights.solution: Careful ordering of munmap call relative to transport shutdown; destructor unmaps only after all queues cleared (d55be09a pattern)

historical_insights.title: Missing URMA connect observation point
historical_insights.problem: URMA connection setup latency not being measured for observability
historical_insights.root_cause: No metric point inserted in connection exchange path
historical_insights.solution: Added METRIC_TIMER(metrics::KvMetricId::URMA_CONNECTION_SETUP_LATENCY) in ExchangeJfr() around ImportRemoteJetty() call

pitfalls.description: Setting registerSegmentFlag_ or importSegmentFlag_ bitfields without first zeroing .value leaves uninitialized bits in the word
pitfalls.consequence: URMA driver crashes under -O2 optimization when it reads entire flag word including uninitialized padding
pitfalls.prevention: Always zero flag_.value = 0 before any .bs field assignments; follow the pattern in constructor: registerSegmentFlag_.value = 0; registerSegmentFlag_.bs.xxx = ...

pitfalls.description: Adding new flags without updating urma_manager.h and all call sites that check FLAGS_urma_connection_size or similar
pitfalls.consequence: Flag deprecation warnings appear (as seen in Init()—'urma_connection_size is deprecated and ignored'), or new flags don't propagate correctly
pitfalls.prevention: When adding gflags (declared with DS_DECLARE_*), verify all sites that read them, update documentation, and add deprecation warnings if replacing old flags

pitfalls.description: Calling SetClientUrmaConfig() after Init() has already been called, or with inconsistent parameters across different invocations
pitfalls.consequence: ubTransportMemSize_ atomic compare_exchange may fail silently, clientMode_ static flag may not reflect intended mode, causing clients to use wrong memory size or mode
pitfalls.prevention: SetClientUrmaConfig() must be called exactly once, before Init(), with consistent parameters throughout the process lifetime

pitfalls.description: Modifying clientIdMapping_ without holding clientIdMutex_ lock
pitfalls.consequence: Race condition in HasRemoteClient(), RemoveRemoteClient(), or ExchangeJfr() leading to lost or incorrect client mappings
pitfalls.prevention: All access to clientIdMapping_ must be protected by std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(clientIdMutex_)

pitfalls.description: Handling new URMA error codes without updating GetUrmaErrorHandlePolicy() table
pitfalls.consequence: New error codes default to DEFAULT policy (just report), missing specialized recovery (e.g., RECREATE_JETTY for code 9)
pitfalls.prevention: New error codes requiring special handling must be added to urmaErrorHandlePolicyTable static map with corresponding UrmaErrorHandlePolicy enum value

pitfalls.description: Not initializing or properly shutting down perfThread_ and serverEventThread_
pitfalls.consequence: Thread handles leak, stop flags not honored, deadlock if threads access destroyed objects in destructor
pitfalls.prevention: Follow the pattern in destructor: check joinable(), log, join(), then reset(); serverEventThread and perfThread must both be set in Init() and cleaned in Stop()/destructor

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/servi.description: Implements the Get operation for the worker object cache service, handling both local and remote object retrieval with coordination to master metadata service. Manages request lifecycle including authentication, batching remote gets into chunks of 32, timeout enforcement via reqTimeoutDuration.CalcRealRemainingTime() checks in retry loops, version consistency validation against master metadata, and rate limiting for data migration during worker rebalancing. Critical invariants: timeout must be checked before each retry (b18c649f); metadata addresses must be validated via SetObjectEntryAccordingToMeta (67212574); migratedBytes must be accumulated and passed to UpdateNotifyRemoteGetRateLimit to maintain backpressure.

data_constants: GET_LOCAL_PROCESSING_SLOW_US=1000, GET_QUERY_META_RPC_SLOW_US=1000, GET_REMOTE_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US=2000: slow-request thresholds that control VLOG dynamic logging levels
maxBatchSize=32 in NotifyRemoteGet: affects memory footprint and latency per remote-get batch; changing requires performance validation
EXIST_LOCAL_CHECK_TIMEOUT_US=50.0: timeout for local exist checks
US_PER_MS=1000.0: microseconds-to-milliseconds conversion for metrics
edit_checklist.warning: Removing reqTimeoutDuration.CalcRemainingTime() checks from retry loops will cause RPC calls after client deadline expires, creating pointless retries and potential hangs.

historical_insights.title: Pointless RPC retries after client deadline
historical_insights.problem: Retry loops in DeleteObjectsMetaUnacked and similar paths continued making RPC calls after remaining time was exhausted, wasting resources.
historical_insights.root_cause: No time-remaining check at loop iteration boundary.
historical_insights.solution: Added reqTimeoutDuration.CalcRealRemainingTime() > 0 checks in do-while loop conditions; break when time exhausted to avoid RPC timeouts (b18c649f shows this pattern in DeleteObjectsMetaUnacked which applies here too).

historical_insights.title: Stale metadata migration during worker rebalancing
historical_insights.problem: Objects migrated from leaving worker even when master had a newer version, moving stale data into the cache.
historical_insights.root_cause: No version consistency check between master and leaving worker before including in migration batch.
historical_insights.solution: NotifyRemoteGet now maps leaving-worker versions and compares against queryMeta version; if versions differ, skip object migration with info log (dc12dddd).

historical_insights.title: Undefined behavior with metadata missing valid locations
historical_insights.problem: Master metadata without a selected address could cause get failures when code tried to fetch from invalid location.
historical_insights.root_cause: No validation that queryMeta address was set before use in remote get.
historical_insights.solution: Use SetObjectEntryAccordingToMeta() to safely construct entries; treat metadata without selected address as absent unless only copy exists at local primary (67212574).

historical_insights.title: Excessive logging obscuring slow requests
historical_insights.problem: Unconditional LOG(INFO) on every Get request made slow requests hard to spot and created log volume issues under load.
historical_insights.root_cause: No threshold-based filtering; all requests logged equally.
historical_insights.solution: Convert to VLOG dynamic levels: slow requests (elapsed > threshold) log at INFO with metrics, normal requests at VLOG(1); use GET_LOCAL_PROCESSING_SLOW_US / GET_QUERY_META_RPC_SLOW_US / GET_REMOTE_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US thresholds (4fbd1b94, 9e5c9bed).

historical_insights.title: Missing network path visibility in batch remote gets
historical_insights.problem: BatchGetObjectRemote lacked source/destination address logging, making network latency issues invisible.
historical_insights.root_cause: Batch code paths did not record RPC endpoints.
historical_insights.solution: Enhanced logs with src/dst address and transfer path (UB/RDMA/TCP) to enable network issue diagnosis (a44464e0).

historical_insights.title: Unthrottled data migration during worker rebalancing
historical_insights.problem: Worker-to-worker data migration during rebalancing could saturate network without backpressure.
historical_insights.root_cause: No rate limiting on migratedBytes flowing from leaving worker.
historical_insights.solution: Added rateController_ member; UpdateNotifyRemoteGetRateLimit() calls SlidingWindowUpdateRate(migratedBytes) and returns new rate limit to client (0f36e6ac).

src/datasystem/client/object_cache/objec.description: ObjectClientImpl is the client-side implementation of a distributed object cache, managing RPC communication with cache workers, async operation orchestration across thread pools, and lifecycle management. It handles device transfers (H2D/D2H for GPU/NPU), metrics collection via PerfPoint, and worker failover. Critical non-obvious contracts: (1) async_release_buffer thread pool is a concurrency bottleneck—must remain ≥4 threads or high-throughput scenarios will block; (2) placeholder buffers from MCreate NX must have isSeal=false to avoid spurious 'already sealed' errors that fail subsequent MSet calls; (3) worker switching requires switchPost_.Set() notification and proper state transitions or requests hang/fail silently.

data_constants: MSET_MAX_KEY_COUNT = 8: batch size limit for multi-set operations
QUERY_SIZE_OBJECT_LIMIT = 10000: max keys per Exist/GetMetaInfo/Expire
OBJ_META_MAX_SIZE_LIMIT = 64: metadata size constraint
SHM_REF_RECONCILE_INTERVAL_MS = 5000: shared memory reference reconciliation interval
CLIENT_LOCAL_OR_RPC_SLOW_US = 1000: threshold for PLOG slow-request diagnostics
K_SEPARATOR = '$': object key component delimiter
Thread pool env vars (CLIENT_PARALLEL_THREAD_MIN_NUM, MAX_NUM, MEMORY_COPY_THREAD_NUM, MEMCOPY_PARALLEL_THRESHOLD): must be read by ReadOptFromEnv
edit_checklist.warning: Thread pool lifecycle during shutdown is fragile: asyncReleasePool_ must be nulled while holding shutdownMux_ lock, but lock must be released before joining/destroying to avoid deadlock and stalled requests.

historical_insights.title: Async release buffer thread pool became concurrency bottleneck
historical_insights.problem: asyncReleasePool_ sized at 1 thread serialized all buffer reclamation, blocking new requests behind pending releases under high concurrency
historical_insights.root_cause: Single-threaded design assumed low-frequency buffer release; not validated under production load
historical_insights.solution: Increased thread pool from 1 to 4 threads to parallelize buffer release operations

historical_insights.title: MCreate NX placeholder buffer isSeal incorrectly true
historical_insights.problem: Placeholders returned when key already exists had isSeal=true, causing downstream MSet to fail with K_OC_ALREADY_SEALED during MSet validation
historical_insights.root_cause: isSeal hardcoded to true for placeholders; MSet validation rejects sealed buffers; placeholder semantics not distinguished from real data
historical_insights.solution: Changed placeholder isSeal to false; added filtering for dataSize==0 placeholders in MSet; early return when all placeholders

historical_insights.title: UB/URMA transport unable to reconnect after client reconnection
historical_insights.problem: After client reconnected, UB (ultra-bandwidth) transport failed to reestablish connections, leaving stale connection state
historical_insights.root_cause: Reconnection logic did not properly invalidate/refresh UB transport state during client failover
historical_insights.solution: Added URMA diagnostics and RPC delay metrics; improved fallback to TCP; added worker availability checks before publishing to UB

historical_insights.title: Worker switch caused set request failures
historical_insights.problem: Multiple incidents where switching to new active worker left requests in failed state or hanging
historical_insights.root_cause: Insufficient state management during worker failover; switchPost_ notification alone insufficient for all callers; RAII cleanup incomplete
historical_insights.solution: Refined ProcessShutdown/ProcessDestruct state transitions; improved RAII guard usage; validated listenWorker_ updates trigger switchPost_.Set()

historical_insights.title: munmap operations blocking get/set request paths
historical_insights.problem: Synchronous unmapping of old memory pointers in logging code caused get/set operations to stall, reducing throughput
historical_insights.root_cause: Memory cleanup not deferred from critical paths; spdlog file rotation collisions forced rapid retries
historical_insights.solution: Deferred munmap away from hot paths; added millisecond precision to log rotation filenames to reduce collision-driven retries

historical_insights.title: Insufficient observability for slow and failed RPC requests
historical_insights.problem: RPC failures and slow operations lacked structured diagnostics, making production debugging difficult
historical_insights.root_cause: Ad-hoc logging without consistent thresholds; uncontrolled log volume growth; missing context (clientId, worker, path)
historical_insights.solution: Added PLOG_IF_OR_VLOG for operations >CLIENT_LOCAL_OR_RPC_SLOW_US; structured entrance/exit logs with clientId/worker/transfer-path; reduced noise via LOG_EVERY_N

src/datasystem/master/object_cache/oc_me.description:
OCMetadataManager manages distributed object cache metadata on the master node, coordinating consistency across workers through RocksDB persistence and optional etcd replication. It tracks object versions, TTL-based expiration, reference counts (clients and objects), and orchestrates recovery flows via event subscriptions (worker timeout, scale operations, restart). Core design: metaTable_ is a concurrent TBB hash map protected by metaTableMutex_; updatingObjsTable_ tracks in-flight updates with separate mutex; background monitor logs statistics every 60 seconds without holding locks.

Critical contracts to preserve: (1) TTL must be preserved during recovery (commit 904122c2); (2) async delete operations risk RPC timeouts—stay synchronous (revert 3697b5b0); (3) request-path logging must use VLOG dynamic levels, never unconditional LOG(INFO) (commit 4fbd1b94); (4) stale primary locations must be cleaned after slot recovery (commit 5afbefec); (5) metadata without valid locations requires validation before responding (commit 67212574).

data_constants: MSET_PENDING_TTL_US = 60'000'000: 60-second TTL for multi-set pending state (used in metadata operations)
ASYNC_MIN_THREAD_NUM = 2, ASYNC_MAX_THREAD_NUM = 5: bounds for asyncPool_ thread count
MIN_TTL_SECOND = 0: minimum TTL validation threshold
Monitor timeout = 60000ms, interval = 100ms: background statistics logging cadence (never increase interval below 60s or stats become useless)
OC_METADATA_MANAGER = 'OCMetadataManager-': event subscription name prefix
edit_checklist.warning: Async delete was reverted due to RPC timeout cascades (commit 3697b5b0)—keep deletion synchronous on critical paths; if adding async, measure end-to-end latency under load before committing.

historical_insights.title: Unconditional logging in request paths caused log volume explosion
historical_insights.problem: CreateMeta, CreateCopyMeta, QueryMeta, DeleteAllCopyMeta had unconditional LOG(INFO) on every request, overwhelming logs
historical_insights.root_cause: Debug logging left in hot paths without dynamic level gating or batching
historical_insights.solution: Converted to VLOG dynamic patterns (slow request > threshold), aggregated statistics outside loops (LOG_EVERY_N(INFO, 1000)), moved entry logs to end of functions

historical_insights.title: Async delete for eviction triggered RPC timeout cascades
historical_insights.problem: Eviction END_LIFE path using async delete caused RPC timeouts, blocking subsequent requests
historical_insights.root_cause: Async operations introduced contention and latency in critical metadata delete path, cascading to client timeouts
historical_insights.solution: Reverted async delete (commit 3697b5b0); trade synchronous latency for reliability

historical_insights.title: Object TTL lost during recovery from persistent storage
historical_insights.problem: Objects recovered from RocksDB after master restart had TTL reset to zero, causing premature expiration
historical_insights.root_cause: LoadMeta and slot recovery did not copy TTL field from persisted metadata
historical_insights.solution: Preserve TTL during all recovery code paths, verified in commit 904122c2

historical_insights.title: Stale primary location metadata blocked cleanup after recovery
historical_insights.problem: Slot recovery left stale primary location references, causing delete operations to fail on next recovery
historical_insights.root_cause: Location cleanup incomplete during slot recovery state transition
historical_insights.solution: Remove stale primary locations after slot recovery (commit 5afbefec)

historical_insights.title: Metadata without valid location selected treated ambiguously
historical_insights.problem: Master metadata lacking a selected address could not be reliably distinguished as absent vs. recovering
historical_insights.root_cause: No validation of location state validity before responding to queries
historical_insights.solution: Treat metadata without selected address as absent unless only copy is local primary (commit 67212574)

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description:
WorkerOCServiceImpl is the central service implementation for the distributed worker-side object cache. It manages object storage lifecycle, data migration during scale-down, metadata recovery after restarts, and coordinates with the master node via async managers (AsyncSendManager, AsyncRollbackManager). The file employs event-driven synchronization (subscribers to HashRingEvent, NodeRestartEvent, etc.) with careful shutdown in the destructor—every subscriber added must be removed to prevent crashes.

The service's design reflects scale-down resilience: it rate-limits data migration (FLAGS_data_migrate_rate_limit_mb), handles OOM gracefully in NotifyRemoteGet (setting remain_bytes even on partial failure), and recovers metadata after worker restarts (commit 66f4a028). Multiple thread pools (gcThreadPool_, decThreadPool_, oldVerDelAsyncPool_) are sized dynamically—recent changes (e1e5c524) replaced hardcoded min thread counts with FLAGS_oc_thread_num to enable configurable concurrency tuning.

data_constants: GET_MATCH_OBJECT_BATCH=500: object batch size for matching operations
URMA_WARMUP_OBJECT_SIZE=1, URMA_WARMUP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=5000: URMA connection warmup params
SHM_QUEUE_SLOT_NUM=32: shared memory queue slot count
FLAGS_oc_thread_num, FLAGS_data_migrate_rate_limit_mb: configurable thread pool and migration rate limits
OLD_VERSION_DEL_THREAD_{MIN,MAX}_NUM: thread pool bounds for old version deletion
edit_checklist.warning: Event subscriber lifecycle is critical—destructor removes 5+ event subscribers; adding subscribers without corresponding removal in destructor causes resource leaks and crashes on shutdown.

historical_insights.title: Thread pool concurrency bottleneck for Get operations
historical_insights.problem: Hardcoded min thread count (8) limiting concurrent Get operation throughput
historical_insights.root_cause: Thread pool sizing not tunable for varying workload patterns
historical_insights.solution: Replaced hardcoded 8 with FLAGS_oc_thread_num to enable configurable min pool sizing (e1e5c524)

historical_insights.title: RPC latency visibility gap for ZMQ communication
historical_insights.problem: No RPC latency metrics or tracing for client-server communication
historical_insights.root_cause: Metrics instrumentation missing for all RPC path phases
historical_insights.solution: Added end-to-end RPC latency metrics, per-phase latencies (queuing, send, exec, reply), and tracing ticks; refactored MetaPb from const reference to mutable to record timestamps on both sides (6873c11b)
constructs: PerfPoint usage in methods like Exist()

historical_insights.problem: Migrations during voluntary scale-down could hang or crash under memory pressure; no rate limiting
historical_insights.root_cause: No timeout control, OOM detection, or rate limiting in migration pipeline
historical_insights.solution: Added timeout control, OOM processing, and rate limiting flag (FLAGS_data_migrate_rate_limit_mb); changed migration strategy to use remote-get during scale-down (793db60c, bbedebae, 0f36e6ac)

historical_insights.title: Slot recovery assigned to unstable workers causing cascade failures
historical_insights.problem: Slot recovery tasks assigned to restarting/unhealthy workers, triggering repeated failures
historical_insights.root_cause: No stability checks before assigning recovery tasks to candidate workers
historical_insights.solution: Added validation to prevent assignment to unstable workers (d697f413)

historical_insights.title: Metadata lost after owner worker restart
historical_insights.problem: Metadata not recovered when a worker node restarts, causing object orphaning
historical_insights.root_cause: No metadata recovery trigger on node restart event
historical_insights.solution: Added metadata recovery after owner worker restart via NodeRestartEvent subscriber (66f4a028)

historical_insights.root_cause: Recon flag checks not updated when worker Ready transition occurs
historical_insights.solution: Added checks to bypass recon flag timeout after worker ready state (d6d47706, 2648b06d)

pitfalls.description: Adding an event subscriber (e.g., AddLocalFailedNodeEvent::GetInstance().Subscribe(...)) without corresponding removal in destructor
pitfalls.consequence: Event callbacks fire on destroyed object memory; crashes during shutdown or during event processing after service lifecycle ends
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain symmetric list: every Subscribe() in Init() must have exact RemoveSubscriber() call in destructor with same subscription name string

pitfalls.description: Modifying thread pool sizing (gcThreadPool_, decThreadPool_) without considering dynamic scaling or tuning FLAGS_oc_thread_num
pitfalls.consequence: Hardcoding thread counts defeats recent refactoring (e1e5c524); workload throughput becomes inflexible and performance regresses under variable load
pitfalls.prevention: Always use FLAGS_oc_thread_num or similar flags for min/max pool sizing; avoid constants in thread pool creation

pitfalls.description: Changing data migration logic without preserving rate limiting (FLAGS_data_migrate_urma_transport_mode, FLAGS_data_migrate_rate_limit_mb) or timeout handling
pitfalls.consequence: Scale-down migration floods network/memory, triggering OOM cascades or indefinite hangs; violates SLA during cluster rebalancing
pitfalls.prevention: Before modifying data_migrator_ integration, verify rate limits are applied and timeouts are set; test under memory pressure

pitfalls.description: Using NotifyRemoteGet() without checking the remain_bytes logic: ignoring the distinction between partial success (return OK) vs. full OOM failure (return K_OUT_OF_MEMORY)
pitfalls.consequence: Client misinterprets response; all-failed OOM is reported as OK, causing client to retry immediately instead of backoff, creating busy-loop
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the hasSuccess flag logic: only return OK if at least one object succeeded OR if rc is not K_OUT_OF_MEMORY; always set remain_bytes on partial success

pitfalls.description: Assuming ValidateWorkerState(ReadLock, timeout) can be called with arbitrary lock types or after exitFlag_ is set
pitfalls.consequence: Deadlock if lock acquisition order violated; hung requests if exitFlag_ check is skipped, causing graceful shutdown to stall indefinitely
pitfalls.prevention: Always pair ValidateWorkerState() with ReadLock or appropriate lock type; never skip exitFlag_ checks in request validation

src/datasystem/client/object_cache/objec.description: ObjectClientImpl is the core client-side implementation for distributed object caching in a data system, managing object creation, storage, retrieval, and lifecycle across local and remote worker nodes. It implements a sophisticated reference-counting protocol (both local via ClientMemoryRefTable and global via TbbGlobalRefTable) with periodic reconciliation to prevent leaks, coordinates failover between worker nodes via ServiceDiscovery and WorkerSwitchState, and dispatches async operations through dedicated thread pools—all under careful synchronization with multiple mutexes (switchNodeMutex_, globalRefMutex_, shutdownMux_) and background threads for perf monitoring and SHM reconciliation.

data_constants: currentNode_: atomic tracking which worker (LOCAL_WORKER or REMOTE_WORKER) is active
workerApi_: vector of IClientWorkerApi implementations—must always have at least LOCAL_WORKER index
globalRefMutex_: shared_timed_mutex protecting globalRefCount_ TBB map—use shared_lock for reads, unique_lock for writes
switchNodeMutex_: protects worker switching process—must be held when modifying currentNode_ and switchInProgress_
shmRefReconcileThread_: background thread that must be properly shut down before destruction
listenWorker_: vector at bottom of class ensures it destructs before other members
edit_checklist.warning: Worker switching and shutdown are historically fragile (6 recent commits fixed failover bugs); any edit touching switchNodeMutex_, currentNode_, WorkerSwitchState, or background thread lifecycle risks deadlocks or resource leaks.

historical_insights.title: Clients unable to reconnect after worker voluntary scale-down
historical_insights.problem: When a worker voluntarily shut down, clients either reported timeout errors or used stale worker connections; only one worker scenario was especially broken
historical_insights.root_cause: Incomplete coordination between ServiceDiscovery, worker switching logic, and client state manager during scale-down events; switchInProgress_ flag not reliably preventing stale operations
historical_insights.solution: Added ServiceDiscovery integration to detect scale-down, improved WorkerSwitchState transitions, added delayed recovery of preferred same-node worker after local startup

historical_insights.title: Shared memory reference count drift between client and worker
historical_insights.problem: Client-side reference counts (memoryRefCount_, globalRefCount_) could diverge from worker-side counts, causing resource leaks or premature deletion
historical_insights.root_cause: No periodic reconciliation; race conditions during crashes meant client thought objects were freed but worker still held them (or vice versa)
historical_insights.solution: Added periodic ShmRefReconcileThread that reconciles client state with worker via RPC; methods StartShmRefReconcileThread, ShmRefReconcileThreadFunc, DecreaseReferenceCntImpl

historical_insights.title: Rapid logging file rotation collision during worker failover
historical_insights.problem: After kill -9 on worker, all clients reported 'Could not create logging file: File exists' due to timestamp collisions in archive filenames
historical_insights.root_cause: spdlog rotating_file_sink used only second precision; rapid failover triggered multiple rotations per second with identical archive names
historical_insights.solution: Added millisecond precision to archive filenames (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_mmm); also fixed munmap of old log pointers blocking subsequent get/set operations

historical_insights.title: Pipeline H2D and large data transfer optimization
historical_insights.problem: Heterogeneous (GPU/NPU) H2D transfers and UB (ultra-transport) path were suboptimal for large payloads
historical_insights.root_cause: MSet operations didn't use fast UB transport path; H2D pipeline integration incomplete
historical_insights.solution: Integrated UB transport into MSet/MemoryCopy paths, added fastTransportMemSize_ tracking, extended ubTransportMemSize ConnectOption, supported pipeline H2D

pitfalls.description: Calling DecreaseReferenceCnt (void return) instead of DecreaseReferenceCntImpl (Status return) in new code paths
pitfalls.consequence: Errors silently ignored; reference count reconciliation skipped; leaks undetected until ShmRefReconcileThread runs
pitfalls.prevention: Check whether RPC failures during ref-count ops matter in context; prefer DecreaseReferenceCntImpl and handle Status, or document why void variant is safe

pitfalls.description: Reordering member variables or adding new background threads without adjusting shutdown order or initialization guards

pitfalls.prevention: Keep listenWorker_ at bottom; any new thread member should call its Stop/Shutdown in ~ObjectClientImpl before accessing other members

pitfalls.prevention: Use unique_lock for any modification to globalRefCount_; shared_lock only for reads; document lock type in function comments

pitfalls.description: Adding UB-transported buffers without updating fastTransportMemSize_ or ubTransportMemSize_ accounting
pitfalls.consequence: Memory exhaustion; client believes it has transport capacity but worker disagrees, causing buffer allocation failures
pitfalls.prevention: Grep fastTransportMemSize_, ubTransportMemSize_, and SendBufferViaUb whenever adding new Publish/MemoryCopy code paths; verify size tracking matches actual UB usage

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description: Implements worker-to-worker RPC communication for the object cache system, handling remote object retrieval over multiple transports (URMA/RDMA, UCP, and TCP fallback). Supports both single and batch requests with parallel processing via TBB, manages asynchronous gather writes for efficient RDMA transfer, and includes sophisticated fallback logic when primary transports fail. Key non-obvious contracts: (1) URMA warmup requests are detected by a specific pattern match in IsUrmaWarmupRequest() and must bypass normal processing; (2) GetObjectRemote exceptions only allow K_OC_REMOTE_GET_NOT_ENOUGH to escape (other errors are converted to response messages); (3) the FLAGS_enable_transport_fallback flag controls whether TCP fallback is available, and when disabled, transport errors immediately fail the request rather than attempting fallback.

data_constants: K_URMA_WARNING_LOG_EVERY_N = 100: controls URMA warning log frequency
URMA_WARMUP_KEY_PREFIX = '_urma_': prefix for warmup request detection
URMA_WARMUP_OBJECT_SIZE = 1: expected size for warmup objects
GET_REMOTE_WORKER_LOCAL_SLOW_US = 2000: threshold for slow request logging
US_PER_MS = 1000.0: time unit conversion constant for logging
edit_checklist.warning: Fallback logic between URMA/TCP/payload paths has intricate interdependencies on batchWaitFailed flag and FLAGS_enable_transport_fallback; changing one condition can silently break others.

historical_insights.title: URMA reconnection failures and transport fallback
historical_insights.problem: UB unable to reconnect when client reconnected; URMA wait operations failed without proper fallback to TCP
historical_insights.root_cause: Missing fallback logic in error paths and improper handling of transport protocol errors
historical_insights.solution: Added proper TCP fallback when URMA wait fails, preserved error details during fallback, added FLAGS_enable_transport_fallback flag for explicit control

historical_insights.title: Missing source/destination addresses in batch request logs
historical_insights.problem: Batch remote Get path (BatchGetObjectRemote) lacked critical debugging logs and source/destination IP information, hindering latency diagnosis
historical_insights.root_cause: Incomplete logging infrastructure for distributed tracing in batch code paths
historical_insights.solution: Enhanced GetRemoteAddressForLog() to extract source/destination from URMA and UCP info, added AppendSrcDstForLog() wrapper, upgraded log levels with elapsed time metrics

historical_insights.title: Excessive request logging and multi-get optimization
historical_insights.problem: Log volume too high in object cache request path; multi-get h2d path inefficient
historical_insights.root_cause: Verbose INFO-level logging on every request; suboptimal batch processing parallelization
historical_insights.solution: Reduced INFO logging to threshold-based PLOG_IF (only log requests exceeding GET_REMOTE_WORKER_LOCAL_SLOW_US), optimized ParallelBatchGetObject with TBB arena configuration

historical_insights.title: URMA fallback traffic control and resource tuning
historical_insights.problem: Uncontrolled TCP fallback from URMA consumed excessive bandwidth; default transport memory size insufficient
historical_insights.root_cause: No fallback gating mechanism; undersized buffer allocation (128 vs 512)
historical_insights.solution: Added selective fallback logic based on error type and FLAGS_enable_transport_fallback, increased default transport mem size from 128 to 512 in configuration

pitfalls.consequence: URMA warmup requests treated as normal requests, causing setup delays or protocol errors
pitfalls.prevention: Treat IsUrmaWarmupRequest conditions as immutable contract; add new parameters to separate function rather than modifying checks

pitfalls.description: Fallback logic chains three state variables: status.IsError(), subPayload.empty(), and FLAGS_enable_transport_fallback; conditions interact in non-obvious ways (e.g., error + empty payload + fallback disabled = immediate failure)
pitfalls.consequence: Changing one condition can silently alter fallback behavior for other error types (e.g., OOM vs transport error)

pitfalls.description: ParallelBatchGetObject uses shared mutable state (parallelRes array, batchPtr->fallbackPayloads) accessed by TBB parallel_for workers; synchronization relies on TBB's implicit barriers between phases
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions or silent data loss if workers modify each other's indices or payload vectors concurrently
pitfalls.prevention: Each worker accesses disjoint parallelRes[i] and owns its batchPtr; do not add cross-worker writes or remove implicit phase barriers

src/datasystem/worker/worker_oc_server.c.description:
WorkerOCServer implements the core worker node service for a distributed object/stream cache system. It manages client registration with multi-SHM support, async task lifecycle tracking for graceful shutdown, RPC communication with the master and peers, and cluster membership via etcd integration. This file is responsible for critical initialization (RPC warmup, hash ring setup, memory allocation) and clean shutdown (draining async queues, notifying etcd, persisting state).

Key design contracts: (1) CheckAsyncTasks must verify queues remain empty for the full FLAGS_check_async_queue_empty_time_s duration, not just briefly—this prevents data loss during shutdown. (2) NotifyShutdownToEtcd uses bounded retry logic with timeout arithmetic; failures are logged but non-fatal. (3) GFlags validate size/path constraints at definition time; changing them requires updating corresponding Validator:: calls. (4) Thread pool metrics changed from snapshot to interval cumulative (commit 35fe3cb8); new metrics must follow this pattern for observability to be meaningful.

data_constants: LIGHTWEIGHT_SERVICE_THREAD_NUM = 4: service thread count for cost-sensitive scenarios
DEFAULT_STREAM_SOCKET_NUM = 8: stream socket baseline, changed by FLAGS_sc_stream_socket_num
CHECK_ASYNC_SLEEP_TIME_S = 1: polling interval for async queue checks, must divide evenly into FLAGS_check_async_queue_empty_time_s
WARMUP_*_MS constants (1000/30000/110000): URMA connection warmup scan timings, must remain in valid range order
WARMUP_STABLE_ROUNDS = 3: convergence criterion for warmup, critical for RPC stub reliability
MASTER_RPC_WARMUP_THREAD_NUM = 4: parallelism for master RPC preemption, tuned for reliability
minRetryTimes = 3 in NotifyShutdownToEtcd: hardcoded retry count for etcd deletion, used in timeout calculation
THREAD_POOL_SIZE_LIMIT = 4096: maximum concurrent threads, used for validation
edit_checklist.warning: The CheckRule logic requires queues to remain empty for the full FLAGS_check_async_queue_empty_time_s window—if lastRequestArrivalTime changes, the counter resets; incorrect implementation risks data loss during shutdown.

historical_insights.title: Worker crashes during shutdown in error paths
historical_insights.problem: Early init failures caused crashes during shutdown sequence (commit 41d1784d)
historical_insights.root_cause: Shutdown code did not gracefully handle nullptr member variables or partially-initialized state
historical_insights.solution: Added null checks and sequenced shutdown to not access uninitialized services; added separate liveness check path

historical_insights.title: RPC stub warmup unreliable, causing latency spikes
historical_insights.problem: Master RPC connections were not pre-warmed, causing first requests to incur connection setup latency (commits 70f4bbca, cac77a51, 6bb32087)
historical_insights.root_cause: RPC stubs were lazily initialized on first call; no proactive connection establishment to master
historical_insights.solution: Added WARMUP_STABLE_ROUNDS-based convergence check and dedicated master RPC warmup thread (MASTER_RPC_WARMUP_THREAD_NUM) with URMA connection diagnostics

historical_insights.title: Thread pool metrics were meaningless (all zeros)
historical_insights.problem: Snapshot-based metrics (idle/total/waiting) taken every 5s showed idle≈total and waiting=0 even under load (commit 35fe3cb8)
historical_insights.root_cause: Metrics captured point-in-time state; high sampling interval relative to task duration made queuing invisible
historical_insights.solution: Switched to interval cumulative metrics: maxRunning, tasksDelta, maxWaiting, usage (computed as real utilization fraction). ThreadPool now tracks atomic counters with GetAndResetIntervalStats()

historical_insights.title: Client reconnection failures with UB fallback not preserving error context
historical_insights.problem: When URMA failed, fallback to UB (unbound memory transfer) lost diagnostic information (commit 945ee32f)
historical_insights.root_cause: Error details discarded during fallback, making root-cause analysis impossible
historical_insights.solution: Added 'preserve URMA fallback error details' and RPC delay metrics to surface actual failure modes

related_files.relation: 8 shared commits; implements OC service that WorkerOCServer delegates to. HaveAsyncTasksRunning() called by IsAsyncTasksRunning; InitShmRefForClient called by RegisterClient.
related_files.check: Verify HaveAsyncTasksRunning() behavior matches contract (returns true only if tasks are genuinely in-flight, not queued). Check InitShmRefForClient handles multi-SHM correctly.

related_files.relation: 7 shared commits; header defining WorkerOCServer class and member variables. Changes to shutdown logic often require header changes.
related_files.check: Member variables (etcdCM_, objCacheClientWorkerSvc_, etc.) initialization order; checkAsyncTasksDone_ mutex/CV declarations; gflag declarations.

related_files.relation: 6 shared commits; example configuration for gflags. Changes to gflag defaults or validators should update this config.
related_files.check: shared_memory_size_mb, sc_local_cache_memory_size_mb, master_address settings; ensure they pass validators defined in this file.

related_files.relation: 5 shared commits; Kubernetes deployment configuration. ready_check_path and liveness_check_path gflags map to k8s probes.
related_files.check: ready_check_path and liveness_check_path; startup/readiness/liveness probe configuration; environment variable mapping to gflags.

capabilities: registers clients with multi-SHM reference counting and liveness tracking
monitors and drains async task queues to ensure clean shutdown
warms up RPC stubs to master with convergence-based reliability verification
gracefully notifies etcd cluster of node departure with retry-and-timeout logic
exposes thread pool metrics as interval cumulative (maxRunning, tasksDelta, usage)

pitfalls.description: Assuming the async queue is empty once in CheckRule, without enforcing the full quiet duration
pitfalls.consequence: Worker exits while async tasks are still in flight, causing data loss or corruption
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the contract: lastRequestArrivalTime must remain unchanged AND checkNum must decrement to zero; reset checkNum if any change is detected

pitfalls.description: Modifying FLAGS_check_async_queue_empty_time_s or CHECK_ASYNC_SLEEP_TIME_S without ensuring they remain compatible (division/remainder issues)
pitfalls.consequence: Async queue verification hangs or exits prematurely; updateCheckNum calculation becomes nonsensical
pitfalls.prevention: Keep CHECK_ASYNC_SLEEP_TIME_S as a clean divisor of FLAGS_check_async_queue_empty_time_s; validate in tests

pitfalls.description: Changing shutdown timeout arithmetic without bounds checking, e.g., `FLAGS_node_timeout_s * 1000` when FLAGS_node_timeout_s is very large
pitfalls.consequence: Integer overflow in NotifyShutdownToEtcd timeout calculation; retry loop could timeout immediately or hang forever
pitfalls.prevention: Use cast-first pattern: `static_cast<int64_t>(FLAGS_node_timeout_s) * 1000` and cap with min/max like existing code

pitfalls.description: Adding new thread pool metrics or changing how they're collected without following the interval-cumulative pattern
pitfalls.consequence: Metrics become meaningless again (snapshot issues re-emerge); observability degrades
pitfalls.prevention: Use GetAndResetIntervalStats() pattern; accumulate atomically during DoThreadWork(); never snapshot idle/waiting

pitfalls.description: Reordering member variable initialization or adding dependencies on service pointers without null checks
pitfalls.consequence: Early init failures (e.g., during config validation) cause nullptr dereferences in IsAsyncTasksRunning or NotifyShutdownToEtcd
pitfalls.prevention: Verify all service pointers (etcdCM_, objCacheClientWorkerSvc_, etc.) are initialized before use; check for nullptr in shutdown path

reading_guide.start: CheckAsyncTasks and CheckRule: core shutdown gating logic that all other shutdown code depends on
key: IsAsyncTasksRunning: what constitutes 'still running' across all subsystems
NotifyShutdownToEtcd: etcd coordination during graceful shutdown
RegisterClient: client lifecycle tracking entry point
WARMUP_STABLE_ROUNDS and related constants: RPC reliability tuning
reading_guide.skip: Most gflag declarations and validators can be skipped on first pass; focus on the async checking and shutdown sequence

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/servi.description: This file implements batch GET operations for a distributed object cache, orchestrating retrieval of multiple objects from remote workers via grouped RPC requests. It handles memory allocation, shared memory copying, timeout propagation across parallel tasks, and error recovery including location removal from master and object invalidation. Critical design decision: requests are grouped by target address before batching to maximize RPC efficiency; timeouts are calculated per-task with elapsed time subtracted to enforce deadlines across the parallel execution pipeline.

data_constants: batch_get_threshold_mb: 100 MB payload threshold for triggering batch GET optimization
GET_REMOTE_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US: 2000 microseconds threshold for logging slow remote RPC calls
MAX_REMOTE_H2D_BATCH_GET_OBJECTS: 1024 maximum objects per remote batch chunk
US_PER_MS: 1000.0 constant for unit conversion in elapsed time calculations
logInterval: 10 second interval for inflight request logging in LogInflightRemoteGetRequestIfNeeded
logLimit: 16 inflight requests threshold before triggering INFO log
edit_checklist.warning: Timeout calculation and propagation across parallel thread pool tasks is fragile—timer drift or incorrect elapsed time arithmetic can cause cascading premature timeouts; also mergeLastRc overwrites earlier errors from concurrent threads, losing diagnostic context.

historical_insights.title: Timeout not propagated to batch thread pool tasks
historical_insights.problem: Parallel batch tasks launched to workerBatchRemoteGetThreadPool_ lost deadline context, risking unbounded execution
historical_insights.root_cause: Timeout duration was calculated for the batch operation but not forwarded to individual task closures
historical_insights.solution: Calculate remaining time with timer.ElapsedMilliSecond(), compute realTimeoutMs, and re-initialize reqTimeoutDuration in each task lambda before calling BatchGetObjectFromRemoteWorker

historical_insights.title: Remote RPC failures lack endpoint debugging information
historical_insights.problem: RPC failure logs missing source/destination addresses, hindering network troubleshooting
historical_insights.root_cause: Log messages included only operation name and status, not network endpoints
historical_insights.solution: Wrapped logs with AppendSrcDstForLog(message, localAddress_.ToString(), address); added PLOG_IF_OR_VLOG for slow RPC detection (>= 2000 µs)

historical_insights.title: URMA connectivity dropout on client reconnect
historical_insights.problem: Worker unable to recover connection via URMA (UB) when client reconnected, causing cascading RPC failures
historical_insights.root_cause: No reconnection logic after transport failures; fallback path not implemented
historical_insights.solution: Added TryReconnectRemoteWorker() after every clientApi read; wrapped RPC in RetryOnceWithErrorRepent() with specific retryable status codes and TCP fallback

historical_insights.title: Excessive unstructured logging obscures diagnostics
historical_insights.problem: High volume of logs with redundant information made it difficult to parse batch GET failures
historical_insights.root_cause: All operations logged at INFO level without rate limiting or structured fields
historical_insights.solution: Downgraded non-critical logs to VLOG(1); added LOG_EVERY_T/LOG_EVERY_N rate limiting; structured logs include elapsed time, request count, and transfer path (UB/RDMA/TCP)

historical_insights.title: Host-to-Device batch GET path stability under load
historical_insights.problem: H2D batch operations experienced performance degradation and instability with high concurrency
historical_insights.root_cause: Suboptimal chunk sizing and task distribution across parallel workers
historical_insights.solution: Hardened RemoteH2DBatchGetChunkSize() to use FLAGS_oc_worker_worker_parallel_min as lower bound, ensuring chunk size >= max(1024, parallelMin) for better load distribution

related_files.relation: Non-batch single-object GET path sharing error handling, memory management, and RPC timeout patterns; 25 shared commits indicates tight co-evolution
related_files.check: If modifying HandleGetFailureHelper, timeout calculation, or ShmUnit allocation logic, verify corresponding changes in non-batch path maintain consistency; ensure error codes and retry strategies align

related_files.relation: Service layer that invokes batch GET functions; 15 shared commits indicates caller dependency
related_files.check: If batch GET entry point signatures change, verify all callers pass correct parameter types and count; ensure timeout duration is initialized before batch call and lockedEntries map is properly populated

related_files.relation: Header file defining WorkerOcServiceGetImpl class and method signatures for this implementation
related_files.check: Any signature changes to BatchGetObjectFromRemoteWorker, BatchGetRetrieveRemotePayload, or GetObjectsFromAnywhereBatched must be reflected in header; verify workerBatchRemoteGetThreadPool_ and reqTimeoutDuration member declarations

related_files.relation: Defines PerfKey enums (WORKER_GET_BATCH_GROUPBY_DATA_NODE, WORKER_BATCH_REMOTE_GET_RPC, etc.) used in PerfPoint instrumentation throughout this file
related_files.check: If adding new PerfPoint measurements, ensure corresponding key is defined in .def file before use; verify perf key names match exactly

capabilities: Groups remote object GET requests by target address to maximize RPC batching efficiency and reduce network overhead
Allocates and aggregates shared memory (ShmUnit) for multiple objects before retrieval to amortize allocation cost
Orchestrates parallel batch RPC calls via thread pool with deadline enforcement and timeout subtraction per task
Handles remote payload reception with sequential copy to shared memory at calculated offsets
Implements cascading failure recovery: removes location from master, invalidates object state, and triggers cleanup on RPC failure

pitfalls.description: Timer drift in timeout propagation: elapsed time calculated via timer.ElapsedMilliSecond() and subtracted from realTimeoutMs; precision loss or unexpected scheduling delays could cause elapsed > deadline
pitfalls.consequence: Premature K_RPC_DEADLINE_EXCEEDED errors even though wall-clock deadline hasn't passed, triggering unnecessary retries and cascading failures
pitfalls.prevention: Use monotonic timer implementation, validate elapsed ≤ original deadline with assertions, add safety margin to timeout calculations, log actual elapsed vs. expected

pitfalls.description: Race condition in ShmUnit lifecycle: szChanged checks if (shmUnit == nullptr) without holding entry lock; another thread could deallocate between check and allocation
pitfalls.consequence: Double allocation, memory leaks, use-after-free when accessing newly allocated ShmUnit, or data corruption from overlapping writes
pitfalls.prevention: Verify entry lock is held during GetShmUnit() and SetShmUnit() calls; add lock annotations or assertions; avoid releasing locks between check and allocation

pitfalls.description: Silent error masking in parallel execution: mergeLastRc lambda only stores latest Status; if multiple threads error simultaneously, earlier thread's status is overwritten
pitfalls.consequence: Root cause error lost; subsequent exception handling operates on wrong failure reason, making debugging difficult
pitfalls.prevention: Collect all thread errors in a thread-safe vector; use structured error type that preserves all failures; log each thread's error immediately before merging

pitfalls.description: Payload indexing fragility: for loop consumes payloads sequentially with payloadIndex incremented in loop condition; if needReceiveSz doesn't match sum of payload sizes, loop exits with unconsumed payloads
pitfalls.consequence: Data misalignment if payloadLen < needReceiveSz (CHECK_FAIL_RETURN_STATUS catches this), or lost payloads if payloadLen > needReceiveSz (data corruption for next read)
pitfalls.prevention: Add explicit CHECK_FAIL for exact match; validate final payloadIndex position; add unit tests with mismatched payload sizes and boundary conditions

pitfalls.description: RPC retry handler hardcoded status codes: RetryOnceWithErrorRepent() only retries { K_TRY_AGAIN, K_RPC_CANCELLED, K_RPC_DEADLINE_EXCEEDED, K_RPC_UNAVAILABLE, K_URMA_CONNECT_FAILED, K_URMA_WAIT_TIMEOUT }; new transient errors outside this set won't retry
pitfalls.consequence: New transient failures fail immediately without retry, reducing fault tolerance; undocumented new permanent errors might be incorrectly retried
pitfalls.prevention: Document why each status code is retryable with comments; create helper function for retry classification; review when adding new error codes to ensure placement is correct

reading_guide.start: GetObjectsFromAnywhereBatched
key: GetObjectsFromAnywhereBatched: orchestrates full batch workflow—filtering, grouping by address via groupedQueryMetas, parallel task submission, result merging
BatchGetObjectFromRemoteWorker: implements RPC construction, retry logic with RetryOnceWithErrorRepent, payload reception pipeline
timeout propagation pattern: shows elapsed time subtraction (timer.ElapsedMilliSecond(), realTimeoutMs) and per-task timeout init via reqTimeoutDuration.Init()
groupedQueryMetas structure: demonstrates address-based request grouping strategy for batch efficiency
reading_guide.skip: AggregateAllocateHelper and traversalHelper lambda—memory allocation optimization details; defer to later pass when performance tuning is needed

src/datasystem/common/rdma/urma_manager..description:
UrmaManager is a singleton managing the URMA (remote memory access transport) subsystem for RDMA-based inter-process and inter-node communication. It orchestrates device initialization, memory buffer pooling (with ShmUnit backing), local and remote Jetty (communication endpoints) management, segment registration/import, and asynchronous RDMA operations (read/write) with event-based notification. The design uses concurrent_hash_map for thread-safe endpoint/event tracking and std::shared_timed_mutex for segment map access.

Key design contracts: (1) Member structs registerSegmentFlag_, importSegmentFlag_, urmaLogCallback_ must always use {} zero-initialization to prevent heap garbage corruption in the URMA driver (commit 74e80ee7); (2) SetClientUrmaConfig() mode and initState_ guard must be respected—the system supports both client (UB Put/Get) and server (RDMA responder) modes with different initialization paths; (3) Jetty and JFR/JFS management is now per-connection (not global) following commit 39a0f890, and RemoveRemoteResources must clean up both to avoid leaks; (4) Default buffer sizes (ubMaxGetDataSize_=32MB, ubMaxSetBufferSize_=8MB) cap RDMA payload—changes require coordination with all call sites.

data_constants: DEFAULT_TENANTID = "": tenant isolation default, used in client config
UB_MAX_GET_DATA_SIZE, UB_MAX_SET_BUFFER_SIZE: environment variable names for RDMA limits
MAX_POLL_JFC_TRY_CNT = 10: polling loop iteration limit
ubMaxGetDataSize_ = 32 * 1024 * 1024: RDMA GET payload cap, coordinated with UrmaRead callers
ubMaxSetBufferSize_ = 8 * 1024 * 1024: RDMA PUT payload cap per request, coordinated with UrmaWritePayload callers
registerSegmentFlag_{}, importSegmentFlag_{}, urmaLogCallback_{}: MUST use {} zero-init to prevent driver corruption
edit_checklist.warning: Member structs registerSegmentFlag_, importSegmentFlag_, urmaLogCallback_ MUST remain zero-initialized with {} or risk heap garbage corruption in URMA driver.

historical_insights.title: Uninitialized URMA struct members corrupted driver state
historical_insights.problem: Heap allocations contained garbage in registerSegmentFlag_, importSegmentFlag_, urmaLogCallback_ which corrupted URMA driver state under -O2 optimizations.
historical_insights.root_cause: Class member URMA structs were not zero-initialized; padding/reserved bits retained heap garbage.
historical_insights.solution: Added explicit zero-initialization using {} initializers for all URMA struct members.

historical_insights.title: Removed obsolete InfiniBand transport mode
historical_insights.problem: IB (InfiniBand) mode code was unreachable but still in codebase, creating maintenance burden and confusion.
historical_insights.root_cause: Project transitioned to URMA/UB mode exclusively (CMake URMA_OVER_UB=ON always), but legacy IB code and #ifdef guards remained.
historical_insights.solution: Deleted UrmaMode enum, GetUrmaMode(), EthToRdmaDevName(), ReadGids(), IB_PATH constant; removed all #ifdef URMA_OVER_UB conditionals.

historical_insights.title: Transitioned to per-connection Jetty/JFR/JFS management
historical_insights.problem: Global Jetty state could not support multiple independent connections to different targets.
historical_insights.root_cause: Original design used singleton Jetty/JFR/JFS resources, limiting scalability.
historical_insights.solution: Moved to per-connection JFS/JFR with localJettyMap_ keyed by connection ID, persistent across reconnects.

historical_insights.title: Added TCP fallback for worker-to-worker RDMA failures
historical_insights.problem: RDMA write failures in worker-to-worker communication caused hard failures.
historical_insights.root_cause: URMA/UB unavailable or degraded in some network conditions.
historical_insights.solution: Implemented TCP fallback mechanism when RDMA fails (coordinated with calling code).

key_constructs.name: UrmaManager
key_constructs.rationale: Singleton managing all URMA subsystem state; initialization guarded by initState_ and WaitPost; supports both client (UB) and server modes.

key_constructs.name: BufferHandle
key_constructs.rationale: RAII wrapper for ShmUnit-backed buffers; self-releasing in destructor via Release().

related_files.relation: Implementation; 33 shared commits indicate tight coupling.
related_files.check: Verify registerSegmentFlag_{}, importSegmentFlag_{}, urmaLogCallback_{} zero-initialized in constructor; verify RemoveRemoteResources() cleanup completeness; inspect concurrent map access patterns.

related_files.relation: Primary test suite; 10 shared commits; validates client-side buffer management and RDMA operations.
related_files.check: If changing buffer pool logic, segment registration, or RDMA semantics, run these tests.

related_files.relation: Major consumer for worker-to-worker communication; 9 shared commits.
related_files.check: If changing RDMA write/read APIs or Jetty management, verify worker reconnection and TCP fallback still work.

related_files.relation: Wrapper around UrmaManager for UB transport; 9 shared commits.
related_files.check: If changing SetClientUrmaConfig or buffer size limits, coordinate with wrapper initialization.

related_files.relation: Dynamic loading of URMA library; 6 shared commits.
related_files.check: If URMA API changes, verify dlopen bindings and flag structs remain zero-initialized.

related_files.relation: Provides underlying URMA resource (urmaResource_ member); 6 shared commits.
related_files.check: If changing UrmaResource lifecycle, verify UrmaManager::Stop() and destructor properly clean up urmaResource_.

capabilities: Initialize and configure URMA device for local/remote RDMA operations
Allocate self-releasing memory buffers backed by shared memory (ShmUnit) for client transport
Register local memory segments and import remote segments for RDMA access
Perform asynchronous RDMA write (UrmaWritePayload) and read (UrmaRead) with optional blocking
Manage local Jetty endpoints per connection and import remote Jetties for target communication
Track pending RDMA requests with events and notify completion via polling/async handlers

pitfalls.description: Removing {} zero-initializers from registerSegmentFlag_, importSegmentFlag_, urmaLogCallback_
pitfalls.consequence: Uninitialized heap garbage corrupts URMA driver state, causing silent data corruption or driver crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Always verify these three members retain {} initializers; add static_assert if possible to detect bare declarations.

pitfalls.description: Modifying concurrent_hash_map logic (localJettyMap_, urmaConnectionMap_, tbbEventMap_) without understanding accessor semantics
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions, iterator invalidation, or missed updates across threads.
pitfalls.prevention: Understand TBB const_accessor patterns; use consistent accessor semantics; test with thread sanitizer.

pitfalls.description: Changing UrmaConnection* to non-pointer type or not maintaining pointer validity in connectionKeys_
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or dangling pointers in the connectionKeyMutex_-protected map.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep UrmaConnection* as opaque pointers; ensure RemoveRemoteResources() is called before UrmaConnection deletion.

pitfalls.description: Altering ubMaxGetDataSize_ or ubMaxSetBufferSize_ defaults without updating all call sites
pitfalls.consequence: RDMA operations silently exceed limits or callers pass oversized payloads.
pitfalls.prevention: Grep all GetUBMaxGetDataSize() and GetUBMaxSetBufferSize() usages; update all enforce-limit sites; document in commit.

pitfalls.description: Incomplete cleanup in RemoveRemoteResources() or missing per-connection JFS/JFR cleanup
pitfalls.consequence: Resource leaks (file descriptors, memory) accumulate; connection reuse fails.
pitfalls.prevention: Review RemoveRemoteResources() to ensure both JFR and JFS are cleaned up per connection; test reconnection.

reading_guide.start: UrmaManager class declaration and singleton Instance() pattern
key: BufferHandle: RAII memory buffer wrapper — understand self-release in destructor before editing memory allocation
Init(HostPort): Device initialization path; guards with initState_ and WaitPost
UrmaWritePayload, UrmaRead: Core RDMA operations; understand blocking vs async modes and event_keys return
GetOrCreateLocalJetty, ImportTargetJetty: Per-connection Jetty lifecycle and reuse across reconnects
Member variables (registerSegmentFlag_{}, importSegmentFlag_{}, urmaLogCallback_{}, concurrent maps, mutexes): Critical for state management
reading_guide.skip: Detailed parameter docstrings (see .cpp for full semantics); URMA-specific driver constants from included headers

cli/benchmark/kv/command.py.description: KVCommand implements a CLI for distributed KV performance benchmarking by extending BenchmarkBaseCommand. It parses three argument categories (test config, run config, cluster config), validates them through a dedicated validator module, collects system information from local and remote workers before running tests, and builds benchmark suites via KVBenchSuiteBuilder. Non-obvious contracts: table formatting uses hardcoded col_widths dictionaries (in _print_node_info_table) and embedded width integers (in _print_worker_params_table) that must stay synchronized with f-string format specifications; remote node info unexpectedly reads from stderr rather than stdout; feature flags --concurrent and --skip_local are passed to the suite builder, which must coordinate its test case generation accordingly.

data_constants: col_widths dict in _print_node_info_table: 8 width values (ip, version, commit_id, total_mem, free_mem, thp, hugepages, cpu_mhz) must match the order and field count in header and row f-strings
required_fields list in _get_remote_node_info: 8 field names that dsbench show output must provide; defaults to empty string if missing
Hardcoded column widths in _print_worker_params_table header/row: 20, 15, 10, 10, 12, 15, 12 for 7 columns; no separate dict, embedded in format strings
Validator check sequence in validate(): must call all five (check_duplicate_args, validate_range_arguments, validate_format_arguments, validate_file_arguments, validate_mutex_arguments)
edit_checklist.warning: Table formatting is fragile — col_widths dict (_print_node_info_table) and hardcoded format widths (_print_worker_params_table) must stay synchronized with f-string layout or tables misalign; adding columns requires changes in three places.

historical_insights.title: Thread concurrency bounds not validated
historical_insights.problem: KV benchmark allowed client_num * thread_num outside the 1-128 range, accepting invalid parameter combinations
historical_insights.root_cause: validate() method lacked compound constraint checking for this product constraint
historical_insights.solution: Added validator.validate_range_arguments() call to enforce bounds

historical_insights.title: Mutual exclusivity of --all and --testcase_file not enforced
historical_insights.problem: Users could specify both test configuration flags simultaneously, causing conflicts in test case selection
historical_insights.root_cause: No mutex validation between these two test configuration options
historical_insights.solution: Added validator.check_mutex_arguments() to validate(), enforced in validator module

historical_insights.title: Concurrent set/get workload mode added
historical_insights.problem: Benchmark only supported sequential set→get→delete; couldn't stress-test concurrent read/write on same data
historical_insights.root_cause: Feature request for concurrent workload testing scenarios
historical_insights.solution: Added --concurrent flag to _add_run_config_arguments; passed to KVBenchSuiteBuilder via args

historical_insights.title: Skip local worker data in get operations
historical_insights.problem: Distributed tests couldn't exclude keys written by same-address worker during get phase
historical_insights.root_cause: Feature request for cross-worker benchmarking scenarios
historical_insights.solution: Added --skip_local flag to _add_run_config_arguments; passed to suite builder

historical_insights.title: Node and worker information display refactored
historical_insights.problem: System information display was fragmented or unstructured
historical_insights.root_cause: Code organization and clarity issues in pre_run()
historical_insights.solution: Extracted display logic into dedicated methods _print_node_info_table() and _print_worker_params_table()

historical_insights.title: Column width alignment in table output broken
historical_insights.problem: Table columns misaligned or overlapped in output
historical_insights.root_cause: Hardcoded column widths not matching f-string format specifiers
historical_insights.solution: Fixed col_widths dict values and updated format strings to match

python/yr/datasystem/kv_client.py.description: Python wrapper for the C++ datasystem key-value cache client, exposing libds_client_py through pybind11 for distributed state storage with TTL, transactional writes, and batch operations. Provides a typed interface with comprehensive parameter validation and C++ error-to-exception conversion. Critical contract: service_discovery (if provided) overrides host/port for worker location; parameter range checks (e.g., timeout_sec 1–INT32_MAX_SIZE) are defensive and must be preserved; ReadOnlyBuffer objects require rlatch/unrlatch pairing to protect buffers from concurrent write corruption.

data_constants: DEFAULT_FAST_TRANSPORT_MEM_SIZE: 256 MB default memory pool size passed to C++ client
MAX_FAST_TRANSPORT_MEM_SIZE: 2 GB upper bound enforced in fast_transport_mem_size validation
edit_checklist.warning: KVClient.__init__ has mutually exclusive logic for service_discovery vs host/port parameters; changes to this conditional or argument insertion order risk breaking both legacy and new initialization paths.

key_constructs.name: set
key_constructs.docstring:
Invoke worker client to set the value of a key.

Args:
    key(str): The key of string data.
    val(memoryview, bytes, bytearray, str): The data to be set.
    write_mode(WriteMode): controls whether data is written to the L2 cache to enhance data reliability.
        The options are as follows:
            WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE:
                Indicates that objects are only written to the cache.
            WriteMode.WRITE_THROUGH_L2_CACHE:
                Indicates that objects are synchronously written to both the cache and the L2 storage.
                This mode ensures high data reliability but may affect performance due to 
                the synchronous write operation.
            WriteMode.WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE:
                Indicates that objects are initially written to the cache, and an asynchronous task is 
                triggered to write the data to the L2 storage.
                This improves performance but may lead to data loss if the worker fails before the data is 
                persisted to the L2 storage.
            WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE_EVICT:
                Indicates that objects are only cached and are evictable when the cache capacity is 
                insufficient. Data reliability is not required, and the data will not be written to 
                the L2 storage upon eviction.
            WriteMode.WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE_EVICT:
                Indicates that objects are cached and are evictable when the cache capacity is insufficient.
                The data is asynchronously written to the L2 storage to improve reliability, 
                but the object may be evicted from the cache before the write completes if the cache is full.
    ttl_second(uint32): controls the expire time of the data:
        If the value is greater than 0, the data will be deleted automatically after expired.
        If set to 0, the data need to be manually deleted.
Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if fails to set the value of the key.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

Check the existence of the given keys in the data system.

Args:

Raises:

src/datasystem/client/client_worker_comm.description: This file implements two client classes (LocalCommonApi for embedded scenarios, RemoteCommonApi for remote RPC) that manage client-worker communication, including registration, heartbeat, URMA (RDMA) handshakes, and transport setup. The core design separates connection-phase timeouts (connectTimeoutMs, used for handshakes) from business-operation timeouts (requestTimeoutMs) via CalculateSingleRpcTimeout() — a critical contract established in commit 6e9ab1d6 after cross-node worker switching failed when requestTimeoutMs was too small (20ms). Heartbeat timing is calculated by SetHeartbeatProperties() using worker-supplied deadlines and safety ratios (retryNum=3, reduceRatio=5), ensuring the client heartbeats complete before the worker declares it dead. The RemoteCommonApi uses async URMA handshake with exponential-backoff retry (capped at 8s) and worker-version tracking to ignore stale results after reconnection.

data_constants: CalculateSingleRpcTimeout thresholds (shorterSplitTime=30s, longerSplitTime=90s, rpcMaxTimeout=10min, retryTimes=3): controls RPC timeout splitting for cross-node operations
SetHeartbeatProperties ratios (retryNum=3, reduceRatio=5): control heartbeat interval to complete before worker timeout
Heartbeat timeout/interval mins/maxes (MIN/MAX_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS, MIN/MAX_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS): contracts with worker service
ScheduleUrmaHandshakeRetry constants (maxRetryIntervalMs=8s, retryCheckIntervalMs=100ms, rpcTimeout=15s): exponential backoff caps and polling intervals
edit_checklist.warning: Modifying CalculateSingleRpcTimeout thresholds or heartbeat ratio calculations can silently break cross-node worker switching or cause heartbeat timeouts; test with realistic network latencies.

historical_insights.title: Connection-phase RPC timeout decoupling
historical_insights.problem: Connection-phase RPCs (GetSocketPath, RegisterClient) incorrectly used rpcTimeoutMs_ derived from small requestTimeoutMs (e.g. 20ms for business ops). TCP connection establishment exceeds 20ms, causing cross-node worker switching failures.
historical_insights.root_cause: Business operation timeout was conflated with connection establishment timeout; no separate timeout track for connection-phase.
historical_insights.solution: Extracted CalculateSingleRpcTimeout() static function. Connection-phase RPCs now use connectTimeoutMs to derive per-RPC timeout, decoupling from business requestTimeoutMs.

historical_insights.title: JFR import blocking heartbeat
historical_insights.problem: Importing JFR (JVM Flight Recorder?) fails and takes a long time, causing heartbeat to be lost and worker to mark client as dead.
historical_insights.root_cause: JFR import on critical path blocking heartbeat dispatch.
historical_insights.solution: Delay fast transport (JFR) initialization until heartbeat is active (commit 27dcbcf2). Implies heartbeat must be established and healthy before transport setup completes.

historical_insights.title: URMA reconnection after client reconnect
historical_insights.problem: UB (User Buffer over RDMA) unable to reconnect when client reconnected to worker.
historical_insights.root_cause: URMA handshake not properly handling client reconnection state; stale handshake attempts not being discarded.
historical_insights.solution: Added URMA diagnostics, RPC delay metrics, and preserved URMA fallback error details. Implemented version-tracking in ScheduleUrmaHandshakeRetry() to ignore stale attempts after workerVersion increments.

historical_insights.title: Excessive logging reducing heartbeat reliability
historical_insights.problem: High log volume from client/worker layers could delay heartbeat dispatch and cause timeouts.
historical_insights.root_cause: Excessive Get/Set operation logging without rate limiting.
historical_insights.solution: Structured logging with clientId/worker/path info at entry/exit only. Intermediate logs moved to VLOG(1) or removed. Log frequency reduced with LOG_EVERY_N(INFO, 1000). Log rate limit synced from worker via SetHeartbeatProperties().
