pitfalls.description: Modifying CalculateSingleRpcTimeout thresholds (shorterSplitTime=30s, longerSplitTime=90s) without testing cross-node latencies
pitfalls.consequence: Worker switching fails when per-RPC timeout is too small relative to actual network latency; client registration hangs.
pitfalls.prevention: Test with realistic cross-node network conditions; log actual vs. derived timeouts; verify with commit 6e9ab1d6 context (cross-node TCP takes >20ms).

pitfalls.description: Adding logging (especially in hot paths like heartbeat or RPC) without respecting LogRateLimiter
pitfalls.consequence: Heartbeat dispatch delayed, worker marks client dead, connection lost.
pitfalls.prevention: Use LOG_EVERY_N, VLOG(1), or LogRateLimiter; check commit 945ee32f for observability refactoring pattern.

pitfalls.description: Calling TryUrmaHandshake or acting on its result without checking workerVersion_
pitfalls.consequence: Stale handshake attempts overwrite newer ones after reconnection; URMA fails intermittently.
pitfalls.prevention: In ScheduleUrmaHandshakeRetry and UrmaHandshakeOnFirstHeartbeat, always load workerVersion before and after RPC; ignore if version changed.

pitfalls.description: Changing heartbeat interval/timeout calculation (retryNum=3, reduceRatio=5) without understanding worker-side timeout expectations
pitfalls.consequence: Heartbeat completes after worker timeout; client marked dead before it can recover.
pitfalls.prevention: Heartbeat must complete with (retryNum-1) retries before clientDeadTimeoutMs; interval must leave margin; verify ratio constants match worker assumptions.

pitfalls.description: Not signaling recvPageWaitPost/recvPageNotify or not joining recvPageThread in destructor
pitfalls.consequence: RecvPageFd thread hangs or leaks; destructor deadlocks.
pitfalls.prevention: Always set stopRecvPageFd=true and call recvPageWaitPost->Set() + recvPageNotify->Set() before join(); verify thread cleanup order in ~ClientWorkerRemoteCommonApi.

setup.py.description: setup.py configures the openyuanrong-datasystem Python package, a distributed heterogeneous cache system. Beyond standard setuptools configuration, it implements a sophisticated build-time dependency extraction pipeline using ldd to identify required .so (shared object) files, automatically discovers package data from multiple source directories via recursive scanning, and performs post-build cleanup to remove unused libraries and reorganize UCX-specific libraries. A critical non-obvious contract: all hardcoded dependency set members (libdatasystem.so, libds_client_py.so, libacl_plugin.so, libcuda_plugin.so, libp2p_transfer.so) must remain in sync with the actual library build outputs, and any change to directory structure (yr/datasystem/lib, datasystem_worker location) will silently break the build—these are not validated at runtime.

data_constants: all_dependencies_for_datasystem set: must remain synchronized with actual compiled .so files that get included in the wheel
package_datas directory list: {sdk_lib_list, datasystem_worker, dsbench_cpp, lib, include, helm_chart, cpp_template, docker_entryfile} must match actual source layout
libuc*.so.0 fnmatch pattern: UCX versioning strategy; changing breaks UCX reorganization
directory structure: yr/datasystem/lib, yr/datasystem/datasystem_worker—paths are not validated, just assumed
edit_checklist.warning: System dependency on 'ldd' tool is not declared in install_requires and will fail on non-Linux systems without clear error messaging.

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description: Manages GetRequest lifecycle for remote object cache queries from clients. Tracks completion status of batch object retrievals, delegates responses to either shared memory or no-SHM paths, and handles Remote H2D (host-to-device) data transfer with comm ID validation. Must respect: (1) atomic `readyCount_` for lockless progress tracking while `lastRc_` and `params` require `mutex_` protection, (2) dual response modes (enableReturnObjectIndex_ vs response object keys) that propagate through response construction, (3) URMA conditional logic (hasUbGetInfo_, ubBufferSize_ gates) that fallback to TCP, (4) Remote H2D comm ID mapping validation in MarkSuccessImpl to ensure client can receive responses.

data_constants: readyCount_: atomic counter with memory_order_relaxed; incremented in MarkFailed, MarkSuccessImpl, UpdateAfterLocalGet
objects_: unordered_map<ObjectKey, GetObjInfo> keyed by namespace-uri; protected by mutex_ when updating params field
enableReturnObjectIndex_: bool flag controlling dual response mode (indices vs keys); must be consistent in SetShmObjectInfoPb/SetNoShmObjectInfoPb/SetDefaultObjectInfoPb
hasUbGetInfo_: controls URMA/UB path; disabled if ubBufferSize_ == 0; validated in Init()
clientCommId_: used for Remote H2D comm ID mapping validation in MarkSuccessImpl; empty string disables validation
edit_checklist.warning: URMA/UB connection fallback and Remote H2D comm ID validation are heavily intertwined across multiple commits (945ee32f, feddf7de, 0d3e4ea7); changes to MarkSuccessImpl or RemoteH2DHostInfoMap lookup must preserve K_TRY_AGAIN error semantics and validate against co-change files (worker_oc_service_get_impl.cpp, worker_oc_service_batch_get_impl.cpp).

historical_insights.title: URMA/UB connection stability and TCP fallback traffic explosion
historical_insights.problem: URMA fallback to TCP lacked error detail propagation; connections could not recover after client reconnection; TCP rate limiting did not apply when UB unavailable; URMA fallback generated excessive TCP traffic
historical_insights.root_cause: No mechanism to preserve URMA failure context; connection recovery not implemented; TCP rate limits were UB-specific; missing coordination of URMA availability state
historical_insights.solution: Added URMA fallback error detail preservation (945ee32f); implemented UB connection recovery between client/worker (e191e90b); bound meta-moving redirect retry sleep; added TCP rate limiting applicability when UB unavailable (783db47e); controlled fallback behavior with availability checks (feddf7de); limited URMA fallback TCP traffic with explicit checks (0d3e4ea7)

historical_insights.solution: Added device ID empty-check before ParsePiplnH2DRequest (e4420986); fixed request parsing in pipeline scenario (e4420986); enhanced get remote logs with elapsed time and src/dst address information (a44464e0); optimized mget H2D path (85858713)

Tracks request completion with atomic counters and thread-safe status aggregation
Supports URMA ultradata access with TCP fallback and connection recovery
Validates Remote H2D host-to-device comm ID mapping before returning responses

pitfalls.description: Mixing atomic and lock-based synchronization: readyCount_ is atomic (memory_order_relaxed) but lastRc_ and params are mutex-protected. An editor could add a new field and protect it inconsistently.
pitfalls.consequence: Race condition where completion count increments but error status is not yet visible, or vice versa; callers see inconsistent state.
pitfalls.prevention: For per-object metadata, use mutex (consistent with params field); only use atomics for counters visible to MarkSuccessImpl/MarkFailed/ReturnToClient path.

pitfalls.description: URMA conditional gates (hasUbGetInfo_, ubBufferSize_, GetClientCommUuid()) are checked independently across multiple methods. Disabling one without understanding all can silently break Remote H2D validation.
pitfalls.consequence: Remote H2D responses returned without comm ID validation, causing client rejection or data mismatch; falls back to TCP when it should use H2D.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying URMA checks in MarkSuccessImpl or SetShmObjectInfoPb, verify all three gates are consistent; check co-change files (worker_oc_service_get_impl.cpp, worker_oc_service_batch_get_impl.cpp) for corresponding logic.

pitfalls.description: Duplicate object key handling allows same key multiple times only if offsetInfo matches. Validator checks equality but an editor could relax or remove the validator without understanding batch deduplication semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Duplicate keys with different offsets silently ignored or processed twice, causing inconsistent result counts returned to client.
pitfalls.prevention: Never remove or bypass the offsetInfo equality check in Init(); if adding support for multiple reads of same key with different offsets, redesign the data structure.

pitfalls.description: Response mode (enableReturnObjectIndex_ vs responseObjectKeys_) is set once in Init() but used in three separate info-setting methods (SetShmObjectInfoPb, SetNoShmObjectInfoPb, SetDefaultObjectInfoPb). An editor could update one method without the others.
pitfalls.consequence: Some responses include object keys while others include indices, causing client parsing failures or index-out-of-bounds errors.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing response construction, search all three methods and verify enableReturnObjectIndex_ logic is identical; test both modes (with and without index mode).

pitfalls.description: Remote H2D comm ID validation in MarkSuccessImpl returns K_TRY_AGAIN (not K_INVALID) to signal 'not ready yet'. An editor might change this to K_INVALID thinking all lookup failures are bad.
pitfalls.consequence: Client retries are aborted instead of delayed, causing data loss or timeout; Remote H2D responses blocked indefinitely.
pitfalls.prevention: K_TRY_AGAIN is intentional and part of the completion-wait protocol; preserve it; see commits 85858713 and feddf7de for why this matters.

pitfalls.description: DeleteObjects uses a function-pointer callback (deleteFunc_) that silently fails if nullptr. An editor could assume failure always logs and not check initialization.
pitfalls.consequence: Objects never deleted, leaking memory/resources; no error signal to caller.
pitfalls.prevention: Verify SetDeleteObjectsFunc is called before DeleteObjects; consider adding CHECK or warning log if deleteFunc_ is nullptr.

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description: WorkerOCServiceImpl is the main RPC service handler for distributed object cache operations on worker nodes, managing lifecycle of cached objects (create, publish, delete, expire), data migration during scale-down, and L2 cache persistence. It coordinates multiple subsystems: object storage via thread pools (memCpyThreadPool, gcThreadPool, decThreadPool), metadata recovery managers, async operation managers (AsyncRpcRequestManager, AsyncSendManager, AsyncRollbackManager), and delegated operation handlers (createProc_, publishProc_, deleteProc_, etc.). Critical design: uses WriterPrefRWLock (reconFlag_) to serialize reconciliation with common RPCs, maintains strict thread pool destruction order (documented in comments), and ensures async tasks complete before shutdown via AsyncTasksDoneChecker callback pattern. The class is not a monolith—it delegates actual work to separate service-impl classes while acting as the coordination and RPC entry point.

data_constants: MEMCOPY_THREAD_NUM = 16: controls memory copy operation parallelism
PARALLEL_THREAD_NUM = 8: controls general parallel operation count
WriterPrefRWLock reconFlag_: protects reconciliation state and blocks common RPCs; must be acquired via ValidateWorkerState before RPC processing
voluntaryScaleDownClearIds_ vector: must stay in sync with actual migration state; guarded by clearIdsMutex_
Thread pool destruction order: gcThreadPool_ before evictionManager_, threadPool_ before clientReconnectPost_, decThreadPool_ before decreaseRPCQ_
edit_checklist.warning: Thread pool destruction order is fragile—adding new pools or changing member order without updating documented dependencies can cause use-after-free; reconciliation lock (reconFlag_) can deadlock RPC processing if held during long operations.

historical_insights.solution: Added MigrateDataRateController (0f36e6ac: 2026-06-06)

pitfalls.description: Modifying thread pool declaration order without updating documented destruction dependencies
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or double-free if a thread pool is destroyed before one it depends on; e.g., if devThreadPool_ moves before gcThreadPool_ without updating the 'must destruct' comment
pitfalls.prevention: Always preserve the relative declaration order of pools with documented destruction constraints; add a comment explaining any reordering and verify no pool accesses another after its own shutdown

pitfalls.description: Holding reconFlag_ write lock across a long-running operation or I/O
pitfalls.consequence: All common RPC processing (Put, Get, Delete) blocks waiting for read lock, starving the service while reconciliation completes
pitfalls.prevention: Keep write lock scope minimal; prefer separate atomics or conditions for operation-specific state; see ValidateWorkerState for the pattern

pitfalls.description: Creating new async operation manager without registering AsyncTasksDoneChecker
pitfalls.consequence: Async tasks may still be in flight when service shuts down, causing crashes or data loss; graceful shutdown depends on checker completeness
pitfalls.prevention: If adding a new async operation type (e.g., AsyncFooManager), ensure it either participates in the existing checker or register a new checker via RegisterAsyncTasksDoneChecker before starting operations

pitfalls.description: Modifying voluntaryScaleDownClearIds_ without also updating the migration completion logic
pitfalls.consequence: Worker may exit before all data is migrated; or objects may be deleted before migration completes, leaving orphaned data on destination nodes
pitfalls.prevention: Always update both GroupAndRemoveMeta (which populates needMigrateIds), BeforeMigrateData, and the completion handler in tandem; test with --gtest_repeat to catch races

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to call oldVerDelAsyncPool_->Submit() after adding a new L2 cache version
pitfalls.consequence: Old L2 cache files accumulate on disk, causing storage leak and eventual out-of-disk condition
pitfalls.prevention: When adding L2 cache operations, immediately submit old-version deletion to oldVerDelAsyncPool_ as part of the same transaction; code review should flag any L2 write without a corresponding async delete

.codex/hooks/context_survival.py.description:
This file implements a Codex hook system for context survival across sessions. It handles seven types of events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop) by extracting touched paths from git and tool inputs, routing them to relevant documentation via a configurable route map, validating shell commands against destructive patterns, and persisting working state to .codex/context/working-state.md. The design relies on bounded constants (MAX_CONTEXT_DOCS=6, MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS=5000, MAX_WORKING_STATE_LINES=80) to keep persisted context small, and safe_fallback() to prevent hooks from bricking sessions on errors.

A future editor must respect these contracts: (1) route_context_docs() returns at most MAX_CONTEXT_DOCS docs, sized for inclusion in LLM context; (2) PATH_RE and KEYWORD_PATHS regex patterns must stay synchronized with actual repository structure or context routing silently fails; (3) DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS blocks are security-critical; (4) WorkingState must fit within MAX_WORKING_STATE_LINES or recent context truncates silently; (5) module-map.json at .codex/context/module-map.json can override DEFAULT_ROUTE_MAP but not the regex patterns.

data_constants: MAX_CONTEXT_DOCS (6): bounds returned docs, keep ≤ context window
MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS (5000): bounds additional_context_for_paths() output
MAX_WORKING_STATE_LINES (80): bounds render_working_state(), truncates silently
MAX_RECENT_TOOLS (8): bounds tools in working state snapshot
DEFAULT_CONTEXT_DOCS: engineering-principles.md and tests-and-reproduction.md, always loaded
DEFAULT_ROUTE_MAP: path prefixes map to .repo_context/ docs, must match src/datasystem structure
DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS: security-critical blocks for git reset/checkout/clean, rm, sudo, chmod
KEYWORD_PATHS: keywords→paths (worker, master, etcd, client, test, cmake) for natural language routing
PATH_RE: regex matching paths in text (src/datasystem/*, tests, cmake, etc.)
RTK_EXPECTED: convention check for rtk tool prefix
edit_checklist.warning: If PATH_RE or KEYWORD_PATHS regex patterns diverge from actual repository structure (e.g., src/datasystem renamed), context routing silently fails to find relevant docs with no error.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_ROUTE_MAP
key_constructs.rationale: Hardcoded path-prefix-to-docs mapping (worker→worker-runtime, master→overview, etcd→etcd-metadata, client→client-sdk, tests→test-suite-design, etc.). Fallback if module-map.json missing. Must stay in sync with repository structure.

key_constructs.name: WorkingState
key_constructs.rationale: Dataclass holding all state for session recovery: repo_root, event_name, touched_paths, recent_tools, last_assistant_message. Fed to render_working_state() which produces markdown written to .codex/context/working-state.md.

key_constructs.name: run_git
key_constructs.signature: run_git(repo_root: Path, args: list[str], timeout_s: int=3)

key_constructs.name: route_context_docs
key_constructs.rationale: Core routing function mapping touched paths to relevant docs via DEFAULT_ROUTE_MAP or module-map.json. Returns at most MAX_CONTEXT_DOCS docs; critical for keeping context bounded.

key_constructs.name: PATH_RE
key_constructs.rationale: Compiled regex extracting file paths from text (src/datasystem/*, include/datasystem, tests, cmake, etc.). Used by extract_paths_from_text(). Must be updated if repo structure changes.

key_constructs.name: KEYWORD_PATHS
key_constructs.rationale: List of (regex, synthetic_path) tuples mapping keywords to paths (worker/object_cache, master/metadata, etcd/lease, client/sdk, test/tests, cmake/build). Enables natural language routing.

key_constructs.name: DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS
key_constructs.rationale: Security-critical (regex, reason) tuples blocking git reset --hard, checkout --, clean -f, rm -rf, sudo, chmod 777. Breaking any pattern opens system to unsafe commands.

key_constructs.name: RTK_EXPECTED
key_constructs.rationale: Regex matching conventional repository commands (git, rg, sed, python, cmake, etc.). Advisory-only check suggesting rtk prefix if not used; emits advisory message, not blocking.

key_constructs.name: evaluate_bash_command
key_constructs.rationale: Safety gate checking shell commands against DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS. Returns CommandDecision with blocked flag; called from handle_pre_tool_use. Security-critical.

key_constructs.signature: additional_context_for_paths(paths: list[str], repo_root: Path | None=None, advisory: str='')

key_constructs.name: hook_specific
key_constructs.signature: hook_specific(event_name: str, additional_context: str='', extra: dict[str, Any] | None=None)

key_constructs.name: HANDLERS
key_constructs.rationale: Dict routing event names to handler functions (SessionStart→handle_session_start, etc.). Dispatched by run_event() which also catches exceptions via safe_fallback().

entities: Hook events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop)
Working state (touched files, recent tools, git status, last message)
Repository structure (src/datasystem, tests, cmake, client, etc.)
capabilities: Routes code paths to relevant documentation based on git changes and keyword matching
Blocks destructive git and shell commands (reset --hard, checkout --, clean -f, rm -rf, sudo, chmod 777) at hook time
Persists working context (touched files, recent tools, git branch/status) to markdown for session recovery
Extracts file paths from text, tool inputs, and git status using compiled regex and keyword patterns
Validates shell commands against safety policy and emits advisory for rtk convention compliance

pitfalls.description: Path normalization is inconsistent: normalize_path() is called in load_route_map() and route_context_docs() but not in extract_paths_from_text() or extract_tool_paths().
pitfalls.consequence: Paths with leading ./ or quotes may not match route prefixes, causing context routing to miss docs or return duplicates.
pitfalls.prevention: Always normalize extracted paths before adding to lists; consider normalizing at entry point in extract_paths_from_text().

pitfalls.description: render_working_state() silently truncates to MAX_WORKING_STATE_LINES (~80 lines) with no warning when tail is dropped.
pitfalls.consequence: Important context lost from working state if touched paths + docs + tools exceed 80 lines.
pitfalls.prevention: Verify touched paths/docs fit within ~80 lines before editing; add warning if truncation occurs.

pitfalls.description: module-map.json can override DEFAULT_ROUTE_MAP, but PATH_RE and KEYWORD_PATHS are hardcoded and cannot be overridden.
pitfalls.consequence: New directories or keywords won't be routed unless source code patterns are updated.
pitfalls.prevention: If adding new top-level directories, update both PATH_RE and KEYWORD_PATHS; document in comments.

pitfalls.description: extract_tool_paths() assumes tool_input is dict or string, doesn't handle lists or nested structures.
pitfalls.consequence: Complex tool inputs with lists or nested dicts lose path information, reducing routing accuracy.
pitfalls.prevention: Extend extract_tool_paths() to handle lists/nested structures if tools start passing complex inputs.

pitfalls.description: run_git() has hardcoded 3-second timeout that fails silently (returns empty string) on slow repos.
pitfalls.consequence: git_touched_paths() returns empty on slow operations, losing visibility of modified files.
pitfalls.prevention: Test on large repos or make timeout configurable; monitor for silent failures.

reading_guide.start: HANDLERS dict (line 446) and run_event() (line 464)—shows event-dispatch architecture: events→handlers→hook output.
key: route_context_docs (line 148): central routing engine mapping paths to docs; called by all handlers needing documentation context
evaluate_bash_command (line 207): safety gate for shell execution; blocks destructive commands via DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS
WorkingState and render_working_state (lines 68, 343): session persistence; shows what state is saved and how it's formatted for recovery
handle_pre_tool_use and handle_post_tool_use (lines 246, 416): bracket tool execution with context routing and state updates
reading_guide.skip: Detailed regex syntax in PATH_RE, KEYWORD_PATTERNS, DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS (lines 159–204)—understand they exist and how they're used, but regex internals not critical; git helper functions run_git, git_touched_paths, summarize_git (lines 95–307) are utilities; understand contracts (git operations return strings, touched paths from diff/status) without internalizing git plumbing.

python/yr/datasystem/ds_tensor_client.py.description: This module provides a Python interface to a distributed tensor cache client (DsTensorClient) that transfers tensor data between host and device(s) with support for both PyTorch and MindSpore frameworks. It wraps low-level C++ bindings (via pybind11) and enforces strict tensor validation: contiguous memory layout, correct device type (npu/Ascend), valid data pointers, and proper storage offset handling for tensor views. The design treats PyTorch and MindSpore tensors asymmetrically—MindSpore tensors on Ascend devices require adding storage_offset to the base data pointer, a distinction critical for correctness that was fixed in commit 4db2fe50 after a tensor view address bug.

data_constants: Device types: 'Ascend' and 'npu' in _check_tensor_device_type and _is_ms_tensor
Default timeouts: connect_timeout_ms=60000ms, req_timeout_ms=0 (defaults to connect_timeout if ≤0)
WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE_EVICT for mset_page_attn_blockwise_d2h cache behavior
CopyRange fields: src_offset, dst_offset, length (all in bytes)
PageAttnUtils methods wrap C++ bindings that expect device_idx, block_ids, element sizes
edit_checklist.warning: Device ID handling in _page_attn_blockwise_dbls is asymmetric: uses passed device_id for MindSpore but extracts it from tensor.device.index for PyTorch—passing wrong device_id will silently corrupt C++ calls for PyTorch tensors.

historical_insights.title: Incorrect tensor view data address for PyTorch
historical_insights.problem: Obtaining incorrect memory address when accessing PyTorch tensor view data
historical_insights.root_cause: PyTorch tensor views have storage_offset that wasn't being accounted for in the data pointer calculation; MindSpore requires this adjustment but PyTorch logic was missing it or vice versa
historical_insights.solution: Modified _get_tensor_data_ptr to conditionally add (storage_offset() * element_size()) for MindSpore tensors only, leaving PyTorch tensors as raw data_ptr

key_constructs.name: DsTensorClient
key_constructs.docstring:
Data system Tensor Cache Client management for python, which provides named object support for
efficient transfer of Tensor data between D2D or H2D/D2H.

Args:
    host(str): The host of the worker address.
    port(int): The port of the worker address.
    device_id (int): The identifier of the device.
    connect_timeout_ms(int): The timeout_ms interval for the connection between the client and worker.
    token(str): A string used for authentication.
    client_public_key(str): The client's public key, for curve authentication.
    client_private_key(str): The client's private key, for curve authentication.
    server_public_key(str): The worker server's public key, for curve authentication.
    req_timeout_ms(int): The timeout of request, when req_timeout_ms<=0, req_timeout_ms is the same with
    connect_timeout_ms.
    enable_remote_h2d(bool): Whether the remote h2d feature is enabled or not, default off.
Raises:
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if the client failed to invoke api.
key_constructs.rationale: Main client class that orchestrates all tensor operations. Holds HeteroClient connection and device_id. Every public method enforces tensor validation (contiguity, device type, data pointer) before delegating to async operations.

key_constructs.name: _get_tensor_device_type
key_constructs.docstring: Safely get tensor device type with proper error handling
key_constructs.rationale: Critical discriminator for PyTorch vs MindSpore. Raises AttributeError if tensor.device is a string (indicating msadapter is missing). Callers must handle this for proper user guidance.

key_constructs.name: _is_ms_tensor
key_constructs.docstring: check if the tensor is mindspore type
key_constructs.rationale: Conditional used to determine device.index vs passed device_id in page attention methods. Checks device type is 'Ascend' (MindSpore's device type string).

key_constructs.docstring: check the tensor type

key_constructs.docstring:
Check if the tensor memory layout is contiguous.

Args:

Raises:

key_constructs.rationale: Enforced by 12 call sites. Non-contiguous tensors raise TypeError with remediation hints (call .contiguous(), check transpose/narrow operations). This validation is load-bearing.

key_constructs.rationale: Must be used for all tensor pointer accesses. Handles MindSpore storage_offset adjustment; raw tensor.data_ptr() will give wrong address for views on MindSpore. See commit 4db2fe50.

key_constructs.name: mset_d2h
key_constructs.signature: mset_d2h(self, keys: List[str], tensors: List[Tensor], set_param: SetParam=SetParam())
key_constructs.docstring:
Write the tensors of the device to the host. If the data of the device contains multiple memory addresses,
the device automatically combines data and writes the data to the host.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys associated with the tensors.
                      Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to send.
    set_param(SetParam): The set param , default value:
        write_mode = WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE
        ttl_second = 0
        existence = ExistenceOpt.NONE
        cache_type = CacheType.MEMEORY

Returns:
    None.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to mset_d2h the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: mget_h2d
key_constructs.signature: mget_h2d(self, keys: List[str], tensors: List[Tensor], sub_timeout_ms: int=0)
key_constructs.docstring:
Obtain tensors from the host and write the tensors to the device.
mget_h2d and mset_d2h must be used together.
If multiple memory addresses are combined and written to the host during mset_d2h, the host data is
automatically split into multiple memory addresses and written to the device in mget_h2d.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys associated with the tensors.
                      Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to store the retrieved data.
    sub_timeout_ms (int, optional): Timeout for the subscription in milliseconds.
        Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The keys that failed to get.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to mget_h2d the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

Returns:

Delete the tensor datas of keys from worker.
The delete interface works with mget_h2d and mset_d2h.

Returns:

key_constructs.name: dev_mset
key_constructs.docstring:
The data system caches data on the device and writes the metadata of the key corresponding to
tensors to the data system so that other clients can access the data system.
dev_mset and dev_mget must be used together. Heterogeneous objects are not automatically deleted after
dev_mget is executed. If an object is no longer used, invoke dev_local_delete or dev_delete to delete it.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_mset and dev_mget interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list. Constraint: The number of keys
                cannot exceed 10,000.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to store the retrieved data.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The failed dev_mset keys.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to set the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_mget
key_constructs.signature: dev_mget(self, keys: List[str], tensors: List[Tensor], sub_timeout_ms: int=0)
key_constructs.docstring:
Obtains data from the device and writes the data to tensors. Data is transmitted directly through
the device-to-device channel.
dev_mset and dev_mget must be used together. Heterogeneous objects are not automatically deleted after
dev_mget is executed. If an object is no longer used, invoke dev_local_delete or dev_delete to delete it.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_mset and dev_mget interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.
During the execution of dev_mget, do not exit the process where dev_mset is executed. Otherwise, dev_mget fails.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list. Constraint: The number of keys
                cannot exceed 10,000.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to store the retrieved data.
    sub_timeout_ms(int): The sub_timeout_ms of the get operation.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The failed dev_mget keys.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to get the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_mget_into_tensor
key_constructs.signature: dev_mget_into_tensor(self, keys: List[str], tensor: Tensor, copy_ranges: List[CopyRange], sub_timeout_ms: int=60000)
key_constructs.docstring:
Retrieves data from the device for multiple keys and copies each data segment into specified
locations within a single target tensor, based on user-defined copy ranges.
The data is transmitted directly through the device-to-device channel.

This method is designed to work in conjunction with dev_mset. Heterogeneous objects are not automatically
deleted after dev_mget_into_tensor is executed. If an object is no longer needed, you should explicitly invoke
dev_local_delete、async_dev_delete or dev_delete to remove it.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): A list of keys corresponding to the data on the device.
                    Constraint: The number of keys must not exceed 10,000.
    tensor (Tensor): The target tensor into which the retrieved data will be copied.
                    The tensor must have sufficient size to accommodate all specified copy ranges.
    copy_ranges (List[CopyRange]): A list of CopyRange named tuples specifying the source offsets,
                                destination offsets (within the tensor), and lengths (in bytes)
                                for each data segment to be copied.
                                Each CopyRange contains:
                                    - src_offset (int): Starting offset in the source (in bytes).
                                    - dst_offset (int): Starting offset in the destination tensor (in bytes).
                                    - length (int): Length of the data to be copied (in bytes).
    sub_timeout_ms (int, optional): The sub-timeout (in milliseconds) for the get operation. Default is 60s.

Returns:
    List[str]: A list of keys for which the dev_mget_into_tensor operation failed.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raised when the operation fails to retrieve the values for all specified keys.
    TypeError: Raised when one or more input parameters are of an invalid type.

key_constructs.name: dev_delete
key_constructs.docstring:
Delete the device info from the host.
The dev_delete interface is used together with the dev_mset / dev_mget interface.

Args:
    keys(list): The data list of string type. Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The failed delete keys.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if fails to delete the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: async_dev_delete
key_constructs.docstring:
Delete the device info from the host asynchronously.
The async_dev_delete interface is used together with the dev_mset / dev_mget interface.

Args:
    keys(list): The data list of string type. Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.

Returns:
    Future: A Future object representing the asynchronous operations. When calling `future.get()`, if there are
            partial failures, the keys of the failed operations are returned; if all keys fail, an RuntimeError
            exception is thrown.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_local_delete
key_constructs.docstring:
dev_local_delete interface. After calling this interface, the data replica stored in the data system by the
current client connection will be deleted.
The dev_local_delete interface is used together with the dev_mset / dev_mget interface.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list. Constraint: The number of keys
                cannot exceed 10,000.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The keys that failed to be deleted.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if fails to get the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_send
key_constructs.docstring:
Send the tensors cache on the device as a heterogeneous object of the data system.

Heterogeneous objects can be obtained through dev_recv.
dev_send and dev_recv must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_send and dev_recv interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through dev_recv, the data system automatically deletes the heterogeneous object
and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): A list of keys corresponding to the tensor list.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors corresponding to the keys.

Returns:
    List[Future]: A list of Future objects representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to dev_send the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_recv
key_constructs.docstring:
Receive heterogeneous objects of the data system and writes data to tensors.

Tensor data is directly transmitted through the device-to-device channel.
dev_send and dev_recv must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_send and dev_recv interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through dev_recv, the data system automatically deletes the heterogeneous object
and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.
During the execution of dev_recv, do not exit the process where dev_send is executed. Otherwise,
dev_recv fails.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): A list of keys corresponding to the tensor list.
    tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to store the retrieved data.

Returns:
    List[Future]: A list of Future objects representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to dev_recv the values of all keys.

key_constructs.name: put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d
key_constructs.signature: put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d(self, keys: list[str], layer_tensors: list[Tensor], block_ids: list[int])
key_constructs.docstring:
Put PagedAttention layer-wise tensors on the device as a heterogeneous object of the data system.

Heterogeneous objects can be obtained through get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d.
put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d and get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d and
get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d interfaces cannot belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d, the data system automatically
deletes the heterogeneous object and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys for the Device heterogeneous object.
        Constraint: Maximum 10,000 keys allowed.
    layer_tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to put.
        Constraint: All tensors must have contiguous memory layout.
    block_ids (List[int]): List of block IDs to be published.

Returns:
    List[Future]: A list of Future objects representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to put the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d
key_constructs.signature: get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d(self, keys: list[str], layer_tensors: list[Tensor], block_ids: list[int])
key_constructs.docstring:
Receive heterogeneous objects of the data system and writes data to PagedAttention layer-wise tensors.

Tensor data is directly transmitted through the device-to-device channel.
put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d and get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d and
get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d interfaces cannot belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d, the data system automatically
deletes the heterogeneous object and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.
During the execution of get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d, do not exit the process where put_page_attn_layerwise_d2d
is executed.
Otherwise, get_page_attn_layerwise_d2d fails.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys for the Device heterogeneous object.
        Constraint: Maximum 10,000 keys allowed.
    layer_tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors corresponding to the keys.
        Constraint: All tensors must have contiguous memory layout.
    block_ids (List[int]): List of block IDs to be subscribed.

Returns:
    List[Future]: A list of Future objects representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to get the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: mset_page_attn_blockwise_d2h
key_constructs.signature: mset_page_attn_blockwise_d2h(self, keys: list[str], layer_tensors: list[Tensor], block_ids: list[int])
key_constructs.docstring:
Write the PagedAttention block-wise tensors of the device to the host asynchronously.

If the data of the device contains multiple memory addresses, the device automatically combines data and writes
the data to the host.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys for the Host.
        Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.
    layer_tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to send from device to host.
    block_ids (List[int]): List of block IDs to be published.

Returns:
    Future: A Future object representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to mset_page_attn_blockwise_d2h the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: mget_page_attn_blockwise_h2d
key_constructs.signature: mget_page_attn_blockwise_h2d(self, keys: list[str], layer_tensors: list[Tensor], block_ids: list[int], sub_timeout_ms: int=0)
key_constructs.docstring:
Obtain the PagedAttention layer-wise tensors from the host and write the tensors to the device asynchronously.

If multiple memory addresses are combined and written to the host during mset_page_attn_blockwise_d2h,
the host data is automatically split into multiple memory addresses and written to the device in
mget_page_attn_blockwise_h2d.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys (List[str]): List of keys for the Host.
        Constraint: Maximum 10,000 keys allowed.
    layer_tensors (List[Tensor]): List of tensors to store the retrieved data.
        Constraint: All tensors must have contiguous memory layout.
    block_ids (List[int]): List of block IDs to be subscribed.
    sub_timeout_ms (int): Subscription timeout in milliseconds.
        Default: 0 (no timeout).

Returns:
    Future: A Future object representing the asynchronous operations.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to mget_page_attn_blockwise_h2d the values of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to validate tensor contiguity before passing to a data operation (e.g., mset_d2h, async_mget_h2d)
pitfalls.consequence: Undefined behavior or data corruption when non-contiguous tensor memory is interpreted as contiguous by C++ code
pitfalls.prevention: All public methods that accept tensors MUST call _check_tensors_is_contiguous(layer_tensors) early. This is enforced in existing methods but must be preserved in any new tensor-accepting methods.

pitfalls.description: Mixing PyTorch and MindSpore tensors in the same layer_tensors list, or passing tensors from different devices
pitfalls.consequence: Runtime errors in _construct_layerwise_tensors validation, or C++ bindings receive inconsistent device indices
pitfalls.prevention: _construct_layerwise_tensors already validates all tensors have same device.index, but _check_tensor_device_type must be called first to ensure compatible device type (npu/Ascend)

pitfalls.description: Calling page attention methods with device_id that doesn't match the actual tensor device for PyTorch (or doesn't match self._device_id for MindSpore)
pitfalls.consequence: C++ bindings receive wrong device index; operations may fail silently or corrupt memory on wrong device
pitfalls.prevention: For PyTorch, device_id is extracted from tensor, so don't override. For MindSpore, ensure self._device_id matches the tensors' device. In _page_attn_blockwise_dbls, the asymmetric logic should not be changed without validating device_id consistency.

pitfalls.description: Assuming tensor.data_ptr() is always valid; empty or uninitialized tensors return 0
pitfalls.consequence: RuntimeError raised with message about null data_ptr, halting operation
pitfalls.prevention: _get_tensor_data_ptr already checks for null pointer; ensure all tensor pointer accesses go through this method, not raw tensor.data_ptr()

scripts/generate_trace_latency_report.py.description: Generates interactive HTML reports analyzing KVCache latency by correlating SDK client access logs with Worker logs (access + info) via trace IDs. Segments end-to-end latency into phases (request delay, queue wait, RPC network, master processing, remote operations, URMA writes) and produces ECharts visualizations with percentile statistics (P50/P90/P99/P99.99), per-request timelines, and top-N high-latency traces with error/retry details. Non-obvious contracts: the latency segmentation model (documented in docstring) drives implementation in `_fill_get` and `_fill_set`, using keys like 'get_delay_us', 'get_qwait_us', 'remote_net_us' that flow into chart data structure D[...]. Log format is rigid—pipe-separated fields at fixed indices (0=ts, 3=host, 5=trace_id, 9=duration_us) and regex patterns (_RE_GET_DONE, _RE_ZMQ, etc.) must match exact worker log output or parsing silently fails.

data_constants: PIPE_RE: regex for log field splitting—changing delimiters breaks all parsing
_RE_* regex patterns (11 of them): must match actual worker log message formats or latency extraction fails silently
Field indices in _access() (0, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10): positions in pipe-separated SDK access logs

Latency segment keys ('get_delay_us', 'get_qwait_us', 'master_proc_us', 'remote_net_us', 'urma_us', 'x_*' exceed counters): used in _fill_get/_fill_set and must match chart data keys D['get_*'], D['set_*']
Percentile list [50, 90, 99, 99.9, 99.99]: used in _seg_stat for all statistics
edit_checklist.warning: Regex patterns (_RE_GET_DONE, _RE_MASTER_DONE, _RE_ZMQ, etc.) and field indices in _access() are fragile—worker/SDK log format changes cause silent parsing failures with no errors or partial data.

src/datasystem/common/rdma/fast_transpor.description:
Wrapper facade that provides a unified interface for fast transport operations (URMA and UCP/RDMA) in the datasystem. The file is heavily feature-gated with compile-time preprocessor directives (`USE_URMA`, `USE_RDMA`, `BUILD_HETERO`) and delegates all substantive work to either UrmaManager or UcpManager instances based on configuration.

The file enforces a critical design contract: functions accept parameters that may be unused depending on enabled transport backends, requiring void-casts to suppress warnings. This pattern conceals potential bugs in disabled code paths. Key decisions preserved: (1) segment registration is controlled by per-backend flags (FLAGS_urma_register_whole_arena, IsRegisterWholeArenaEnabled) determining whether the whole arena or per-object memory is registered; (2) handshake construction involves jetty/JFR resource allocation in ConstructHandshakePb that must outlive reconnections; (3) connection stability checking and instance ID retrieval both have TCP fallback paths added in recent commits (47dda1a1).

data_constants: GetSegmentInfoFromShmUnit logic: FLAGS_urma_register_whole_arena and IsRegisterWholeArenaEnabled() control memory registration strategy
Parameter void-casting pattern: all delegated functions must cast unused parameters to (void) to suppress warnings in disabled configurations
Function return value: all wrapper functions return Status::OK() as fallback if neither backend is enabled
edit_checklist.warning: Conditional compilation means code paths are untested in disabled configurations; editing a gated function without checking the actual backend implementation can hide bugs in unsupported build modes.

historical_insights.title: Jetty/JFR per-connection resource lifecycle
historical_insights.problem: Handshake construction needed to allocate and reuse Jetty resources across reconnections without leaking or creating duplicates
historical_insights.root_cause: JFR (Jetty For Recv) resources were previously created per-connection rather than reused; per-connection JFS/JFR support (39a0f890) required lifecycle management
historical_insights.solution: ConstructHandshakePb now calls GetOrCreateLocalJetty to allocate once and reuse (feddf7de, 8043aca4); added jetty diagnostics (feddf7de)

historical_insights.title: Delayed fast transport initialization
historical_insights.problem: Fast transport initialization was happening too early, before heartbeat was active, causing connection failures
historical_insights.root_cause: InitializeFastTransportManager was called before cluster state was stable
historical_insights.solution: Moved initialization to occur after heartbeat activation (27dcbcf2); added INJECT_POINT for testing delays

historical_insights.title: TCP fallback when fast transport disabled
historical_insights.problem: Code using GetLocalTransportInstanceId failed when fast transport was disabled at runtime
historical_insights.root_cause: Function had no error path for DISABLE_RDMA scenarios
historical_insights.solution: Added K_URMA_ERROR return when neither URMA nor UCP is enabled (visible in GetLocalTransportInstanceId return clause)

historical_insights.title: DISABLE_RDMA configuration not propagated
historical_insights.problem: URMA remote writer failed to respect DISABLE_RDMA flag in some code paths
historical_insights.root_cause: Wrapper was calling UrmaManager methods without checking if RDMA was explicitly disabled
historical_insights.solution: Added explicit flag checks in delegation functions (6c59487f)

python/yr/datasystem/hetero_client.py.description: Python wrapper for a C++ heterogeneous data system client (pybind11 bindings), enabling host-to-device (H2D) and device-to-host (D2H) memory transfers on NPU devices. Manages asynchronous operations via `Future`, represents device memory segments as `Blob` objects grouped in `DeviceBlobList`, and delegates to native C++ via wrapped objects that convert to C++ equivalents through `to_ds_*()` methods. Non-obvious contracts: dual operation pairs (`mget_h2d`+`mset_d2h` for host memory, `dev_mget`+`dev_mset` for device memory), error pattern (`status.is_error()` check then raise on error), and `_change_blob_list_from_py_to_cpp()` uses duck typing to accept both Python wrappers and native C++ objects interchangeably.

data_constants: Future.get() timeout_ms default: 60000 — preserve for backward compatibility across 515 files
HeteroClient.__init__() connect_timeout_ms default: 60000; req_timeout_ms default: 0
Wrapper method names: to_ds_blob(), to_ds_blob_list() must match C++ pybind11 API expectations
Error idiom: status.is_error() → raise RuntimeError(status.to_string()) replicated across all public methods

key_constructs.docstring:
Write the data of the device to the host. If the BLOB of the device contains multiple memory addresses,
the device automatically combines data and writes the data to the host.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys(list): The data list of string type. Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.
    data_blob_list(list): The list of data info.
    set_param(SetParam): The set param , default value:
        write_mode = WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE
        ttl_second = 0
        existence = ExistenceOpt.NONE
        cache_type = CacheType.MEMEORY

Returns:
    None.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to put device objects.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: async_mget_h2d
key_constructs.signature: async_mget_h2d(self, keys: list, data_blob_list: list, sub_timeout_ms: int)
key_constructs.docstring:
Obtain data from the host and write the data to the device.
mget_h2d and mset_d2h must be used together.
If multiple memory addresses are combined and written to the host during mset_d2h, the host data is
automatically split into multiple memory addresses and written to the device in mget_h2d.
If the key of the host is no longer used, you can call the delete interface to delete it.

Args:
    keys(list): Keys in the host. Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.
    data_blob_list(list): The list of data info.
    sub_timeout_ms(int): The sub_timeout_ms of the get operation.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The keys that failed to get.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to get device objects.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: delete
key_constructs.docstring:
Delete the key from the host.
The delete interface works with mget_h2d and mset_d2h.

Args:
    keys(list): The data list of string type. Constraint: The number of keys cannot exceed 10,000.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The keys that failed to be deleted.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to delete the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.
key_constructs.rationale: High-traffic method (996 refs, 294 files); error handling and validation pattern is reference implementation for other methods.

key_constructs.name: dev_publish
key_constructs.docstring:
Publish the memory on the device as a heterogeneous object of the data system.
Heterogeneous objects can be obtained through dev_subscribe.
dev_publish and dev_subscribe must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_publish and dev_subscribe interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through dev_subscribe, the data system automatically deletes the heterogeneous object
and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list.
    data_blob_list(list): A list of structures describing the Device memory.

Returns:
    List[Future] is returned. You can use the get() method of the future object
    to wait for and access the result of HcclRecv.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to publish the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_subscribe
key_constructs.docstring:
Subscribes to heterogeneous objects of the data system and writes data to data_blob_list.
Data is directly transmitted through the device-to-device channel.
dev_publish and dev_subscribe must be used together.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_publish and dev_subscribe interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.
After data is obtained through dev_subscribe, the data system automatically deletes the heterogeneous object
and does not manage the device memory corresponding to the object.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list.
    data_blob_list(list): A list of structures describing the Device memory.

Returns:
    List[Future] is returned. You can use the get() method of the future object
    to wait for and access the result of HcclRecv.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to subscribe to the values of all keys fails.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_mset
key_constructs.docstring:
The data system caches data on the device and writes the metadata of the key corresponding to
data_blob_list to the data system so that other clients can access the data system.
dev_mset and dev_mget must be used together. Heterogeneous objects are not automatically deleted after
dev_mget is executed. If an object is no longer used, invoke dev_local_delete or dev_delete to delete it.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_mset and dev_mget interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list. Constraint: The number of keys
                cannot exceed 10,000.
    data_blob_list(list): A list of structures describing the Device memory.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The failed dev_mset keys.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to set the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_mget
key_constructs.docstring:
Obtains data from the device and writes the data to data_blob_list. Data is transmitted directly through
the device-to-device channel.
dev_mset and dev_mget must be used together. Heterogeneous objects are not automatically deleted after
dev_mget is executed. If an object is no longer used, invoke dev_local_delete or dev_delete to delete it.
The device memory addresses in the input parameters of the dev_mset and dev_mget interfaces cannot
belong to the same NPU.

Args:
    keys(list): A list of keys corresponding to the data_blob_list. Constraint: The number of keys
                cannot exceed 10,000.
    data_blob_list(list): A list of structures describing the Device memory.
    sub_timeout_ms(int): The sub_timeout_ms of the get operation.

Returns:
    failed_keys(list): The failed dev_mget keys.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if failing to get the value of all keys.
    TypeError: Raise a type error if the input parameter is invalid.

key_constructs.name: dev_delete

key_constructs.name: generate_key
key_constructs.docstring:
Generate a key with the Worker UUID of the data system.

Returns:
    key(string): The unique key, if the key fails to be generated, an empty string is returned.

Examples:
    >>> from yr.datasystem.hetero_client import HeteroClient
    >>> client = HeteroClient('127.0.0.1', 18482)
    >>> client.init()
    >>> client.generate_key()

key_constructs.name: exist
key_constructs.docstring:
Check the existence of the given keys in the data system.

Args:

Returns:

Raises:

    >>> client = HeteroClient('127.0.0.1', 18482)

    >>> client.exist(['key1', 'key2', 'key3'])

pitfalls.description: DeviceBlobList.append_blob() line 714 calls self.blob.append(blob) but __init__ only creates self.blob_list
pitfalls.consequence: AttributeError at runtime; method is untested (zero references) so bug is undetected
pitfalls.prevention: Either fix (rename self.blob to self.blob_list) or remove method entirely; verify it's not part of public API before deleting

pitfalls.description: Five methods have zero references (set_dev_ptr, set_dev_idx, set_src_offset, get_blob_list, append_blob) — unclear if public API, internal debug, or dead code
pitfalls.consequence: Removing them could break external code not captured in reference counts; keeping buggy ones risks silent failures
pitfalls.prevention: Check external users, documentation, or version history before removal; consider deprecation warnings if unsure

pitfalls.description: Documented constraints ('keys cannot exceed 10,000') are not validated in Python, only in C++ backend
pitfalls.consequence: Invalid inputs pass Python validation but fail in C++ with cryptic error messages from status.to_string()
pitfalls.prevention: Add explicit len(keys) > 10000 checks in Python before calling C++ for faster, clearer failure feedback

pitfalls.description: _change_blob_list_from_py_to_cpp() uses hasattr(blob_list, 'to_ds_blob_list') duck typing with no type validation
pitfalls.consequence: Silent type coercion; method existence doesn't guarantee correct return type, errors surface downstream in C++
pitfalls.prevention: Add isinstance() checks or stricter type hints; validate cpp_blob_list is actually a ds.DeviceBlobList before returning

pitfalls.description: Authentication parameters (token, access_key, secret_key, public/private keys) validated only at C++ connection time in init(), not at __init__()
pitfalls.consequence: Invalid credentials only fail during init(), making errors appear later than they should
pitfalls.prevention: Add minimal Python-side validation (e.g., not all empty, minimal length) if credential errors are a support issue

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/servi.description: WorkerOcServiceGetImpl is a service handler implementing GET operations for a distributed object cache in a worker node. It retrieves objects from local cache, remote workers, or master-managed metadata, with support for multi-AZ failover, location redirects during migration, and proactive URMA connection warmup. The class manages async location updates via AsyncUpdateLocationManager, enforces rate limiting on voluntary scale-down migrations, provides L2 cache access for primary copies, and handles complex retry logic across multiple failure modes—all coordinated through concurrent RPC handlers and background thread pools.

data_constants: QueryMetaMap: unordered_map<string, master::QueryMetaInfoPb>—query result cache key
OBJECTS_NOT_EXIST_IDX/OBJECTS_PUZZLED_IDX: tuple indices (0/1) in ObjectKeysQueryMetaFailed—must preserve for query result categorization
TbbTransportStubTable: concurrent_hash_map<string, shared_ptr<WorkerRemoteWorkerTransApi>>—thread-safe stub cache
asyncUpdateLocationManager_: unique_ptr lifecycle—must call Stop() in destructor and Init() after construction
rateController_: MigrateDataRateController for NotifyRemoteGet bandwidth control—passed in constructor, used in UpdateNotifyRemoteGetRateLimit
edit_checklist.warning: Concurrent async metadata updates, redirect logic, and multi-AZ retry paths interact with rate-limited migration triggers; changes to timeout, ordering, or flag handling in location update flows can silently break distributed consistency during scale-down.

historical_insights.title: URMA connection latency on first get

historical_insights.title: Bandwidth saturation during scale-down migrations

historical_insights.root_cause: No rate limiting on NotifyRemoteGet data transfer volume
historical_insights.solution: Added MigrateDataRateController integration with UpdateNotifyRemoteGetRateLimit callback to enforce bandwidth caps

capabilities: Retrieve objects from local cache or remote workers with master metadata lookup

pitfalls.description: AsyncUpdateLocationManager not stopped before destruction
pitfalls.consequence: Background worker threads continue executing async callbacks after object is freed, causing use-after-free and memory corruption
pitfalls.prevention: Destructor calls Stop() before nulling the unique_ptr; never reorder destructor or skip Stop() call

pitfalls.description: Redirect metadata responses ignored in UpdateLocationRedirectKeysToRetry flow
pitfalls.consequence: Objects stored at new location after migration still queried at old address, causing persistent get failures
pitfalls.prevention: Always check RedirectMetaInfo in responses and move affected keys to retryParams, not failedIds

pitfalls.description: Multi-AZ metadata query fallback not triggered for transient master errors
pitfalls.consequence: Temporary primary AZ master outage blocks access to objects stored in other AZs until timeout expires
pitfalls.prevention: In ProcessQueryMetaFailedObjsWhenMetaStoredInEtcd, ensure objectKeysMayInOtherAz is populated and retry loop is entered

pitfalls.description: Rate limit accounting incomplete when NotifyRemoteGetRspPb returned
pitfalls.consequence: Scale-down migration exceeds configured bandwidth ceiling, causing SLO violations for concurrent workloads
pitfalls.prevention: Call UpdateNotifyRemoteGetRateLimit with actual migratedBytes before response completion in PostProcessRemoteGetInNotificationImpl

pitfalls.description: URMA connection keep-alive not maintained after WarmupGetObjectFromRemoteWorker
pitfalls.consequence: Connections established by warmup idle-disconnect before workload arrives, negating latency benefit
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure TryReconnectRemoteWorker is called with lastResult check to detect stale connections and refresh proactively

src/datasystem/worker/worker_service_imp.description: Worker service implementation managing IPC socket setup, client connection caching, and FD passing for a distributed data system. Handles both Unix domain socket (UDS) and TCP communication paths (SCMTCP), with client authentication, version checking, and heartbeat. Core responsibility: accept incoming connections into an unbounded FD cache (with millisecond-timestamp expiry), send FDs back to clients for shared memory communication, and periodically clean expired cached FDs to prevent resource leaks.

data_constants: UNBOUNDED_FD_MULTIPIER (10): multiplies FLAGS_max_client_num to set maxCacheUnboundedUnixSockFdsCount_
maxCacheUnboundedUnixSockFdsCount_: cache capacity; hard limit on unboundedUnixSockFds_.size() in operator()
CACHE_UDS_SOCK_FD_TIMEOUT_MS: expiry duration in milliseconds for cached FDs; compared in CloseExpiredUnixSockFd()
checkCacheFdIntervalMs (3000): polling interval to trigger CloseExpiredUnixSockFd() in operator() loop
edit_checklist.warning: Cleanup interval (3000ms) is independent of poll timeout; if accept() rate spikes, unboundedUnixSockFds_ can exceed cache size or accumulate expired FDs until next cleanup tick, risking FD exhaustion.

historical_insights.solution: Fixed socket creation and cleanup in Init() method; added unlink() in destructor and proper bind error handling

historical_insights.root_cause: SCMTCP (IPPROTO_SCMTCP over TCP) needed as alternative transport for environments where UDS unavailable
historical_insights.solution: Added conditional TCP socket creation in Init() when FLAGS_shared_memory_worker_port > 0; reuses same FD send/recv protocol

capabilities: Accept incoming UDS or TCP connections and cache them with millisecond-precision creation timestamp
Send file descriptors to clients over sockets for shared memory communication
Authenticate requests against tenant identity and AK/SK credentials
Periodically evict cached FDs older than timeout threshold
Manage client registration, disconnection, and heartbeat state

pitfalls.description: operator() accepts connections without checking if unboundedUnixSockFds_ is already at maxCacheUnboundedUnixSockFdsCount_; only closes fd after insertion fails
pitfalls.consequence: Brief moment where cache size exceeds limit; if close() fails, FD is lost entirely
pitfalls.prevention: Check size before accept (or before insertion) and reject early; add metrics to track overflow events

pitfalls.description: RegisterClient erases serverFd from cache before it finishes sending the FD to client; if send fails, client never receives it and fd is already removed from cache
pitfalls.consequence: Client gets no FD, hangs waiting for registration to complete; FD already closed by then
pitfalls.prevention: Defer erase until after all socket sends complete, or use RAII to ensure fd closes only if sends succeed

pitfalls.description: CloseExpiredUnixSockFd() compares (now - storeTimeMs) > timeout_ms, but GetSteadyClockTimeStampMs() raw milliseconds could overflow after ~49 days; comparison is then undefined
pitfalls.consequence: After 49+ days uptime, all cached FDs appear expired and get closed simultaneously; or never expire
pitfalls.prevention: Store steady_clock time_point directly instead of converting to milliseconds; only convert for logging

pitfalls.description: destructor unlinks sockPath_ but does not verify socket actually closed; if listenFd_ socket is still in use by operator() loop, unlink races with accept()
pitfalls.consequence: Socket file deleted while operator() still polling it; new connections accepted on orphaned FD or accept() returns ENOENT
pitfalls.prevention: Signal operator() to stop before destroying; use atomic interrupt check; or defer unlink until after close

src/datasystem/client/client_worker_comm.description: This header defines the C++ client interface for communicating with a datasystem worker service over RPC, shared memory (SHM), and URMA ultra-bandwidth transports. It establishes three classes: a base attribute holder (ClientWorkerCommonApiAttribute), an abstract interface (IClientWorkerCommonApi), and concrete implementations (ClientWorkerLocalCommonApi, ClientWorkerRemoteCommonApi) that manage connection lifecycle, heartbeat timing, health state, and asynchronous FD reception. The design preserves EDA scenario compatibility through specific heartbeat timeout/interval ranges (15–60ms and 3–30s) and uses atomics extensively to track invoke counts, health status, worker version (for recovery detection), and socket state. Critical contracts: heartbeat must be active before fast transport initialization (27dcbcf2); SHM is enabled only when payload size exceeds shmThreshold_; invoke counting drives lifecycle decisions; and worker version increments on reconnection to detect restarts.

data_constants: MIN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS (15s) / MAX_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS (60s): EDA compatibility—do not change without stakeholder approval
MIN_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS (3s) / MAX_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS (30s): EDA compatibility bounds
CLIENT_TIMEOUT_DESCEND_FACTOR (0.9): request timeout reduction—used in ClientGetRequestTimeout
CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MINUS_MILLISECOND (1000): minimum offset in timeout adjustment
INVALID_SOCKET_FD (-1): sentinel for unavailable/closed sockets
HeartbeatType enum (NO_HEARTBEAT, RPC_HEARTBEAT, UDS_HEARTBEAT): affects SHM endpoint selection and connection type

pitfalls.description: Modifying MIN/MAX_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS or MIN/MAX_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS constants without considering EDA scenario impacts
pitfalls.consequence: EDA deployments become unstable or disconnect; heartbeat timeout/interval violations break compatibility
pitfalls.prevention: Verify all EDA-dependent code paths before changing; add comment citing EDA compatibility requirement; update related tests

pitfalls.description: Calling TryFastTransportAfterHeartbeat before heartbeat is confirmed active (e.g., in PostRegisterClient before heartbeat thread starts)
pitfalls.consequence: Fast transport handshake fails or times out; fallback to slow RPC path; performance degradation
pitfalls.prevention: Only call TryFastTransportAfterHeartbeat after SendHeartbeat has succeeded at least once; add assertion in FastTransportHandshake

pitfalls.description: Assuming invokeCount_ is atomic-safe; ignoring the 'FIXME: not atomic' comment in DecreaseInvokeCount
pitfalls.consequence: Race condition: if invokeCount_ becomes 0 during fetch_sub, false 'invoke count error' logged and counter reset, losing track of active ops; subsequent Disconnect/cleanup may happen while ops in flight
pitfalls.prevention: Use mutex or lock-free compare-swap; never rely on invokeCount_ == 0 check after fetch_sub for critical decisions; consider using reader-writer lock

pitfalls.description: Not verifying shmThreshold_ is set before calling ShmCreateable(size) or assuming SHM is enabled without checking IsShmEnable()
pitfalls.consequence: SHM disabled for large payloads that should use it; incorrect fallback to RPC; performance mismatch vs expected behavior
pitfalls.prevention: Always call IsShmEnable() first; verify shmThreshold_ is initialized in ConstructDecShmUnit; add assertions in ShmCreateable

pitfalls.description: Modifying socket FD lifecycle without synchronizing access to socketFd_ atomic or respecting INVALID_SOCKET_FD sentinel
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-close, double-close, or passing invalid FD to system calls; memory safety violation
pitfalls.prevention: Only modify socketFd_ via atomic operations; always check == INVALID_SOCKET_FD before use; centralize FD cleanup in Disconnect

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to call ScheduleUrmaHandshakeRetry after URMA handshake failure in TryUrmaHandshake
pitfalls.consequence: URMA transport never recovers after transient failure; client stuck in degraded mode with only RPC available
pitfalls.prevention: TryUrmaHandshake must always retry on failure; ensure urmaHandshakeRetryPool_ is initialized; log retry scheduling

cli/benchmark/common.py.description:
cli/benchmark/common.py provides the orchestration framework for benchmark execution, defining abstract base classes that manage the complete lifecycle: validation, initialization, pre-run setup, suite building, and execution. It implements a hierarchical delegation model (BenchmarkBaseCommand → BenchSuite → BenchTestCase → BenchTask) where each level recursively runs its children sequentially through a unified run() method.

Critical design contracts: (1) BenchmarkBaseCommand.logger is a process-level singleton named 'dsbench' initialized once; the _configure_logging() method checks the logger name to prevent re-initialization and handler duplication. (2) BenchTestCase.generate_env() must only produce environment variables prefixed with 'DATASYSTEM_' for the benchmark subprocess. (3) All run() methods return synchronously after recursive traversal—no parallelism at suite/testcase level. (4) Abstract methods (validate, initialize, pre_run) return bool; build_suite returns BenchSuite; run() returns SUCCESS (0) or FAILURE (1), and the lifecycle short-circuits immediately on first False or exception.

data_constants: SUCCESS = 0, FAILURE = 1: must be returned by run() and checked by callers as exit codes
Logger name 'dsbench': hard-coded in _configure_logging() identity check to ensure singleton idempotency
DATASYSTEM_* prefixes: all environment variables in generate_env() must use this prefix
Console handler format '%(message)s': preserve for INFO-level console output
File handler format '[%(asctime)s] [%(levelname)s] %(message)s': preserve for DEBUG-level file logging
Result directory pattern 'result_{name}_{start_time}': referenced in log_dir path generation
edit_checklist.warning: The singleton logger pattern at class level is not thread-safe; concurrent BenchmarkBaseCommand.__init__() calls can corrupt handler state via list iteration and closing.

historical_insights.title: Early return on validation failure
historical_insights.problem: Benchmark suite would attempt to run despite invalid arguments
historical_insights.root_cause: Missing check for validate() result before proceeding to initialization
historical_insights.solution: Added explicit FAILURE return if validate(args) returns False in run() method

key_constructs.rationale: Master orchestrator: validate → initialize → generate_bench_args → pre_run → build_suite → suite.run(). Short-circuits on any False return or exception. Returns SUCCESS (0) or FAILURE (1) to signal completion.

key_constructs.rationale: Master orchestrator: validate → initialize → generate_bench_args → pre_run → build_suite → suite.run(). Short-circuits on any False return or exception. Returns SUCCESS (0) or FAILURE (1) to signal completion.

key_constructs.name: BenchmarkBaseCommand
key_constructs.rationale: Main abstract base for all benchmark CLI commands. Owns the singleton logger and orchestrates the complete run() lifecycle. Subclasses implement 5 abstract methods; the run() method is the entry point that calls them in strict order.

key_constructs.name: _configure_logging
key_constructs.docstring: Configure logging format and handlers.
key_constructs.rationale: Singleton initializer for the 'dsbench' logger. Checks if logger exists with that name to skip re-initialization. Removes old handlers and adds new ones. Critical: the name check prevents handler duplication.

key_constructs.name: validate
key_constructs.docstring: Validate command-line arguments. Return False if invalid.

key_constructs.name: initialize
key_constructs.docstring: Initializes the benchmark runner with the provided arguments.

key_constructs.name: pre_run
key_constructs.docstring: Performs pre-execution checks and setup tasks.

key_constructs.name: build_suite
key_constructs.docstring: Constructs and returns a benchmark suite object.

key_constructs.name: generate_bench_args
key_constructs.docstring: Creates and returns BenchArgs from parsed command-line arguments.
key_constructs.rationale: Factory creating BenchArgs from parsed CLI arguments. Generates timestamped result directory name and CSV filename. Called once per run() invocation.

key_constructs.name: run
key_constructs.docstring: Executes the complete benchmark lifecycle from setup to tear-down.
key_constructs.rationale: Master orchestrator: validate → initialize → generate_bench_args → pre_run → build_suite → suite.run(). Short-circuits on any False return or exception. Returns SUCCESS (0) or FAILURE (1) to signal completion.

capabilities: Orchestrates multi-level benchmark execution from validation through suite completion with early error exit
Manages process-level singleton logger with dual console/file handlers and deferred initialization
Generates DATASYSTEM_-prefixed environment configuration for benchmark subprocesses
Delegates task output processing through before/after hooks via abstract BenchOutputHandler
Enforces deterministic lifecycle flow with bool validation gates and SUCCESS/FAILURE exit codes

pitfalls.description: Changing the logger name string 'dsbench' or the identity check 'logger.name == "dsbench"' breaks _configure_logging() idempotency, causing multiple invocations to add duplicate handlers instead of skipping.
pitfalls.consequence: Duplicate console/file output, file handle leaks, multiple handler chains processing the same message
pitfalls.prevention: Treat the name 'dsbench' and the identity check as immutable. Document the singleton contract. Any logger changes require updates across all callers.

pitfalls.description: Adding environment variables in generate_env() without the DATASYSTEM_ prefix violates the contract expected by benchmark subprocesses, which may ignore or fail on unknown variable names.
pitfalls.consequence: Benchmark tasks receive incomplete configuration, silently use defaults, or crash with missing-variable errors
pitfalls.prevention: Always prefix new environment variables with 'DATASYSTEM_'. Comment why each variable is necessary. Check task.py and executor.py to confirm expected variable names.

pitfalls.description: Modifying the return type or value contracts of abstract methods (validate/initialize/pre_run return bool, build_suite returns BenchSuite, run() returns SUCCESS/FAILURE int) without updating all subclasses causes silent failures where errors pass through.
pitfalls.consequence: run() proceeds past critical failures; subclasses return values that don't satisfy the type contract but fail silently
pitfalls.prevention: Never change abstract method signatures. If extending error handling, use exceptions in the try-except block or introduce a new method.

pitfalls.description: Handler cleanup in _configure_logging() iterates over handlers[:] while closing them. If called concurrently, the iteration may skip handlers or raise RuntimeError on concurrent list modification.
pitfalls.consequence: Some handlers not properly closed (resource leak); concurrent instantiation of BenchmarkBaseCommand fails with RuntimeError
pitfalls.prevention: Add thread synchronization (e.g., threading.Lock) if multi-threaded initialization is possible. Document current single-threaded assumption.
