_configure_logging(): documents the singleton initialization strategy and why the logger name check is essential
BenchSuite.run() and BenchTestCase.run(): shows the recursive delegation pattern and sequential (non-parallel) execution
Abstract method signatures (validate, initialize, pre_run, build_suite): defines the extension contract subclasses must fulfill

src/datasystem/client/object_cache/clien.description: ClientWorkerRemoteApi implements remote RPC communication between client and worker service in a distributed data cache system. It handles object lifecycle operations (Create, Get, Publish, Exist, Expire, GetMetaInfo) with complex timeout handling, URMA-to-TCP fallback, shared memory queue management, and exclusive connection mode support. Critical design contracts: the shared memory queue element size must exactly match worker construction (InitDecreaseQueue comment); exclusive socket paths must be updated after every reconnection/stub recreation; URMA fallback payload filtering is transparent to callers; timeout hierarchy uses clientDeadTimeoutMs > connectTimeoutMs > requestTimeoutMs with specific min() logic in RecreateOCStub/Init.

data_constants: BIT_NUM_OF_INT = 32: used in lockId_ % BIT_NUM_OF_INT for queue slot assignment
RETRY_ERROR_CODE set: determines which RPC failures trigger automatic retries
CLIENT_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US = 2000: threshold for triggering slow RPC logging in LogClientWorkerRpcDone()
P2P_TIMEOUT_MS = 60000: timeout for peer-to-peer operations
P2P_SUBSCRIBE_TIMEOUT_MS = 20000: timeout for subscription operations
edit_checklist.warning: Queue element size in InitDecreaseQueue must stay synchronized with worker implementation or IPC will fail silently; exclusive connection socket path updates must occur in Init, ReconnectWorker, and RecreateOCStub or reconnection will silently use wrong socket.

historical_insights.title: URMA fallback TCP traffic exhausting limits
historical_insights.problem: URMA (high-speed RDMA) payload fallback to TCP had no traffic limit, causing congestion
historical_insights.root_cause: AppendPublishPayload accepted all fallback payloads without rate limiting
historical_insights.solution: Added UrmaFallbackTcpLimiter::TryAcquire call in AppendPublishPayload with pendingBytes tracking and rejection on limit

historical_insights.title: Client unable to reconnect after worker reconnection
historical_insights.problem: Client would fail to re-establish connection when worker IP changed or service restarted
historical_insights.root_cause: Stub recreation did not update exclusive connection socket paths; old stub held stale connection info
historical_insights.solution: Added exclusiveConnSockPath_ update in ReconnectWorker and RecreateOCStub after stub creation

historical_insights.title: Slow RPC requests not flagged for investigation
historical_insights.problem: High-latency RPC calls were silent; no visibility into performance problems
historical_insights.root_cause: Logging only occurred on errors; normal slow requests were invisible
historical_insights.solution: Added PLOG_IF_OR_VLOG macro in LogClientWorkerRpcDone with CLIENT_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US = 2000µs threshold

historical_insights.title: RPC batch paths missing source/destination debug info
historical_insights.problem: Remote batch Get failures were hard to diagnose without knowing which worker/client endpoints involved
historical_insights.root_cause: LogClientWorkerRpcDone logged operation name and count but not network addresses
historical_insights.solution: Enhanced log output to include src/dst address information and elapsed time tracking

historical_insights.title: Get RPC timeout exceeding request timeout
historical_insights.problem: Get operations could timeout longer than configured requestTimeoutMs
historical_insights.root_cause: Timeout comparison logic did not properly clamp derived timeouts
historical_insights.solution: Fixed timeout hierarchy so actual RPC timeout never exceeds explicitly configured timeout

related_files.check: If changing RPC method signatures or error handling in this file, verify object_client_impl.cpp error paths still work; if changing logging thresholds, may affect its own observability

related_files.check: If URMA conditional logic changes, verify FillCreateUrmaInfo #ifdef USE_URMA still compiles both ways; if URMA info structure changes, update FillCreateUrmaInfo

related_files.relation: Worker-side handler for RPC calls; must interpret queue size and element layout from InitDecreaseQueue
related_files.check: If changing queue element size formula in InitDecreaseQueue, must coordinate update to worker queue construction

related_files.relation: Header defines ClientWorkerRemoteApi class interface and member variables (exclusiveId_, exclusiveConnSockPath_, requestTimeoutMs_, clientDeadTimeoutMs_)
related_files.check: If editing timeout fields or exclusive connection state in .h, verify Init/ReconnectWorker/RecreateOCStub all handle the new fields; if adding RETRY_ERROR_CODE, update the set definition

capabilities: Establish and maintain RPC connection to worker service with automatic reconnection
Transparently fall back from URMA to TCP for payload delivery with traffic limiting
Manage shared memory queue for efficient reference counting operations
Execute cache operations (Create, Get, Publish, Exist, Expire, GetMetaInfo) with retry on transient errors
Log RPC performance metrics and diagnose slow/failed requests

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to update exclusiveConnSockPath_ after changing hostPort or recreating stubs
pitfalls.consequence: Exclusive connection mode will use stale socket paths, reconnections silently fail or use wrong sockets
pitfalls.prevention: RecreateOCStub and ReconnectWorker both call stub_->SetExclusiveConnInfo(); any stub recreation must do the same

pitfalls.description: Changing shared memory queue element size in InitDecreaseQueue without updating worker side
pitfalls.consequence: Client and worker will misalign on queue layout; IPC reads/writes corrupt queue data or cause silent failures
pitfalls.prevention: The elementSize formula (futexFlag + shmId + clientId sizes) must remain synchronized with worker_oc_service implementations

pitfalls.description: Removing or changing error codes in RETRY_ERROR_CODE set without understanding retry semantics
pitfalls.consequence: Transient failures no longer retry; operations fail immediately on temporary network/resource issues
pitfalls.prevention: Each code (K_TRY_AGAIN, K_RPC_CANCELLED, K_RPC_DEADLINE_EXCEEDED, K_RPC_UNAVAILABLE, K_OUT_OF_MEMORY) is retryable; removing requires understanding calling patterns

pitfalls.description: Adjusting CLIENT_WORKER_RPC_SLOW_US threshold or removing slow-request logging to reduce noise
pitfalls.consequence: Actual RPC performance regressions become invisible; teams lose visibility into latency degradation
pitfalls.prevention: This constant drives observability for debugging production issues; lower thresholds if noise is high, but don't disable the log

pitfalls.description: Modifying timeout calculation logic in Init/RecreateOCStub without understanding the min() hierarchy
pitfalls.consequence: Connection timeouts become too aggressive or too lenient; reconnection hangs or fails prematurely
pitfalls.prevention: If clientDeadTimeoutMs_ > 0, both methods do std::min(clientDeadTimeoutMs_, static_cast<uint64_t>(requestTimeoutMs_)); this contract must hold

.skills/ds-create-pr/scripts/create_pr.p.description: Creates GitCode pull requests via v5 OpenAPI with flexible token authentication (env vars, file, or explicit) and multi-layer validation. Enforces repository-specific PR body templates (hardcoded for openeuler/yuanrong-datasystem), validates content against sensitive data patterns (credentials, IPs, paths, SSH keys) before submission, and detects merge conflicts via follow-up API query. Core design: token loading prioritizes explicit > env vars > file with clear error at each stage; PR body validation is repository-specific (template required only for REPO_TEMPLATE_TARGET); sensitive content check is non-bypassable and blocks submission if any pattern matches.

data_constants: TOKEN_ENV_NAMES: tuple of env var names checked in load_token—update if GitCode changes var names
REPO_TEMPLATE_TARGET: hardcoded (owner, repo) pair—only this repo enforces template requirements
SENSITIVE_CONTENT_PATTERNS: regex tuple for credentials, IPs, paths, keys—any change risks allowing secrets into PRs
DEFAULT_PR_TEMPLATE_FILE: path derivation depends on REPO_ROOT being three levels up from this file
API field names in build_payload: must match GitCode v5 schema (title, head, base, body, labels, etc.)
edit_checklist.warning: Modifying SENSITIVE_CONTENT_PATTERNS regexes can unintentionally allow credentials, IPs, or keys into PRs if patterns are weakened—test against test_build_payload_rejects_sensitive_* cases.

src/datasystem/master/object_cache/oc_me.description:
OCMetadataManager is the master-side coordinator for distributed object cache metadata, managing creation, mutation, deletion, and location tracking of cached objects across worker nodes. It maintains multiple concurrent hash tables (object metadata, subscriptions, reference tracking) protected by various mutex types, and orchestrates complex state machines (multi-set operations via MULTI_SET_STATE, object deletion phases via isDeletingObjs_, metadata updates via updatingObjsTable_) while handling failover scenarios and slot recovery.

Critical design contracts: ObjectMeta::locations (worker address → AckState) must stay synchronized with worker propagation state and trigger notifyWorkerManager_ on changes; MULTI_SET_STATE gates concurrent multi-object operations to ensure atomicity; heavyOpMutex_ serializes causal sets and sync deletes to prevent corruption; and deletion/update gates (isDeletingObjs_, updatingObjsTable_) block conflicting operations. All L2 cache type checking (HasL2Cache, IsWriteBackL2Cache, IsWriteThroughL2Cache, etc.) routes to different persistence and eviction paths and must be kept in sync with WriteMode protobuf enums.

data_constants: MULTI_SET_STATE enum (IDLE=0, PENDING=1): atomic gate for concurrent multi-object set operations
AckState enum (UNACK=0, ACK=1): location propagation state to workers; UNACK means pending push
ObjectMeta::locations: unordered_map<ImmutableString, AckState>; critical for replica distribution tracking
ObjectMeta L2 cache WriteMode variants: WRITE_THROUGH_L2_CACHE, WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE, WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE_EVICT, NONE_L2_CACHE, NONE_L2_CACHE_EVICT (must match master_object.proto)
TbbMetaTable, TbbSubMetaTable, TbbReqIdTable, TbbRemoteClientIdRefTable: concurrent container types; used with accessor pattern
Heavy op gate: heavyOpMutex_ + heavyOps_ set; one operation per object at a time
edit_checklist.warning: Four different mutex types (mutex, shared_mutex, shared_timed_mutex) protect overlapping state (isDeletingObjMutex_, updatingObjsTableMutex_, subTableMutex_, heavyOpMutex_)—deadlock risk if acquired out of order; establish and document lock ordering globally.

historical_insights.title: Stale primary location after slot recovery caused delete failures
historical_insights.problem: Delete operations failed because stale primary locations from recovered slots were not cleaned up
historical_insights.root_cause: Slot recovery invalidated locations in durable store but not in ObjectMeta::locations in-memory map
historical_insights.solution: Remove stale primary location after slot recovery before allowing subsequent deletes on that object

historical_insights.solution: Add redirect for get object locations; implement ProcessForwardDeleteAllCopyMetaInCurrAz for cross-AZ forwarding

historical_insights.title: Data loss during voluntary scale-down of master nodes
historical_insights.problem: Metadata state (especially delete in-flight markers) was lost when master shut down gracefully
historical_insights.root_cause: isDeletingObjs_ and updatingObjsTable_ tracking state not persisted; recovery lost ongoing delete operations
historical_insights.solution: Persist and recover deletion state; respect isDeletingObjMutex_ gates during shutdown and recovery

historical_insights.title: L2 cache write modes (through/back/back-evict) added and require comprehensive type checking
historical_insights.problem: Multiple L2 cache strategies introduced without complete type discriminator coverage
historical_insights.root_cause: Business requirements evolved; cache modes grew but type checkers did not scale
historical_insights.solution: Add WriteMode enum variants; implement HasL2Cache() and per-mode Is*() checkers in ObjectMeta

pitfalls.description: Modifying ObjectMeta::locations without acquiring isDeletingObjMutex_ and checking isDeletingObjs_ first
pitfalls.consequence: Location update races with delete operation, leading to workers propagating locations for objects being deleted
pitfalls.prevention: Always check isDeletingObjs_.count(key) under isDeletingObjMutex_ before updating locations; skip update if object is marked for deletion

pitfalls.description: Changing ObjectMeta::locations without resetting changed entries to AckState::UNACK
pitfalls.consequence: Workers never learn about location changes if ACK state already marks them as propagated
pitfalls.prevention: After mutating locations map, set affected entries to UNACK; call notifyWorkerManager_->Notify() to queue propagation

pitfalls.description: Allowing new multi-object set operations while ObjectMeta::multiSetState is still PENDING
pitfalls.consequence: Concurrent callers see partial multi-set state, violating atomicity; some workers get some objects, others get different subset
pitfalls.prevention: Check multiSetState == IDLE before accepting new multi-set requests; wait for timeout or completion to reset to IDLE

pitfalls.description: Adding new L2 cache WriteMode without updating both HasL2Cache() and new Is*Mode() checker in ObjectMeta
pitfalls.consequence: Some cache modes silently bypass L2 persistence or eviction logic, data corruption or memory bloat
pitfalls.prevention: Keep HasL2Cache() and all Is*CacheType() checkers in sync; add new variants to both simultaneously with proto enum

pitfalls.description: Acquiring heavyOpMutex_, isDeletingObjMutex_, updatingObjsTableMutex_ in non-deterministic order across call sites
pitfalls.consequence: Potential deadlock when one thread acquires A→B while another acquires B→A
pitfalls.prevention: Document and enforce global lock order: heavyOpMutex_ > isDeletingObjMutex_ > updatingObjsTableMutex_ > subTableMutex_

python/yr/datasystem/object_client.py.description:
This file provides Python bindings for a distributed object cache client library, wrapping C++ implementations via `libds_client_py`. It exports three configuration Enums (WriteMode with five reliability modes, ConsistencyType with PRAM/CAUSAL, CacheType with MEMORY/DISK), a Buffer class managing concurrent access via latching primitives and publish/seal semantics, and an ObjectClient class orchestrating cache operations (init, create, put, get, reference counting). The design enforces type and range validation on all inputs (timeouts 1-INT32_MAX_SIZE), uses status-based error handling (caller must check `.is_error()` before proceeding), and tracks nested object dependencies explicitly via `nested_object_keys` lists in publish/seal operations.
A critical non-obvious contract: Buffer operations require balanced latch pairs (wlatch/unwlatch, rlatch/unrlatch) to prevent deadlocks, status objects must be checked immediately after every C++ client call, and the CacheType.MEMEORY spelling (note the typo) maps to the correct MEMORY constant and must be preserved for backwards compatibility despite the misspelling.

data_constants: WriteMode enum: five modes (NONE_L2_CACHE, WRITE_THROUGH_L2_CACHE, WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE, NONE_L2_CACHE_EVICT, WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE_EVICT) mapped 1:1 to ds.WriteMode
ConsistencyType enum: two modes (PRAM, CAUSAL) mapped to ds.ConsistencyType
CacheType enum: MEMEORY (typo) and DISK; MEMEORY maps to ds.CacheType.MEMORY
Latch timeout defaults: 60 seconds for wlatch() and rlatch()
Parameter range bounds: timeout values validated to 1 ≤ x ≤ INT32_MAX_SIZE; timeout_ms allows 0 ≤ x ≤ INT32_MAX_SIZE
edit_checklist.warning: CacheType.MEMEORY is a persistent typo mapping to MEMORY; renaming it breaks all existing callers and must never be 'fixed'.

key_constructs.name: WriteMode
key_constructs.docstring:
The `WriteMode` class defines the reliability of object.

Currently, the following 'WriteMode' are supported:

===================================  ==================================================================
Definition                            Description
===================================  ==================================================================
`WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE`            Object only store in cache. Default mode.
`WriteMode.WRITE_THROUGH_L2_CACHE`   Object store in cache and synchronized to L2.
`WriteMode.WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE`      Object store in cache and asynchronized to L2.
`WriteMode.NONE_L2_CACHE_EVICT`      Object is volatile, if cache resource is lack it would be delete.
`WriteMode.WRITE_BACK_L2_CACHE_EVICT` Object store in cache and asynchronized to L2, evictable when cache is full.
===================================  ==================================================================
key_constructs.rationale: Enum defining object persistence/reliability (cache-only, sync-to-L2, async-to-L2, evictable variants); required in create() and put(); directly determines latency/durability trade-off

key_constructs.name: wlatch
key_constructs.docstring:
Acquire the write-lock to protect the buffer from concurrent reads and writes.

Args:
    timeout_sec(int): The try-lock `timeout_sec`, the default value is 60 seconds.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raise a type error if `timeout_sec` is invalid.
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if acquire write latch fails.

key_constructs.name: unwlatch
key_constructs.docstring:
Release the write-lock.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if release write latch fails.

key_constructs.name: rlatch
key_constructs.docstring:
Acquire the read-lock to protect the buffer from concurrent writes.

Args:
    timeout_sec(int): The try-lock `timeout_sec`, the default value is 60 seconds.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raise a type error if `timeout_sec` is invalid.
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if acquire read latch fails.

key_constructs.name: unrlatch
key_constructs.docstring:
Release the read-lock.

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Raise a runtime error if release read latch fails.

src/datasystem/common/object_cache/buffe.description:
Buffer manages data storage and lifecycle for the object cache system, supporting three storage modes: shared memory (with inter-process locking), heap-allocated memory (with CommonLock), and remote data on worker nodes (via RDMA/UB). It bridges between application code (via MemoryCopy/Publish/Seal) and the ObjectClientImpl client layer (via weak_ptr reference), handling reference counting, deprecation detection, and visibility flags.

Critical design contracts: (1) Init() must be called before use to determine storage mode and initialize the appropriate lock type; (2) Move assignment must maintain careful ordering (release old → move members → reset source) to avoid double-free of shm buffers; (3) Data layout has metadataSize bytes of overhead at pointer start, then dataSize bytes of user data, with a visibility flag at (pointer + metadataSize - 1); (4) clientImpl_ is weak_ptr to detect client destruction; (5) shm buffers must call DecreaseReferenceCnt exactly once via Release().

data_constants: DEBUG_LOG_LEVEL = 2: controls verbosity of VLOG messages in core paths (MemoryCopy, WLatch, RLatch, UnRLatch, UnRLatch)
Data pointer offset: MutableData/ImmutableData return (pointer + metadataSize), visibility flag at (pointer + metadataSize - sizeof(uint8_t))
Shm detection: isShm_ = true iff pointer is non-null and points to shm (determined in Init), locks differ by storage mode
Seal invariant: isSeal must be false before MemoryCopy/Publish/WLatch; Publish/Seal set it to true
edit_checklist.warning: Move assignment operator=(Buffer&&) has critical ordering: must reset source AFTER moving all members, and must move isReleased_ alongside other state to prevent skipped DecreaseReferenceCnt on reused buffers (see commit 0ae58c00).

historical_insights.title: MCreate(NX) SIGSEGV on null Buffer pointer
historical_insights.problem: MCreate with ExistenceOpt::NX returned null shared_ptr<Buffer> for existing keys. Callers invoked MemoryCopy() without null checks, causing SIGSEGV.
historical_insights.root_cause: No placeholder buffer created for NX-rejected keys; callers assumed non-null Buffer was always safe to use.
historical_insights.solution: Create placeholder Buffers with dataSize=0 and isSeal=true for NX-rejected keys so MemoryCopy safely returns K_INVALID instead of dereferencing null.

historical_insights.title: Buffer move assignment corrupted clientId_ and isReleased_ state
historical_insights.problem: operator=(Buffer&&) assigned clientId_ after other.Reset(), making clientId_ always empty; isReleased_ was never moved, causing reused buffers to skip DecreaseReferenceCnt.
historical_insights.root_cause: Assignment order bug: clientId_ = std::move(other.clientId_) happened after other.Reset() (which sets clientId_ = ""), and isReleased_ was not moved at all.
historical_insights.solution: Reorder assignments to move clientId_ before other.Reset(), and add isReleased_ to the move sequence.

historical_insights.title: MutableData returned nullptr on allocation failure
historical_insights.problem: When ubUrmaDataInfo exists but pointer is null, MutableData calls MallocBufferHelper. If malloc fails, MutableData returns nullptr without error propagation.
historical_insights.root_cause: Error handling added but logs error instead of propagating status; caller gets nullptr without knowing allocation failed.
historical_insights.solution: Added LOG(ERROR) in MutableData to surface allocation failures; callers must check for nullptr.

historical_insights.title: UB (User Buffer) acceleration added for data transfers
historical_insights.problem: Client-to-worker data transfers used only TCP; needed RDMA acceleration via UB.
historical_insights.root_cause: Original Put/Get paths did not leverage high-speed interconnects available on some deployments.
historical_insights.solution: Added ubUrmaDataInfo branch in Init, MemoryCopy (with TCP fallback), and Publish to send data via UB when available. Flag ubDataSentByMemoryCopy tracks if data already transferred.

historical_insights.title: Out-of-memory (OOM) when creating buffer without data
historical_insights.problem: Buffer allocation caused OOM in certain scenarios involving Create without immediate data.
historical_insights.root_cause: Allocation logic or path in Init/MallocBufferHelper not handling zero-size or minimal-footprint cases.
historical_insights.solution: Optimized allocation path to avoid premature malloc for buffers that don't immediately need heap storage.

related_files.check: If adding new ObjectClientImpl calls in Buffer, verify signatures in header. Check GetClientId, GetLockId, memoryCopyThreadPool_, memcpyParallelThreshold_ are available.

related_files.relation: Status return type used throughout Buffer. K_RUNTIME_ERROR, K_INVALID, K_OC_ALREADY_SEALED, K_BUFFER_DEPRECATED error codes defined here.
related_files.check: When adding error returns, verify status code is defined in status.h. Check IsOk(), IsError() methods work as expected.

capabilities: Initialize buffer in shared memory, heap, or remote mode with appropriate lock type
Copy application data into buffer with optional RDMA/UB acceleration and TCP fallback

pitfalls.description: Calling methods after client is destroyed (clientImpl_.lock() returns nullptr).
pitfalls.consequence: All methods return K_RUNTIME_ERROR with message 'Client already destroyed or Shutdown() invoked'. Callers must handle this error code or risk silent failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Check return status from all operations; callers should hold a shared_ptr to the client or ensure client lifetime outlives buffer usage.

pitfalls.description: Arithmetic errors in data pointer calculation: MutableData returns (pointer + metadataSize), but visibility flag is at (pointer + metadataSize - 1). Off-by-one in either direction corrupts data or lock state.
pitfalls.consequence: Visibility flag overwrites start of user data, or data overwrites lock frame. Seal/Publish checks fail or corrupt shared memory.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the exact offset arithmetic in MutableData, GetVisiblePointer, SetVisibility. Test pointer arithmetic against metadataSize values.

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to call Init() after construction via CreateBuffer() or direct constructor before any data operation.
pitfalls.consequence: latch_ is nullptr, causing nullptr dereference in WLatch/RLatch. bufferInfo_->pointer may not be properly initialized.
pitfalls.prevention: CreateBuffer() calls Init() automatically and is the factory method; use it instead of constructing directly. Direct constructors require explicit Init() call.

pitfalls.description: Move assignment operator=(Buffer&&) called with *this == other, or source buffer reused after move without Reset().
pitfalls.consequence: Double-free of malloc'd pointer, double-decrement of reference count on shm buffers, or memory leak.
pitfalls.prevention: Implement self-check (if (this != &other)) as shown in code. Ensure caller does not reuse source buffer after move.

pitfalls.description: Calling WLatch/RLatch after Publish/Seal (which set isSeal=true), or MemoryCopy on sealed buffer.
pitfalls.consequence: WLatch returns K_OC_ALREADY_SEALED; MemoryCopy returns K_INVALID. Operations silently fail if caller does not check status.
pitfalls.prevention: Check seal state before operations. Once Publish/Seal is called, buffer is read-only; create a new buffer for further writes.

pitfalls.description: UB send failure in MemoryCopy or Publish triggers malloc fallback, but ubDataSentByMemoryCopy flag may not be set consistently.
pitfalls.consequence: Data sent twice (once via failed UB, again via TCP) or never reaches server. Worker may see partial/corrupted data.
pitfalls.prevention: In MemoryCopy: ubDataSentByMemoryCopy=true after successful MemoryCopy copy. In Publish: check ubDataSentByMemoryCopy before UB send attempt. Test UB fallback paths.

reading_guide.start: Buffer::Init() — determines all storage mode and lock initialization logic that drives subsequent behavior
key: Constructor + Init: establishes buffer in one of three states (shm, heap, remote); sets isShm_ and latch_ type
MemoryCopy: main data-write path with UB support; sets ubDataSentByMemoryCopy
Publish/Seal: finalization paths that set isSeal and manage visibility
Release: cleanup and reference-count decrement; must be called exactly once per buffer
operator=: move semantics with critical ordering to avoid double-free
CheckDeprecated: validation that detects worker restarts and client destruction
reading_guide.skip: WLatch/RLatch/UnWLatch/UnRLatch locking mechanics (tested separately); Trace and PerfPoint instrumentation (diagnostic only); INJECT_POINT test injection points

src/datasystem/worker/cluster_manager/et.description:
EtcdClusterManager orchestrates cluster membership and request routing for distributed workers, integrating with ETCD to discover and track nodes across local and remote AZs. It maintains node state machines (active/timeout/failed), consistent hash rings for routing, metadata caches with version tracking, and publishes membership changes via event subscribers. The core responsibility is translating ETCD cluster events into a queryable, cached routing topology.

Non-obvious contracts: Node failure is delayed—a timeout state must elapse for an additional (dead_timeout_s - timeout_s) seconds before transitioning to failed, preventing flapping during transient outages. Initial node discovery uses fake ETCD events to bootstrap tables and includes early-exit logic if no progress is detected for 10 seconds, avoiding 60-second blocks on startup. Hash ring caches are version-aware and invalidated atomically. Cross-AZ support maintains separate node tables and hash rings protected by distinct mutexes.

data_constants: FAKE_NODE_EVENT_VALUE: '0;start' sentinel used in SetupInitialClusterNodes; changing breaks node bootstrap
TOTAL_WAIT_NODE_TABLE_TIME_SEC=60: maximum wait duration for node table completion
WAIT_NODE_TABLE_INTERVAL_MS=10: polling granularity during CheckWaitNodeTableComplete
NO_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SEC=10: early-exit threshold when no nodes discovered; critical performance fix from 77b0f3a1
Node state machine: ACTIVE → TIMEOUT → FAILED with DemoteTimedOutNode() using (dead_timeout_s - timeout_s) delay
Hash cache structure: hash2MetaInfo.{first: map<HashPosition, {Range, MetaAddrInfo}>, second: cacheVersion}
edit_checklist.warning: Timing logic for node demotion and table wait early-exit is fragile—changes to flag comparisons or progress detection break restart performance and cluster stability (see commit 77b0f3a1).

historical_insights.title: Worker restart blocked for 60s instead of ~10s after kill -9
historical_insights.problem: CheckWaitNodeTableComplete() waited full TOTAL_WAIT_NODE_TABLE_TIME_SEC even when cluster had stabilized
historical_insights.root_cause: Loop unconditionally waited 60 seconds; no early exit when node discovery made no progress
historical_insights.solution: Added NO_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SEC check to terminate early if no new nodes for 10s; CompleteNodeTableWithFakeNode() handles missing nodes on exit

historical_insights.title: High CPU usage in orphan node monitor thread
historical_insights.problem: StartOrphanNodeMonitorThread caused CPU spikes during operation
historical_insights.root_cause: Orphan monitoring polling loop lacked proper wait/notification primitives
historical_insights.solution: Switched to event-driven pattern using orphanWaitPost_ for thread synchronization

historical_insights.title: RPC latency metrics required mutable MetaPb parameters
historical_insights.problem: ZMQ tracing needed to record metric ticks on both client and server sides; const references blocked this
historical_insights.root_cause: MetaPb parameter was const-qualified in routing methods, preventing tick timestamp recording
historical_insights.solution: Changed GetMetaAddressNotCheckConnection and GetPrimaryReplicaLocationByAddr to accept mutable MetaPb&

manages node lifecycle state transitions with delayed demotion to prevent flapping
routes metadata queries using consistent hashing with version-aware caches

pitfalls.description: Modifying FLAGS_node_timeout_s or FLAGS_node_dead_timeout_s without understanding DemoteTimedOutNode demotion window
pitfalls.consequence: Nodes fail prematurely or linger indefinitely in TIMEOUT state; cluster stability degrades
pitfalls.prevention: Remember both flags measure time since ETCD contact loss (not absolute time); DemoteTimedOutNode checks if (dead_timeout_s - timeout_s) seconds have elapsed in TIMEOUT state before promoting to FAILED

pitfalls.description: Refactoring CheckWaitNodeTableComplete or SetupInitialClusterNodes and removing NO_PROGRESS_TIMEOUT_SEC logic
pitfalls.consequence: Worker restarts block for full 60 seconds, reverting the performance fix from commit 77b0f3a1
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the no-progress early-exit condition; keep CompleteNodeTableWithFakeNode() as fallback to seed missing nodes

pitfalls.description: Clearing hash2MetaInfo cache without updating the cacheVersion in ProcessNotHitCacheWhenRouting
pitfalls.consequence: Hash ring cache becomes stale after version change; routes use wrong metadata addresses
pitfalls.prevention: When cache.clear() is called due to currHashRingVersion mismatch, always increment cacheVersion to currHashRingVersion before repopulating

pitfalls.description: Adding cross-AZ routing without synchronizing otherClusterNodeTable_ and otherAzHashRings updates
pitfalls.consequence: Cross-AZ lookups return inconsistent or missing metadata; QueryMasterAddrInOtherAz and CheckIfOtherAzNodeConnected fail silently
pitfalls.prevention: Lock otherClusterNodeMutex_ consistently; ensure ConstructOtherAzHashRings() builds rings for all AZs in FLAGS_other_cluster_names

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description:
WorkerOcEvictionManager orchestrates object cache eviction on worker nodes, managing memory and spill eviction through multiple specialized thread pools (memEvictTaskThreadPool_, spillEvictTaskThreadPool_, primaryEndLifeThreadPool_, etc.) and an EvictionList. It handles five eviction actions (DELETE, FREE_MEMORY, SPILL, END_LIFE, MIGRATE) with watermark-based triggering, async spill task tracking via futures, and coordination with master metadata deletion via RemoveMetaFromMasterForEviction().

Design contracts: (1) Multiple thread pools must reset in destructor order to avoid dangling references; (2) Watermarks are now gflags-configurable (commit 5acbcb42), so IsAboveLowWaterMark() and GetLowWaterMark() logic depends on runtime flags, not constants; (3) Cache type variants (MEMORY vs L2) require distinct eviction paths (DeleteL2CacheEvictableObject vs DeleteNoneL2CacheEvictableObject); (4) Spill operations are async futures that must be tracked in spillTasks map; (5) Primary end-life objects use separate queue and drain mechanism to handle metadata-deleted retry scenarios.

data_constants: Action enum values (UNKNOWN, DELETE, FREE_MEMORY, SPILL, END_LIFE, RETAIN, MIGRATE): used in GetActionName() and action-based control flow
TaskType enum (SINGLE, BATCH): determines spill task batching strategy
Thread pool members (memEvictTaskThreadPool_, primaryEndLifeThreadPool_, spillEvictTaskThreadPool_, spillTaskThreadPool_, masterTaskThreadPool_): destruction order in destructor is critical
EvictionTrace and ActionSummary structures: logging and aggregation contracts for debug output
CacheType parameter semantics: MEMORY vs L2 determines which DeleteXxxEvictableObject() path to call
edit_checklist.warning: Watermarks are now runtime-configurable gflags (commit 5acbcb42); code assuming static watermarks or calling GetLowWaterMark() without considering flag values will break under dynamic configuration.

historical_insights.title: Watermark thresholds exposed as gflags
historical_insights.problem: Memory and spill high/low watermarks were hardcoded, blocking operational tuning
historical_insights.root_cause: Missing feature flag infrastructure for eviction tuning
historical_insights.solution: Exposed watermarks as gflags with startup validation, cached runtime factors, and Helm/worker_config wiring (commit 5acbcb42)

historical_insights.title: Async END_LIFE delete reverted due to RPC timeout blocking
historical_insights.problem: Async delete for eviction END_LIFE caused RPC timeout blocking; had to revert after one commit (2cf4ff31 reverted by 3697b5b0)
historical_insights.root_cause: Async operations in END_LIFE path did not handle timeout or blocking scenarios correctly
historical_insights.solution: Reverted to synchronous approach for END_LIFE metadata deletion to avoid cascading timeouts

historical_insights.title: Batch eviction remove-meta requests
historical_insights.problem: Individual metadata removal requests were inefficient at scale
historical_insights.root_cause: One RPC per object deletion instead of batching
historical_insights.solution: Implemented batching in RemoveMetaFromMasterForEviction() using EvictDeletedObjects map (commit 3e3d2fea)

historical_insights.title: Write-back cache eviction support added
historical_insights.problem: Eviction logic only handled read-only cache types, missing write-back scenarios
historical_insights.root_cause: Cache type variations not accounted for in original design
historical_insights.solution: Added DeleteNoneL2CacheEvictableObject() and DeleteL2CacheEvictableObject() methods with L2 cache fallback (commits c9ced469, 1ba0c238)

pitfalls.description: Thread pool reset order in destructor: multiple pools (memEvictTaskThreadPool_, spillEvictTaskThreadPool_, etc.) are reset sequentially, but if one reset releases a variable another pool references, dangling pointer occurs
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free crash during shutdown; thread still running accesses freed member
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain current destructor reset order and document pool interdependencies; verify no cross-pool task dependencies

pitfalls.description: Assuming static watermarks: code calling IsAboveLowWaterMark() or GetLowWaterMark() without accounting for gflags runtime reconfiguration (post commit 5acbcb42)
pitfalls.consequence: Eviction triggering threshold becomes stale; memory pressure not relieved if flag changed at runtime
pitfalls.prevention: Always call GetLowWaterMark() fresh at decision points; never cache watermark values across invocations

pitfalls.description: Mixing cache type eviction paths: calling DeleteNoneL2CacheEvictableObject() on an L2-cached object or vice versa (due to write-back cache support added in c9ced469)
pitfalls.consequence: Object not evicted or wrong metadata path taken; memory leak or orphaned metadata in master
pitfalls.prevention: Check IsObjectExistInL2Cache() and cacheType parameter before selecting DeleteNoneL2CacheEvictableObject() vs DeleteL2CacheEvictableObject()

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to await spill task futures: SpillTask.future added to spillTasks map but result never checked
pitfalls.consequence: Spill operation errors silently ignored; dangling async work pollutes spillTasks; memory not actually freed
pitfalls.prevention: Always retrieve SpillResult from future before returning from EvictionTask(); validate status and handle failedKeys

pitfalls.description: Lock ordering deadlock between cvMutex_ and primaryEndLifeMutex_: if acquired in inconsistent order across call paths
pitfalls.consequence: Deadlock during eviction task or primary end-life drain, system hangs
pitfalls.prevention: Document and enforce lock acquisition order (e.g., always cvMutex_ then primaryEndLifeMutex_); audit callers for consistency

cli/benchmark/task.py.description: This file implements the task execution framework for dsbench benchmarking, providing abstract and concrete task classes for running single or parallel benchmark commands locally or remotely via SSH. The core design uses a handler pattern for lifecycle callbacks (before/after execution hooks) and separates local versus remote execution paths, with environment variables concatenated as shell arguments for remote execution to work around subprocess shell=False restrictions. Critical contracts: subprocess always uses shell=False with shlex.split() for safe argument parsing, timeouts are hard-coded to 300 seconds, and BenchParallelCommandTask uses ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers sized to task count.

data_constants: timeout=300: hard-coded in both execute_command_locally (line 89) and execute_command_remotely (line 157)
concat_args prefix: passed as empty string '' in execute_command_remotely line 148
max_workers=len(self.tasks): thread pool sizing policy in BenchParallelCommandTask line 208
subprocess.Popen: called with shlex.split() and implicit shell=False in both local and remote paths
edit_checklist.warning: Error message on line 158 claims 'timed out after 600 seconds' but actual timeout is 300s (line 157)—this discrepancy will mislead users and must be fixed together if timeout changes.

historical_insights.title: subprocess shell injection prevention
historical_insights.problem: Subprocess execution was vulnerable to command injection via shell=True or unsafe argument handling
historical_insights.root_cause: Early implementation may have allowed shell interpretation of user input
historical_insights.solution: Enforce shell=False (implicit default) and use shlex.split() for all command parsing; use shlex.quote() on SSH paths/commands

historical_insights.title: parallel task execution support
historical_insights.problem: Benchmark suite needed to run multiple commands concurrently for set/get operations
historical_insights.root_cause: Single-task execution model was insufficient for realistic performance testing
historical_insights.solution: Added BenchParallelCommandTask using ThreadPoolExecutor with dynamic worker pool sized to task count; enforced handler callbacks around concurrent futures

.skills/ds-refresh-docs/scripts/refresh_.description:
This script orchestrates a multi-step workflow to refresh online Chinese documentation on an upstream repository. It prepares detached git worktrees for source (docs generation) and target (docs publishing), builds HTML documentation via `make html`, syncs the built output using `rsync -a --delete` to a release branch, commits the changes, and creates a PR to the upstream repository via a separate PR creation script.

The design carefully separates read-only source worktrees (for building docs from upstream master) from read-write target worktrees (for committing synced docs to a release branch), validates that the push remote is never the upstream itself (forcing PRs through forks), and uses subprocess primitives (`run`, `capture`) extensively to manage git operations, worktree lifecycle, and rsync synchronization. The PR body is pre-generated in Chinese with specific template sections that are validated downstream.

data_constants: ALLOWED_UPSTREAM_URLS: set of 2 valid upstream git URLs - must stay in sync with actual upstream configurations
UPSTREAM_OWNER and UPSTREAM_REPO: 'openeuler' and 'yuanrong-datasystem' - the hardcoded target for PR creation
CREATE_PR_SCRIPT path: parents[2] / 'ds-create-pr' / 'scripts' / 'create_pr.py' - depends on exact relative directory structure
rsync flags in run call: -a --delete - both flags are critical for proper synchronization and cleanup
edit_checklist.warning: The CREATE_PR_SCRIPT path uses fragile relative directory navigation (parents[2]) that will break if the skill directory structure changes.

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/servi.description:
Implements multi-object publish operations for a distributed object cache worker service, supporting both transactional (TX) and non-transactional (NTX) batch semantics. Handles authentication, duplicate detection, ordered batch locking (deadlock prevention), verification, and detailed rollback logic that distinguishes between newly-inserted vs. existing objects. Recent optimization (commit 4fbd1b94) changed from unconditional logging to dynamic VLOG levels based on latency thresholds.

Design contracts: (1) Batch locking uses ordered iteration via objectToIdx map to prevent deadlocks—any future locking changes must preserve this ordering. (2) NX semantics were changed (commit 051fddbf) to return success when key exists; callers rely on this non-obvious behavior. (3) Distributed master mode forbids certain combinations (NX+TX, NONE+NTX); these restrictions are checked early in MultiPublishImpl. (4) Rollback behavior differs by insertion state: newly-inserted objects are erased; existing objects call FreeResources and SetLifeState(OBJECT_INVALID).

data_constants: RETRY_INTERNAL_MS_META_MOVING = 200: retry delay for meta-moving scenarios
THREAD_WAIT_TIME_MS = 10: thread polling interval
MSET_TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_MS = 50'000: transaction timeout in milliseconds
vlogLevel dynamic threshold: 1ms latency marks slow path (VLOG(0) vs VLOG(1))
edit_checklist.warning: Deadlock risk from batch locking—must preserve objectToIdx map iteration order; NX semantics changed in 051fddbf (returns success if key exists)—code relies on this non-obvious behavior

historical_insights.title: Batch lock deadlock from out-of-order locking
historical_insights.problem: Deadlocks occurred when concurrent batch operations locked objects in different orders
historical_insights.root_cause: Without deterministic locking order, circular wait conditions formed across worker threads
historical_insights.solution: Fixed by using std::map<objectKey, index> to impose total ordering on all lock acquisitions (commits ca305bb4, 3eda2a1d optimized the strategy)

historical_insights.title: NX mode semantics change for distributed master compatibility
historical_insights.problem: Initial NX (create-if-not-exists) behavior conflicted with distributed master expectations
historical_insights.root_cause: NX originally returned error when key existed; distributed master required success response
historical_insights.solution: Changed NX to return success if key already exists (051fddbf); multi-pub code checks existence, adds to localExistKeys without error

historical_insights.title: Request path log volume optimization
historical_insights.problem: Unconditional LOG(INFO) for every publish generated excessive log volume in high-throughput paths
historical_insights.root_cause: Batch operations with many keys logged at INFO level regardless of latency impact
historical_insights.solution: Converted to VLOG with dynamic level: VLOG(0) for slow (>1ms) or error cases, VLOG(1) for fast paths

related_files.relation: Client caller: sends MultiPublishReqPb, interprets lastRc and failedKeys in response
related_files.check: Any change to NTX failure semantics or response fields must be coordinated with client expectations

related_files.relation: Master metadata: multi-publish delegates via MultiPublishObject call
related_files.check: Co-changed 6 times; coordinate TX/NTX branching or failure handling changes with master side

related_files.check: If single-publish verification or rollback semantics change, apply to multi-publish variants too

related_files.relation: Header: class definition and method signatures
related_files.check: Co-changed 6 times; verify method signatures match when refactoring TX/NTX paths

entities: SafeObjType: thread-safe object wrapper with read/write lock
objectTable_: hash table of cached objects indexed by key
ExistenceOptPb: enum for existence modes (NX, NONE, etc.)
capabilities: Batch-publish multiple objects with atomic semantics (TX) or per-key delivery (NTX)
Detect and reject duplicate keys within a single batch request
Enforce distributed master mode constraints (forbid NX+TX and NONE+NTX combinations)
Acquire ordered locks to prevent deadlocks across concurrent batch operations
Roll back partial failures with per-object resource cleanup based on insertion state

pitfalls.description: Modifying locking order in BatchLockForSet or BatchLockForSetNtx (e.g., changing objectToIdx iteration)
pitfalls.consequence: Reintroduces deadlocks when two concurrent batch-ops lock the same keys in different orders
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve map-based deterministic iteration; any locking refactor must maintain total order across all threads

pitfalls.description: Assuming NX mode returns error when key already exists (pre-051fddbf behavior)
pitfalls.consequence: Batch operations silently skip conflicting keys; caller logic relying on errors for existence checks fails
pitfalls.prevention: Check localExistKeys set in NTX path; verify distributed master mode contract assumptions

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to update both BatchRollBackEntries overloads when adding rollback logic
pitfalls.consequence: Some failure paths (NTX with failedKeys) skip cleanup, leaking resources or leaving objects in invalid state
pitfalls.prevention: Keep both overloads synchronized; verify each is called in the right scenario (simple vs. with failedKeys set)

pitfalls.description: Removing vlogLevel dynamic branching or changing the 1ms threshold
pitfalls.consequence: Regressions in log volume (excessive INFO spam) or loss of slow-path visibility in production
pitfalls.prevention: Document the 1ms latency threshold; preserve dynamic VLOG pattern for observability

reading_guide.start: MultiPublishImpl: the dispatcher after public MultiPublish wrapper; shows overall flow and distributed master constraints
key: MultiPublishTx vs MultiPublishNtx: why TX is all-or-nothing and NTX records partial failures to failedKeys
BatchLockForSet with objectToIdx map: deadlock prevention contract—order is enforced globally
VerifyObjectsAndRollBackIfNeedTx vs VerifyObjectsNtx: TX rolls back on first error, NTX collects all errors
BatchRollBackEntriesImpl: two cleanup paths (Erase vs FreeResources) depend on ifInserts flag

src/datasystem/common/rdma/fast_transpor.description:
Header file declaring wrapper functions for Fast Transport Manager, which abstracts URMA (Universal RDMA) and UCP (UCX Protocol) implementations for high-performance network data transfer. Provides a unified API for RDMA operations (read, write, gather) with support for both blocking and non-blocking modes, connection initialization, and resource cleanup.

The wrapper conditionally compiles against either URMA or UCP backends (via USE_URMA/USE_RDMA flags). A critical design decision from commit 0051f231 is that InitializeFastTransportManager() with an empty HostPort parameter should initialize only local resources without binding to a specific address—this distinction is essential for client-side early warmup vs. server-side binding behavior.

data_constants: Function signatures and parameter order—consumers depend on exact signatures across .cpp and manager implementations
Conditional directives #ifdef USE_URMA and #ifdef USE_RDMA—code must build with either backend disabled
Empty HostPort() default in InitializeFastTransportManager—controls local-only vs. address-bound initialization
edit_checklist.warning: This header's changes cascade to fast_transport_manager_wrapper.cpp and both backend managers; signature changes break 10 co-changing files.

historical_insights.title: Confusing semantics of InitializeFastTransportManager hostport parameter
historical_insights.problem: Parameter behavior was not clear—whether it applied to client vs. server, or what an empty HostPort meant
historical_insights.root_cause: Design did not distinguish between initialization for early warmup (client-side, local only) vs. full server setup (with address binding)
historical_insights.solution: Refactored in commit 0051f231 to clarify that empty HostPort initializes only local URMA resources without binding

historical_insights.title: TCP fallback not applied uniformly across transport modes
historical_insights.problem: Rate limiting was missing when client-worker connection lacked UB (Universal Buffer) support
historical_insights.root_cause: Fallback logic did not account for all connection paths after UB feature additions
historical_insights.solution: Ensured TCP rate limiting applies in all fallback scenarios per commit 783db47e; modified defaults and fallback routing in feddf7de

historical_insights.title: Client-side URMA resource cleanup on disconnect was incomplete
historical_insights.problem: RemoteRemoteFastTransportClient cleanup was added but required proper lifecycle handling
historical_insights.root_cause: As UB data transfer was rolled out, client resources needed explicit cleanup to avoid leaks
historical_insights.solution: Added RemoveRemoteFastTransportClient() to clean up URMA resources per clientId (commit 816d3c14)

related_files.relation: 10 shared commits; implements all function declarations in this header
related_files.check: Signature changes here must match .cpp implementations exactly; verify blocking/non-blocking semantics match parameter handling

related_files.check: Verify eventKey types and EventWaiter contract; NUMA affinity parameters (srcChipId, dstChipId)

related_files.relation: 4 shared commits; consumer of removal and write operations; uses RemoveRemoteFastTransportNode
related_files.check: If changing node removal or handshake logic, verify worker lifecycle is not broken

related_files.check: Transport mode changes must not break client initialization order or fallback routing

related_files.check: Ensure protobuf message types (UcpRemoteInfoPb) and blocking semantics are consistent

capabilities: Initialize and configure transport backends (URMA or UCP) with optional address binding
Execute async RDMA write/read and gather operations with blocking or event-based completion
Manage connection handshakes and stability checks between client/worker peers
Clean up remote nodes and client resources upon disconnection
Extract segment info from shared memory units for RDMA address resolution

pitfalls.description: Confusing the two removal operations: RemoveRemoteFastTransportNode (takes HostPort) vs. RemoveRemoteFastTransportClient (takes ClientKey)
pitfalls.consequence: Wrong cleanup path used, leading to resource leaks or failed disconnections
pitfalls.prevention: Verify caller intent: RemoveRemoteFastTransportNode for worker teardown, RemoveRemoteFastTransportClient for client cleanup

pitfalls.description: Assuming blocking=true is always safe; callers must handle eventKeys when blocking=false
pitfalls.consequence: Non-blocking operations fire without waiting, and caller loses track of request IDs, causing synchronization bugs
pitfalls.prevention: Check UrmaWritePayload, UrmaGatherWrite, UcpPutPayload, UcpGatherPut contracts: blocking=false requires caller to track eventKeys via optional EventWaiter

pitfalls.description: Mixing srcChipId/dstChipId parameters (UrmaWritePayload) with other operations that lack them
pitfalls.consequence: NUMA affinity lost or misapplied across heterogeneous transfers
pitfalls.prevention: UrmaWritePayload is the only function with chip IDs; verify usage is URMA-side operations only (commit 5555b1d0 added this)

pitfalls.description: Forgetting that InitializeFastTransportManager with empty HostPort does NOT bind to any address
pitfalls.consequence: Client-side early warmup skips address binding (correct), but server-side setup fails to expose resources to remote peers
pitfalls.prevention: Pass explicit HostPort for server initialization; empty parameter reserved for client-side pre-warmup only

reading_guide.start: InitializeFastTransportManager — sets up the entire transport system; read its comment carefully for hostport semantics
key: InitializeFastTransportManager: controls whether transport is local-only (client warmup) or bound to an address (server)
UrmaWritePayload/UcpPutPayload pair: split between URMA and UCP backends; understand blocking vs. eventKeys trade-off
RemoveRemoteFastTransportNode vs. RemoveRemoteFastTransportClient: distinct cleanup paths for different resource types
SetClientFastTransportMode: configures transport size and mode, affects fallback routing
reading_guide.skip: Proto message type definitions (UrmaRemoteAddrPb, etc.) are not declared here; referenced from meta_transport.pb.h. ConstructHandshakePb and FinalizeOutboundConnection are lower-level handshake helpers; can defer unless working on connection setup.

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description: Implements memory eviction management for an object cache system, handling selection and removal of objects when memory thresholds are exceeded. Core responsibilities: (1) track evictable objects via EvictionList, (2) orchestrate multi-threaded eviction strategies (memory-free, spill, migration, delete, end-of-life), (3) batch metadata removal requests to master to avoid RPC storms, (4) aggregate and gate eviction logs to prevent spam, (5) monitor and enforce memory watermarks across service types. Non-obvious contracts: batching delays meta removal up to 10ms while memory remains accounted (BATCH_DELETE_META_MAX_DELAY_MS), version checks in PreventFreeLeak must match object CreateTime exactly or eviction silently fails, IsObjectEvictable requires both IsBinary status *and* L2 cache presence checks (not either/or), memory threshold is computed once at startup from flags and cached in static variable despite calling GetMaxMemorySize repeatedly.

data_constants: BATCH_SPILL_THRESHOLD=512: batching threshold for spill operations
BATCH_DELETE_META_THRESHOLD=300: batching threshold for metadata removal to master
BATCH_DELETE_META_MAX_DELAY_MS=10: max delay before forced flush of batched meta deletes
PRIMARY_END_LIFE_BATCH_MAX_DELAY_MS=10: lifecycle end-of-life batching delay
EVICTION_TRACE_SUMMARY_THRESHOLD=32: eviction event count before trace log output
EVICTION_TRACE_SUMMARY_INTERVAL_MS=60000: max interval between trace summary logs
MASTER_TASK_THREAD_NUM: 4 for tests, 8 for production (conditional compilation)
thread pool names matching their roles: MemEvictionThread, SpillEvictionThread, etc.
memEvictionList_: core data structure, must stay consistent with Add/Erase operations
edit_checklist.warning: Batching delays metadata removal from master while memory is still counted as used; version mismatch in PreventFreeLeak silently prevents eviction without error; async delete was reverted (3697b5b0), do not re-enable without understanding the timeout blocking issue from commit 2cf4ff31.

historical_insights.title: Log spam from eviction traces
historical_insights.problem: High-frequency eviction operations generated excessive log output that impacted performance and observability
historical_insights.root_cause: Every eviction action was logged individually without aggregation or sampling
historical_insights.solution: Implemented EvictionTraceAggregator with batching (EVICTION_TRACE_SUMMARY_THRESHOLD=32 events or 60s interval) and gated output with LOG_EVERY_T macro

historical_insights.title: Inefficient metadata removal requests to master
historical_insights.problem: Removing metadata for evicted objects required individual RPC calls per object, saturating RPC queues
historical_insights.root_cause: RemoveMetaFromMasterForEviction processed objects one-by-one without grouping
historical_insights.solution: Added batch removal: collect up to BATCH_DELETE_META_THRESHOLD=300 objects, flush after max delay of 10ms

historical_insights.title: Async delete blocking on RPC timeouts
historical_insights.problem: Attempt to use async delete for PRIMARY_END_LIFE operations to avoid blocking caused unspecified issues and was reverted
historical_insights.root_cause: Synchronous RPC calls during end-of-life delete could timeout and block the entire eviction thread
historical_insights.solution: Reverted async delete approach (commit 3697b5b0) after 2cf4ff31 experiment; likely due to consistency or ordering issues

historical_insights.title: Hardcoded memory watermark thresholds
historical_insights.problem: Eviction thresholds were compile-time constants with no runtime configuration option
historical_insights.root_cause: Flags infrastructure not exposed for eviction parameters
historical_insights.solution: Exposed eviction and spill watermarks via gflags (FLAGS_eviction_reserve_mem_threshold_mb, FLAGS_spill_thread_num), added startup validation, cached factors as static variables

historical_insights.title: Write-through-only mode assumption
historical_insights.problem: Eviction logic only handled write-through cache mode; write-back mode objects were not evictable
historical_insights.root_cause: IsObjectExistInL2Cache only checked IsWriteThroughMode()
historical_insights.solution: Extended check to also accept write-back mode if stateInfo.IsWriteBackDone() is true

related_files.relation: Header defines all public interfaces, class members, and thread pool declarations; co-changes in 10 shared commits indicate tight coupling
related_files.check: Verify thread pool member names match their initialization in Init(); verify EvictionTrace and ActionSummary struct definitions match usage in aggregator

related_files.relation: Master-side handling of metadata removal requests batched by RemoveMetaFromMasterForEviction; 6 shared commits reflect eviction-metadata coordination
related_files.check: If changing batch size or retry logic, verify master can handle the new batch sizes without timeouts or database overload

related_files.relation: Unit tests for eviction and spill operations; 4 shared commits suggest test coverage evolves with implementation changes
related_files.check: When modifying batching thresholds or version checks, ensure tests still validate correctness under timing races (e.g., version change during batch delay)

related_files.relation: Service layer that triggers eviction via EvictWhenMemoryExceedThrehold; 4 shared commits indicate integration points for cache operations
related_files.check: Ensure calls to Evict() pass correct ServiceType and CacheType; verify memory accounting is consistent with eviction batching delays

capabilities: Selects and evicts objects from memory based on high-watermark thresholds
Batches metadata removal requests to master to optimize RPC efficiency
Aggregates eviction traces and gates log output to reduce spam
Manages object end-of-life and deletion with configurable retry/timeout logic
Monitors and enforces memory watermarks across OBJECT and STREAM service types

pitfalls.description: Forgetting to check *both* IsBinary status and L2 cache presence in IsObjectEvictable
pitfalls.consequence: Unevictable or improperly-cached objects get selected for eviction, causing data loss or corruption
pitfalls.prevention: Remember: non-binary objects must have L2 cache (HasL2Cache), binary objects must have shared memory unit (GetShmUnit() != null); both checks are required

pitfalls.description: Version mismatch in PreventFreeLeak: comparing GetCreateTime() when object was already modified after initial eviction selection
pitfalls.consequence: Eviction is silently skipped, memory is never freed, causing out-of-memory conditions without error
pitfalls.prevention: Version must be captured at selection time and checked atomically just before deletion; if changed, abort the eviction for that object

pitfalls.description: Static memThresInitVal is computed once from initial maxAvailableMemorySize but used repeatedly as global threshold
pitfalls.consequence: If memory limits change at runtime, threshold becomes stale and doesn't reflect new constraints
pitfalls.prevention: If runtime memory reallocation is possible, either recompute threshold or avoid caching it globally

pitfalls.description: Batching delays (BATCH_DELETE_META_THRESHOLD=300 with 10ms max delay) mean metadata removal lags actual memory release
pitfalls.consequence: Memory freed locally is still counted as occupied at master, blocking subsequent allocations; lag up to 10ms under low eviction load
pitfalls.prevention: Understand that freed memory visibility to allocator is delayed; do not assume immediate consistency between local eviction and master metadata state

pitfalls.description: Multiple static variables for memory thresholds (one per ServiceType::OBJECT and ServiceType::STREAM path) could cause race conditions during Init
pitfalls.consequence: Threshold calculations might use stale values if Init is called from multiple threads or if memory changes between static initializations
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure Init is called once and completed before any eviction threads access the static threshold variables

reading_guide.start: EvictWhenMemoryExceedThrehold: entry point for memory-driven eviction; understand threshold calculation, ServiceType dispatch, and call to manager->Evict()
key: RemoveMetaFromMasterForEviction: demonstrates batching pattern and 10ms delay contract
IsObjectEvictable: critical gatekeeper logic requiring both L2-cache *and* shared-memory checks
EvictionTraceAggregator: shows log aggregation pattern and how trace events are batched before output
memEvictionList_.Add: shows Q1/Q2 classification based on ref count (key to understanding eviction order)

src/datasystem/common/rpc/zmq/zmq_stub_c.description:
This file implements ZMQ-based RPC stub connection management, handling bidirectional message routing between frontend (client-facing) async loops and backend services via either ZMQ dealer sockets or direct Unix Domain Socket/TCP connections. ZmqFrontend manages the primary ZMQ socket, async worker thread, and message queue; SockConnEntry manages individual socket connections with encoder/decoder pairs and a socket pool for load distribution.

The critical non-obvious design contract is dynamic protocol selection: BackendToFrontend routes based on method_index and connection state—certain methods (ZMQ_PAYLOAD_HANDSHAKE_METHOD, ZMQ_SOCKPATH_METHOD, etc.) must use ZmqSocket, while others use direct sockets if available. V1MTP is used for backward compatibility; V2MTP for payload operations. The file switched from global write locks to per-entry mutexes (aa27153f) to parallelize connections to different nodes, but RouteToUnixSocket still acquires a read lock on a single connInfo to prevent races with SetSvcFdInvalid. Socket pool load balancing uses atomic fetch_add without locks, and message frames pass through injection points for test corruption.

data_constants: K_ONE_SECOND = 1000: heartbeatInterval_ initialization, timeout base unit
K_HALF_A_SECOND = 500: poll timeout tuning
K_LIVENESS: initial liveness counter value for heartbeat detection
RPC_HWM: high water mark for msgQue_ queue capacity
RPC_POLL_TIME: poll timeout constant in BackendToFrontend and WaitForConnected
ZMQ_SHORT_UUID: substring length for heartBeatID_ extraction
ZMQ_NO_FILE_FD: sentinel value for invalid file descriptors (efd_, ffd_, fd_)
ZMQ_PAYLOAD_PUT_METHOD, ZMQ_PAYLOAD_HANDSHAKE_METHOD, ZMQ_SOCKPATH_METHOD, ZMQ_TCP_DIRECT_METHOD, ZMQ_PAYLOAD_GET_METHOD, ZMQ_STREAM_WORKER_METHOD: method indices for routing decisions
edit_checklist.warning: Changing method_index() checks in BackendToFrontend or RouteToZmqSocket will break routing and client-server protocol negotiation; verify V1/V2 protocol compatibility and test with both UDS and TCP transports before modifying.

historical_insights.title: Global write lock serialized concurrent connections to different nodes
historical_insights.problem: Original design held global connMux_ write lock during entire connection setup, forcing sequential connection to different nodes
historical_insights.root_cause: connMap_ used weak_ptr<ZmqBaseStubConn> with single global lock guarding both map operations and connection initialization
historical_insights.solution: Replaced connMap_ value type to shared_ptr<ZmqConnEntry> where each entry owns its own mutex; GetOrCreateConnEntry acquires global lock only for map lookup/insert, then per-entry lock for connection setup, enabling concurrent connects to different nodes while still serializing same-node access

historical_insights.title: RPC latency instrumentation added for end-to-end observability
historical_insights.problem: No visibility into where time is spent in RPC flow (queueing, network, execution)
historical_insights.root_cause: No latency measurement points; MetaPb was const reference so ticks could not be recorded
historical_insights.solution: Added CLIENT_STUB_SEND tick; changed MetaPb from const reference to mutable; added RecordTick and GetLapTime helpers; added PerfPoint::RecordElapsed for network and queueing latencies with stages CLIENT_ENQUEUE, CLIENT_TO_STUB, CLIENT_SEND, CLIENT_RECV, SERVER_RECV, SERVER_DEQUEUE, SERVER_EXEC_END, SERVER_SEND

historical_insights.title: Client ZMQ IO thread count was hardcoded despite server configurability
historical_insights.problem: Server supported zmq_server_io_context config; client always used default 1 IO thread ignoring environment configuration
historical_insights.root_cause: No zmq_client_io_thread flag or DATASYSTEM_ZMQ_CLIENT_IO_THREAD env var check when creating client ZmqContext
historical_insights.solution: Added zmq_client_io_thread flag (default 1, range [1,32], read from env var) passed to client ZmqContext for symmetric configurability

historical_insights.title: ZMQ heartbeat and worker connect timeouts were too aggressive
historical_insights.problem: Spurious connection failures under load due to hardcoded timeout constants not accounting for latency variability
historical_insights.root_cause: Fixed timeout values did not reflect real-world worker UB connect and ZMQ heartbeat latencies
historical_insights.solution: Modified timeout values in worker UB connect and ZMQ heartbeat logic

related_files.check: Before adding new PerfPoint::RecordElapsed call, ensure corresponding PerfKey is defined and latency metric exists in kv_metrics

related_files.relation: Defines ZMQ_*_METHOD enums, EventType (V1MTP/V2MTP), RPC_HWM, RPC_POLL_TIME used throughout routing logic
related_files.check: When adding new method type or protocol variant, update zmq_common.h first; verify all method_index checks in BackendToFrontend and RouteToUnixSocket are consistent

related_files.relation: Defines ZmqStubConnMgr and likely connMap_ type; per-entry mutex change (aa27153f) affects this header
related_files.check: If editing connection management or concurrency logic, verify connMap_ type and GetOrCreateConnEntry signature match; check that connInProgress_ and connectRc_ are properly initialized

related_files.relation: Configuration flags like zmq_client_io_thread and RPC timeouts declared here
related_files.check: When adding socket or timeout configuration, declare flag in common_gflags.h and read it in ZmqFrontend or SockConnEntry init methods

capabilities: Route RPC requests dynamically to ZMQ dealer or direct socket based on method type and connection state
Manage async message flow between frontend and backend with lock-free queue processing
Maintain socket connection pools with encoder/decoder pairs for efficient local IPC/TCP communication
Detect dead connections via heartbeat and signal reconnection to backend
Record per-stage latency ticks (enqueue, send, network, receive, execute) for end-to-end RPC observability

pitfalls.description: Modifying method_index checks in BackendToFrontend without updating corresponding checks in RouteToUnixSocket
pitfalls.consequence: Requests routed to wrong protocol layer; V1MTP/V2MTP mismatch causes frame decode failures
pitfalls.prevention: Grep for ZMQ_*_METHOD and verify routing decisions are symmetric; test both UDS and TCP paths with each method type

pitfalls.description: Changing liveness counter logic (increment on heartbeat, compare in ResetLiveness) without tracing full heartbeat flow
pitfalls.consequence: Connections prematurely reaped (if threshold lowered) or zombie connections accumulate (if not properly decremented), causing cascading failures
pitfalls.prevention: Trace ZmqSocketToBackend's receiver == heartBeatID_ comparison and ResetLiveness calls; preserve increment/threshold semantics

pitfalls.description: Removing or renaming injection point 'ZmqFrontend.CorruptMetaPb' without updating test code
pitfalls.consequence: Corruption-handling tests silently pass without actually corrupting metadata, missing real bugs
pitfalls.prevention: Search test files for 'CorruptMetaPb' before removal; injection points are typically only used in tests

pitfalls.description: Replacing atomic idx_.fetch_add in GetNextFd with a locking mechanism
pitfalls.consequence: Hot path contention on every message; lock-free load balancing becomes serialized bottleneck
pitfalls.prevention: Keep idx_ atomic and pools_ const after construction; GetNextFd must remain lock-free

pitfalls.description: Resizing or modifying pools_ vector while concurrent GetNextFd calls are indexing into it
pitfalls.consequence: Out-of-bounds access or use-after-free if idx_ % poolSz returns invalid index

pitfalls.consequence: Frames sent but not received, or received but not decoded, breaking request/reply semantics
pitfalls.prevention: Verify encoder and decoder implementations handle new EventType (V1MTP/V2MTP) before adding frame types

src/datasystem/worker/object_cache/worke.description: This header defines WorkerWorkerOCServiceImpl, an RPC service implementing worker-to-worker communication for distributed object retrieval and data migration. It handles single and batch remote object fetches using high-performance URMA/RH2D transport with TCP fallback, plus cluster scaling operations via data migration. The design uses three paired method styles: public RPC entry points (e.g., BatchGetObjectRemote) that delegate to Impl methods (BatchGetObjectRemoteImpl), which orchestrate through Handlers and helpers (ParallelBatchGetObject, MergeParallelBatchGetResult). Batch operations aggregate small requests into shared memory to reduce round-trips; a complex fallback chain (URMA→TCP→payload) maintains reliability when transport fails. Key contracts: AggregateMemory and ParallelRes structs must maintain strict ordering between parallel requests and final response merging; BatchRh2dContext tracks device context across batch handler calls; optional pointer parameters (batchPtr, batchRootInfo) must be null-checked consistently to distinguish single vs. batch code paths.

data_constants: AggregateInfo::canBatchHandler, batchReqSize, batchSizes, batchStartIndex: batch aggregation state—preserve field order and semantics when refactoring batch checks

AggregateMemory::batchCursor, localSgeInfos, fallbackPayloads: shared memory position and fallback chain—cursor increments must align with shmUnit allocation, fallbackPayloads indexed by request in merge path
ParallelRes fields (respPbs, kps, eventKeys, subIndex): parallel result accumulation—vector indices and subIndex must map consistently to original request order for correct merge
BatchRh2dContext::prepared, devId: device context flag—must be set before EstablishConnAndFillSeg calls and checked before device operations
edit_checklist.warning: Fallback path coordination is fragile—URMA failures, TCP fallbacks, and payload fallbacks interact across GetObjectRemoteImpl, WaitFastTransportAndFallback, and ParallelBatchGetObject; a change to one must check all three for consistency.

historical_insights.title: URMA wait failures didn't fall back to TCP
historical_insights.problem: When URMA transport failed, the system did not properly fall back to TCP, causing requests to fail instead of degrading gracefully
historical_insights.root_cause: Fallback mechanism was not implemented in the wait path; also eventMap was leaking resources
historical_insights.solution: Added TCP fallback logic in the URMA wait handler, added explicit event cleanup to prevent leaks, instrumented with nanosleep metrics

historical_insights.title: Small batch requests caused excessive round-trips
historical_insights.problem: Many small object requests in a batch each required separate URMA operations, degrading throughput
historical_insights.root_cause: No aggregation of small requests into shared memory before sending
historical_insights.solution: Implemented AggregateMemory and PrepareAggregateMemory to combine small requests and send as single batches; GatherWrite sends aggregated buffers without remote merging

historical_insights.title: RH2D (Remote Host-to-Device) transport added
historical_insights.problem: System only supported TCP; needed low-latency remote memory access for GPU/NPU acceleration
historical_insights.root_cause: New hardware capabilities required a new transport path alongside existing TCP
historical_insights.solution: Integrated URMA-based RH2D transport with EstablishConnAndFillSeg, RemoteH2DRootInfoPb context, and BatchRh2dContext tracking; maintained TCP as primary fallback

historical_insights.title: Data migration during voluntary cluster scale-down
historical_insights.problem: When workers were removed, their data had to be migrated; existing push-based migration was inefficient
historical_insights.root_cause: Pull-based migration (triggering remote gets) was not implemented for voluntary scale scenarios
historical_insights.solution: Added NotifyRemoteGet RPC method to trigger remote get operations during scale-down, reusing existing GetObjectRemoteImpl path
