capabilities: Retrieves single or batch objects from remote workers with automatic TCP fallback if URMA fails
Aggregates small object requests into shared memory to reduce round-trip overhead
Establishes high-performance P2P URMA connections with segment info for direct remote memory access
Migrates data during cluster scale-down by triggering remote get operations on target workers
Validates cluster state and connection stability against etcd before remote operations

pitfalls.description: ParallelRes vectors (respPbs, kps, eventKeys) index and subIndex can drift from original request order during parallel batch processing, causing final responses to be misaligned with requests
pitfalls.consequence: Responses assigned to wrong request keys; client receives corrupted data
pitfalls.prevention: Always increment subIndex atomically with appending to ParallelRes vectors; validate index consistency in MergeParallelBatchGetResult before using as key to original request array

pitfalls.description: Fallback payloads in AggregateMemory and ParallelRes are populated conditionally (on URMA failure) but not always in same order as requests, leading to skipped or duplicated payloads in WaitFastTransportAndFallback
pitfalls.consequence: Batch response has missing or corrupted data segments; client reads garbage from RPC payload
pitfalls.prevention: When populating fallbackPayloads, explicitly track which request indices have been populated; check that final merge processes each original request exactly once

pitfalls.description: BatchRh2dContext prepared flag and devId can be stale if EstablishConnAndFillSeg fails partway through batch loop, leaving later batch requests using wrong device context
pitfalls.consequence: Later batch requests hit wrong GPU/NPU device; URMA write to wrong segment
pitfalls.prevention: Either reset BatchRh2dContext for each sub-request or validate prepared and devId match current request before EstablishConnAndFillSeg; fail fast if device changes mid-batch

pitfalls.description: Method parameter chains (GetObjectRemoteImpl → GetObjectRemoteHandler → various helpers) pass optional pointers batchPtr, batchRootInfo, fallbackStatus, batchRh2dContext; nullptr checks inconsistent across paths
pitfalls.consequence: Segfault when single-request path hits code expecting batch pointers non-null; or batch path misses initialization because single-request code didn't set pointer
pitfalls.prevention: Document and assert the invariant: batchPtr and batchRootInfo must both be non-nullptr or both nullptr; all code checking one must check both

reading_guide.start: WorkerWorkerOCServiceImpl class definition (line 33) and public methods GetObjectRemote, BatchGetObjectRemote
key: GetObjectRemote: entry point for single-object retrieval; shows delegation to GetObjectRemoteImpl and fallback parameter chain
BatchGetObjectRemoteImpl: orchestrates the full batch pipeline—prepare → parallel process → merge; shows interaction of AggregateMemory and ParallelRes
AggregateMemory & ParallelRes structs: data model for batch operations; cursor/index invariants are critical
EstablishConnAndFillSeg: P2P connection and segment setup; shows RH2D context usage and why CheckConnectionStable exists
reading_guide.skip: Individual helper methods like GatherWrite, WaitFastTransportAndFallback, MergeParallelBatchGetResult are implementation details; skim on first pass unless debugging a specific batch issue. CheckEtcdState and MigrateData are secondary features.

src/datasystem/common/rdma/urma_resource.description: This header defines RAII wrapper classes around URMA (Unified RDMA Management Architecture) API components for managing RDMA connections, jetties (endpoints), memory segments, and completion channels. The file establishes the resource management layer that clients use to create local contexts, register memory, and bind connections to jetties. A critical design contract is the use of weak_ptr for connection tracking in UrmaEvent and UrmaJetty to prevent circular reference cycles—callers must handle expired pointers. The async jetty deletion pattern (via ThreadPool) and dual JFR management (per-connection and context-level shared JFR for send-only jetties) are non-obvious optimizations that must be preserved; the 0xACFE default token and registry synchronization requirements are load-bearing for resource correctness.

data_constants: UrmaEvent::OperationType enum values (UNKNOWN=0, READ=1, WRITE=2): wire protocol compatibility
UrmaResource::urmaToken_ = {0xACFE}: must match device capability checks and segment registration calls
UrmaEvent::createTimeUs_ initialization: must use GetSteadyClockTimeStampUs() for monotonic timing
UrmaJetty::valid_ atomic initialization (false): must remain false until explicitly valid
PendingDeleteJetty struct: used in pendingDeleteJettys_ map, must preserve traceId field for logging
edit_checklist.warning: Weak pointer pattern (UrmaEvent→Jetty, Jetty→Connection) prevents circular refs; changing to shared_ptr introduces deadlock/leak risk—do not modify without understanding the ownership model.

historical_insights.title: Added user-configurable write size capping
historical_insights.problem: URMA write chunk size needed to be capped independently of device capability
historical_insights.root_cause: Device capabilities alone were insufficient; needed user control for workload tuning
historical_insights.solution: Introduced urma_max_write_size_mb gflag (range [1, 2048] MB, default 2 MB), effective max is min(user value, device capability)

historical_insights.title: Removed unreachable InfiniBand mode code
historical_insights.problem: IB (InfiniBand) mode code paths were dead but still present, making codebase confusing and unmaintainable
historical_insights.root_cause: Project fully transitioned to URMA/UB mode (CMake always sets URMA_OVER_UB=ON), but legacy IB code was never removed
historical_insights.solution: Removed UrmaMode enum, GetUrmaMode(), EthToRdmaDevName(), ReadGids(), IB_PATH constant; removed all #ifdef URMA_OVER_UB conditionals; updated flag validation

historical_insights.title: Fixed async event map leak and added URMA disconnect handling
historical_insights.problem: EventMap could leak when URMA operations failed; URMA failures weren't marked on disconnect
historical_insights.root_cause: Async event cleanup missing error paths; disconnect event wasn't propagating failure state
historical_insights.solution: Avoid eventMap leak via proper async cleanup, mark URMA as failed on disconnect, added comprehensive URMA logging

historical_insights.title: Introduced per-connection JFS/JFR instead of shared instances
historical_insights.problem: Shared JFS/JFR became a bottleneck and contention point for concurrent connections
historical_insights.root_cause: Centralized resource allocation didn't scale with connection count
historical_insights.solution: Moved to per-connection JFS/JFR management; retained context-level shared JFR for send-only jetties as optimization (see GetOrCreateSharedJettyJfr)

key_constructs.name: UrmaConnection
key_constructs.rationale: Forward-declared; holds shared_ptr to UrmaJetty (inferred from BindConnection pattern). UrmaEvent must hold weak_ptr to avoid cycle.

key_constructs.name: UrmaJetty
key_constructs.rationale: Local RDMA endpoint wrapper; valid_ atomic flag tracks readiness; MarkInvalid() is one-way transition to error state. Binds to UrmaConnection via mutex-protected weak_ptr. Shared JFR is required by UB provider (jetty_cfg.shared.jfr must be populated).

key_constructs.name: UrmaResource
key_constructs.rationale: Central resource manager; creates contexts, JFC, JFR; manages jetty registry (uint32_t jettyId → weak_ptr). RetireJettyToError moves failed jetties to error state asynchronously. Must maintain token (0xACFE) consistency across segment registration and device capability checks.

key_constructs.name: UrmaEvent
key_constructs.rationale: Represents an in-flight RDMA request; holds weak_ptr to jetty (via GetJetty) and derives connection from it. CreateTimeUs tracks end-to-end latency. Callers rely on GetConnection() returning the owning connection even if jetty has moved to error state.

key_constructs.name: UrmaContext
key_constructs.rationale: Wraps urma_context_t; created per local EID index. ChangeBondingBalanceMode() switches aggregation mode for link bonding.

key_constructs.name: UrmaJfr
key_constructs.rationale: Receive jetty wrapper; counter_ tracks instance count. Shared across send-only jetties (see sharedJettyJfr_ in UrmaResource).

capabilities: Create and manage URMA contexts, jetties, and completion channels with RAII semantics
Register local memory segments and import remote segments with token-based protection
Bind connections to jetties and track in-flight requests via weak pointers to prevent circular refs
Asynchronously retire failed jetties to error state and delete them via thread pool
Provide registry lookup for jetties by ID and lazy creation of context-level shared JFR for send-only jetties

pitfalls.description: Accessing UrmaEvent::GetConnection() or UrmaJetty::GetConnection() after the underlying connection is destroyed will return an expired weak_ptr
pitfalls.consequence: Attempting to use the returned weak_ptr will fail silently; lock() returns nullptr; logging/recovery paths become unreliable
pitfalls.prevention: Always call lock() on weak_ptr before use; check for nullptr before dereferencing; avoid caching the result across async boundaries

pitfalls.description: Jetty registry (jettyRegistry_) can become out-of-sync if jetties are deleted without calling UnregisterJetty
pitfalls.consequence: GetJettyById may return dangling shared_ptr to destroyed jetty; stale entries accumulate, leaking memory
pitfalls.prevention: Always pair CreateJetty with eventual UnregisterJetty call; use RegisterJetty/UnregisterJetty guards around jetty lifetime

pitfalls.description: Changing weak_ptr to shared_ptr for connection tracking in UrmaEvent or UrmaJetty introduces circular reference cycles
pitfalls.consequence: Connections and jetties won't be freed, causing resource leak and potential deadlock in destruction order
pitfalls.prevention: Keep weak_ptr for back-references; verify ownership model before refactoring: UrmaResource→UrmaJetty (shared) → UrmaConnection (shared); connection→jetty must be weak

pitfalls.description: Shared JFR (sharedJettyJfr_) lifetime doesn't outlive jetties using it if destruction order is wrong in UrmaResource dtor
pitfalls.consequence: Jetties holding shared_ptr to JFR may access freed memory after resource cleanup
pitfalls.prevention: Verify UrmaResource destructor destroys jetties before resetting sharedJettyJfr_; check co-change file urma_resource.cpp for dtor logic

pitfalls.description: Making async jetty deletion synchronous (RetireJettyToError without ThreadPool) blocks request paths during high error rates
pitfalls.consequence: Request latency spikes; connection handlers stall waiting for jetty cleanup; cascading failures under load
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve AsyncModifyJettyToError and deleteJettyThread_ design; if modifying error recovery, profile blocking time first

reading_guide.start: UrmaResource class (lines ~480+): the main public API that orchestrates all other components
key: UrmaJetty: why weak_ptr is used for connection tracking and what lifecycle stages (valid→invalid→error→deleted) exist
UrmaEvent: how in-flight requests track their owning connection and handle completion status
UrmaResource::jettyRegistry_: registry synchronization contract and lock ordering
UrmaResource::RetireJettyToError + AsyncDeleteJetty: async cleanup pattern via ThreadPool
reading_guide.skip: Low-level URMA API wrappers (UrmaJfce, UrmaJfc, UrmaJfr) on first pass unless debugging completion channel issues—focus on jetty and resource management
